Dear Medical Records Custodian:
Please be advised that I represent the above named individual. I hereby request copies of any and all
medical/office records concerning my client for the above referenced dates.
Please send me an itemized copy of your bill for professional services and keep me advised of
future billings and/or medical developments. Additionally, it would be extremely helpful if you
could hand write the total charges as well as any outstanding balance at the end of the billing
statement.
Enclosed please find a medical authorization signed by my client which entitles me to receive this
information, in accordance with KRS 422.317, at no charge for initial copies. If the free copy has
already been released, please notify my office of any and all fees prior to sending these records.
Thank you for your cooperation in this matter.
Sincerely,
PreHany
Aaron G, Whaley

[DOB-5639] CARE COORDINATION FOLLOW UP DENISE MOORE, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
CARE PLAN ASSESSMENT, PHE, MEDICATION MANAGEMENT AND DISEASE PROCESSES. RELATED
TO RECENT FALL AND SUBDURAL HEMORRHAGE.
79YEAR OLD FEMALE, LIVES WITH SPOUSE WHO IS PRIMARY CAREGIVER. HE IS WILLING AND
ABLE TO PROVIDE CARE FOR PATIENT. PATIENT WENT TO OUTPATIENT REGISTRATION ON JULY
14TH, FOR PLACEMENT OF PORT FOR FUTURE DIALYSIS USE. PATIENT WAS AMBULATING WITH
ROLLING WALKER IN REGISTRATION AREA, LOST BALANCE AND FELL BACKWARDS. SUSTAINED
LACERATION TO POSTERIOR SCALP, AND BRUISING TO LEFT SIDE OH, SHE DID NOT LOSE
CONSCIOUSNESS. PATIENT WAS ADMITTED TO HOSPITAL WITH DIAGNOSIS SUBDURAL
HEMORRHAGE. PORT PLACEMENT WAS PUT ON HOLD.
ADDITIONAL DIAGNOSIS A-FIB HYPERLIPIDEMIA HYPERTENSION CAD HYPOTHYROIDISM BREAST
CANCER ANEMIA GOUT GERD CKD 4.
HOSPITALIZATION AT BAPTIST LOUISVILLE 7.14 - 7.16

SKILLED SERVICES TO BE PROVIDED:CARE PLAN ASSESSMENT, PHE, MEDICATION MANAGEMENT
AND DISEASE PROCESSES. RELATED TO RECENT FALL AND SUBDURAL HEMORRHAGE.
79YEAR OLD FEMALE, LIVES WITH SPOUSE WHO IS PRIMARY CAREGIVER. HE IS WILLING AND
ABLE TO PROVIDE CARE FOR PATIENT. PATIENT WENT TO OUTPATIENT REGISTRATION ON JULY
14TH, FOR PLACEMENT OF PORT FOR FUTURE DIALYSIS USE. PATIENT WAS AMBULATING WITH
ROLLING WALKER IN REGISTRATION AREA, LOST BALANCE AND FELL BACKWARDS. SUSTAINED
LACERATION TO POSTERIOR SCALP, AND BRUISING TO LEFT SIDE OH, SHE DID NOT LOSE
CONSCIOUSNESS. PATIENT WAS ADMITTED TO HOSPITAL WITH DIAGNOSIS SUBDURAL
HEMORRHAGE. PORT PLACEMENT WAS PUT ON HOLD.
ADDITIONAL DIAGNOSIS A-FIB HYPERLIPIDEMIA HYPERTENSION CAD HYPOTHYROIDISM BREAST
CANCER ANEMIA GOUT GERD
AMBULATION ABILITY:3
PATIENT-CENTERED GOAL: GET PORT PLACED AND NO FURTHER COMPLCIAITONS
FIRST PRIORITY:MEDMGMT
CLINICIAN NAME:HOLLY HOUCHINS
DISCIPLINE:PT
EVALUATION DATE:7/24
CONFERENCE DATE:2/27
ORDER OBTAINED: Y
SKILLED SERVICES TO BE PROVIDED:1W1, 2W4 BEGINNING WORK 8/5. SHE HAS A NUMBER OF
APPTS NEXT WEEK SO SHE REQUESTED TO START THE FOLLOWING WEEK. LIVES WITH SPOUSE.
HAS SIGNIFICANT BALANCE DEFICITS, SEEMS TO HAVE R SIDED NEGLECT. HAS ABSENT
RIGHTING REACTIONS SO BE VERY CAREFUL. SHE FELL WITH A PREVIOUS PT WALKING OUTSIDE,
FELL AND HER ARM WAS SPLAYED OPEN.

SN FOR WOUND ASSESSMENT AND CARE, CP ASSESS, MEDICATION REGIMEN REVIEW,
MEDICATION ADHERENCE PROMOTION, MEDICATION TEACHING AND IN-DEPTH DISEASE
PROCESS REVIEW AND EDUCATION. PATIENT ADVISED NOTIFY SN IF ANY ABNORMAL WOUND
FINDINGS SUCH AS INCREASED DRAINAGE, FOUL ODOR, OR ACUTE PAIN ONSET. DISCUSSED
IMPORTANCE OF MAINTAINING A CLEAN ATMOSPHERE AND WASHING HANDS BEFORE AND AFTER
ANY CONTACT WITH WOUND OR WOUND AREA TO PREVENT SPREAD OF INFECTION. PATIENT
ADVISED ON HIGH PROTEIN DIET CHOICES. BASIC WOUND HEALING CONCEPTS AND THEORIES
APPROPRIATE FOR THIS PATIENTS LEVEL OF UNDERSTANDING, REVIEWED AND
ACKNOWLEDGED. PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 AND ABLE TO VERBALIZE NEEDS
WITHOUT DIFFICULTY. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED
OR STATED AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT IS COMPLIANT WITH ALL MEDICATIONS. PATIENT HAS
NO QUESTIONS ABOUT MEDICATIONS AT THIS TIME. THIS NURSE REVIEWED PRECAUTIONS AND
INTERVENTIONS AS INDICATED BY THE INTERVENTIONS AND GOALS DOCUMENTATION. PATIENT
VERBALIZES UNDERSTANDING AND HAS NO FURTHER QUESTIONS AT THIS TIME. PATIENT STATES
NO REMAINING DEFICITS RELATED TO SUBDURAL HEMATOMA. PATIENTS BLOOD SUGAR IS
ADEQUATELY CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES THAT SHE DOES HAVE SOME
PEAKS AND VALLEYS RELATED TO HER BLOOD GLUCOSE. WOUNDED SCALP IS NEARLY HEALED
AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF INFECTION. THERE ARE NO
SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED OR STATED AT THIS ASSESSMENT.
HUMANA PDGM

ORDER OBTAINED: Y
SKILLED SERVICES TO BE PROVIDED:CARE PLAN ASSESSMENT, PHE, MEDICATION MANAGEMENT AND DISEASE PROCESSES. RELATED TO
RECENT FALL AND SUBDURAL HEMORRHAGE.
79 YEAR OLD FEMALE, LIVES WITH SPOUSE WHO IS PRIMARY CAREGIVER. HE IS WILLING AND ABLE TO PROVIDE CARE FOR PATIENT. PATIENT
WENT TO OUTPATIENT REGISTRATION ON JULY 14TH, FOR PLACEMENT OF PORT FOR FUTURE DIALYSIS USE. PATIENT WAS AMBULATING WITH
ROLLING WALKER IN REGISTRATION AREA, LOST BALANCE AND FELL BACKWARDS. SUSTAINED LACERATION TO POSTERIOR SCALP, AND
BRUISING TO LEFT SIDE OH, SHE DID NOT LOSE CONSCIOUSNESS. PATIENT WAS ADMITTED TO HOSPITAL WITH DIAGNOSIS SUBDURAL
HEMORRHAGE. PORT PLACEMENT WAS PUT ON HOLD.
ADDITIONAL DIAGNOSIS A-FIB HYPERLIPIDEMIA HYPERTENSION CAD HYPOTHYROIDISM BREAST CANCER ANEMIA GOUT GERD
AMBULATION ABILITY:3
PATIENT-CENTERED GOAL: GET PORT PLACED AND NO FURTHER COMPLCIAITONS

DISCIPLINE:PT

SN FOR WOUND ASSESSMENT AND CARE, CP ASSESS, MEDICATION REGIMEN REVIEW, MEDICATION ADHERENCE PROMOTION, MEDICATION
TEACHING AND IN-DEPTH DISEASE PROCESS REVIEW AND EDUCATION. PATIENT ADVISED NOTIFY SN IF ANY ABNORMAL WOUND FINDINGS SUCH
AS INCREASED DRAINAGE, FOUL ODOR, OR ACUTE PAIN ONSET. DISCUSSED IMPORTANCE OF MAINTAINING A CLEAN ATMOSPHERE AND
WASHING HANDS BEFORE AND AFTER ANY CONTACT WITH WOUND OR WOUND AREA TO PREVENT SPREAD OF INFECTION. PATIENT ADVISED
ON HIGH PROTEIN DIET CHOICES. BASIC WOUND HEALING CONCEPTS ANDTHEORIES APPROPRIATE FOR THIS PATIENTS LEVEL OF
UNDERSTANDING, REVIEWED ANDACKNOWLEDGED. PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 AND ABLE TO VERBALIZE NEEDS WITHOUT
DIFFICULTY. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED OR STATED AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT IS COMPLIANT
WITH ALL MEDICATIONS. PATIENT HAS NO QUESTIONS ABOUT MEDICATIONS AT THIS TIME. THIS NURSE REVIEWED PRECAUTIONS AND
INTERVENTIONS AS INDICATED BY THE INTERVENTIONS AND GOALS DOCUMENTATION. PATIENT VERBALIZES UNDERSTANDING AND HAS NO
FURTHER QUESTIONS AT THIS TIME. PATIENT STATES NO REMAINING DEFICITS RELATED TO SUBDURAL HEMATOMA. PATIENTS BLOOD SUGAR IS
ADEQUATELY CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES THAT SHE DOES HAVE SOME PEAKS AND VALLEYS RELATED TO HER BLOOD
GLUCOSE. WOUNDED SCALP IS NEARLY HEALED AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF INFECTION. THERE ARE NO
SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED OR STATED AT THIS ASSESSMENT.

SEVERE LIMITATIONS
PLEASE COMPLETE THE FOLLOWING QUESTIONS AS RELATED TO THE TIMED UP AND GO (TUG) ASSESSMENT. DIRECTIONS FOR TUG
ASSESSMENT ARE FOUND IN REFERENCE SECTION.
WAS THE PATIENT ABLE TO COMPLETE THE TUG TEST?
YES
INDICATE RESULTS IN SECONDS:
48.2
PLEASE COMPLETE THE FOLLOWING QUESTIONS AS RELATED TO THE BERG FUNCTIONAL BALANCE TEST:
1. SITTING TO STANDING
INSTRUCTIONS: PLEASE STAND UP. TRY NOT TO USE YOUR HANDS FOR SUPPORT.
1-NEEDS MINIMAL AID TO STAND OR TO STABILIZE
2. STANDING UNSUPPORTED
INSTRUCTIONS: PLEASE STAND FOR TWO MINUTES WITHOUT HOLDING ON.
(IE THE SUBJECT IS ABLE TO STAND 2 MINUTES UNSUPPORTED, SCORE FULL POINTS FOR SITTING UNSUPPORTED. PROCEED TO ITEM 4.)
1- NEEDS SEVERAL TRIES TO STAND 30 SECONDS UNSUPPORTED
3. SITTING WITH BACK UNSUPPORTED BUT FEET SUPPORTED ON FLOOR OR ON ASTOOL
INSTRUCTIONS: PLEASE SIT WITH ARMS FOLDED FOR 2 MINUTES.
4-ABLE TO SIT SAFELY AND SECURELY 2 MINUTES
4. STANDING TO SITTING
INSTRUCTIONS: PLEASE SIT DOWN
1- SITS INDEPENDENTLY BUT HAS UNCONTROLLED DESCENT
5. TRANSFERS
INSTRUCTIONS: ARRANGE CHAIR(S) FOR A PIVOT TRANSFER. ASKSUBJECT TO TRANSFER ONE WAY TOWARD A SEAT WITH ARMRESTS
AND ONE WAY TOWARD A SEAT WITHOUT ARMRESTS. YOU MAY USE TWO CHAIRS (ONE WITH AND ONE WITHOUT ARMRESTS) OR A BED
ANDA CHAIR.
1- NEEDS ONE PERSON TO ASSIST
6. STANDING UNSUPPORTED WITH EYES CLOSED
INSTRUCTIONS: PLEASE CLOSE YOUR EYES AND STAND STILL FOR 10 SECONDS.
0 - NEEDS HELP TO KEEP FROM FALLING
7. STANDING UNSUPPORTED WITH FEET TOGETHER
INSTRUCTIONS: PLACE YOUR FEET TOGETHER AND STAND WITHOUT HOLDING ON.
1- NEEDS HELP TO ATTAIN POSITION BUT ABLE TO STAND 15 SECONDS FEET TOGETHER
8. REACHING FORWARD WITH OUTSTRETCHED ARM WHILE STANDING

INSTRUCTIONS: LIFT ARM TO 90 DEGREES. STRETCH OUT YOUR FINGERS AND REACH FORWARD AS FAR AS YOU CAN. (EXAMINER PLACES
A RULER AT END OF FINGERTIPS WHEN ARM IS AT 90 DEGREES. FINGERS SHOULD NOT TOUCH THE RULER WHILE REACHING FORWARD.
THE RECORDED MEASURE IS THE DISTANCE FORWARD THATTHE FINGERS REACHES WHILE THE SUBJECT IS IN THE MOST FORWARD
LEAN POSITION. WHEN POSSIBLE,ASK SUBJECT TO USE BOTH ARMS WHEN REACHING TO AVOID ROTATION OF THE TRUNK.)
0 - LOSES BALANCE WHILE TRYING/REQUIRES EXTERNAL SUPPORT
9g.PICK UP OBJECT FROM THE FLOOR FROM A STANDING POSITION
INSTRUCTIONS: PICK UP THE SHOE/SLIPPER, WHICH IS PLACED IN FRONT OF YOUR FEET.
0 - UNABLE TO TRY/NEEDS ASSIST TO KEEP FROM LOSING BALANCE OR FALLING
10. TURNING TO LOOK BEHIND OVER LEFT AND RIGHT SHOULDERS WHILE STANDING
INSTRUCTIONS: TURN TO LOOK DIRECTLY BEHIND YOU OVER TOWARD LEFT SHOULDER. REPEAT TO THE RIGHT.
(EXAMINER MAY PICK AN OBJECT TO LOOK AT DIRECTLY BEHIND THE SUBJECT TO ENCOURAGE A BETTER TWIST TURN.)
0 - NEEDS ASSIST TO KEEP FROM LOSING BALANCE OR FALLING
11. TURN 360 DEGREES
INSTRUCTIONS: TURN COMPLETELY AROUND INA FULL CIRCLE. PAUSE. THEN TURN A FULL CIRCLE IN THE OTHER
DIRECTION.
0 - NEEDS ASSISTANCE WHILE TURNING
12. PLACING ALTERNATE FOOT ON STEP OR STOOL WHILE STANDING UNSUPPORTED
INSTRUCTIONS: PLACE EACH FOOT ALTERNATIVELY ON THE STEP/STOOL. CONTINUE UNTIL EACH FOOT HAS TOUCHED THE STEP/STOOL
FOUR TIMES.
0 - NEEDS ASSISTANCE TO KEEP FROM FALLING/UNABLE TO TRY
13. STANDING UNSUPPORTED ONE FOOT IN FRONT
INSTRUCTIONS: (DEMONSTRATE TO SUBJECT) PLACE ONE FOOT DIRECTLY IN FRONT OF THE OTHER. IF YOU
FEEL THAT YOU CANNOT PLACE YOUR FOOT DIRECTLY IN FRONT, TRYTO STEP FAR ENOUGH AHEAD THAT THE HEEL OF YOUR FORWARD
EOOT IS AHEAD OF THE TOES OF THE OTHER FOOT.
(TO SCORE 3 POINTS,THE LENGTH OF THE STEP SHOULD EXCEED THE LENGTH OF THE OTHER FOOT AND THEWIDTH OF THE STANCE
SHOULD APPROXIMATE THE SUBJECT'S NORMAL STRIDE WIDTH.)
0 -LOSES BALANCE WHILE STEPPING OR STANDING
14. STANDING ON ONE LEG
INSTRUCTIONS: STAND ON ONE LEG AS LONG AS YOU CAN WITHOUT HOLDING ON.
0 - UNABLE TO TRY OR NEEDS ASSIST TO PREVENT FALL

BETTY M PRINCE IS A 79 YR/O FEMALE WITH HISTORY OF BREAST CANCER, INSULIN DEPENDENT DIABETES
MELLITUS, CKDSTAGE 4, ATRIAL FIBRILLATION, CORONARY ARTERY DISEASE, HYPERTENSION,
HYPERLIPIDEMIA, AND GOUT WHO PRESENTED TO THE EMERGENCY DEPARTMENT AFTER A MECHANICAL
FALL. SHE WAS IN THE HOSPITAL FOR RIGHT ARM VENOUS SHUNT PLACEMENT WHEN SHE TRIPPED OVER
HER SHOE, CAUSING HER TO FALL AND SHE HIT HER HEAD AGAINST THE WALL. SHE REPORTS SORENESS
WHERE SHE HIT HER HEAD, BUT SHE DENIES HEADACHE, VISION CHANGES, LOSS OF CONSCIOUSNESS,
UNILATERAL NUMBNESS, WEAKNESS, PARESTHESIAS, NECK PAIN, DIZZINESS, CHEST PAIN, SHORTNESS
OF BREATH, FEVER, CHILLS, NAUSEA, VOMITING, AND DIARRHEA. THERE WERE NO KNOWN AGGRAVATING
OR ALLEVIATING FACTORS. SHE IS ON ASPIRIN DAILY BUT IS NOT ON ANY ANTICOAGULATION. HER HUSBAND
STATES SHE LOSES HER BALANCE FREQUENTLY, AND THAT SHE HAS A WALKER THAT SHE USES
OCCASIONALLY. SKILLED PT INDICATED FOR PROGRESSIVE GAIT TRAINING, FALL PREVENTION, NMR, THEREX, THERACT
INDICATE THERAPY PLAN, INCLUDING THE MAIN FOCUS FOR NEXT VISIT'S TREATMENT:
PLAN FOR NEXT VISIT INCLUDES LE STRENGTHENING, BALANCE, PROGRESSIVE GAIT TRAINING
HOMEBOUND STATUS

YES

BRADEN RISK ASSESSMENT SCALE
SENSORY PERCEPTION -ABILITY TO RESPOND MEANINGFULLY TO PRESSURE-RELATED DISCOMFORT
SLIGHTLY LIMITED - RESPONDS TO VERBAL COMMANDS BUT CANNOT ALWAYS COMMUNICATE DISCOMFORT OR NEED TO BE TURNED, OR
HAS SOME SENSORY IMPAIRMENT WHICH LIMITS ABILITY TO FEEL PAIN OR DISCOMFORT IN 1OR 2 EXTREMITIES.
MOISTURE - DEGREE TO WHICH SKIN IS EXPOSED TO MOISTURE
OCCASIONALLY MOIST -SKIN IS OCCASIONALLY MOIST, REQUIRING AN EXTRA LINEN CHANGE APPROXIMATELY ONCE A DAY.
ACTIVITY - DEGREE OF PHYSICAL ACTIVITY
WALKS OCCASIONALLY - WALKS OCCASIONALLY DURING DAY, BUT FOR VERY SHORT DISTANCES, WITH OR WITHOUT ASSISTANCE.
SPENDS MAJORITY OF DAY IN BED OR CHAIR.
MOBILITY -ABILITY TO CHANGE AND CONTROL BODY POSITION
SLIGHTLY LIMITED - MAKES FREQUENT THOUGH SLIGHT CHANGES IN BODY OR EXTREMITY POSITION INDEPENDENTLY.
NUTRITION - USUAL FOOD INTAKE PATTERN
ADEQUATE - EATS OVER HALF OF MOST MEALS. EATS A TOTAL OF 4 SERVINGS OF PROTEIN (MEAT, DAIRY PRODUCTS) PER DAY.
OCCASIONALLY WILL REFUSE A MEAL, BUT WILL USUALLY TAKE A SUPPLEMENT WHEN OFFERED, OR IS ON A TUBE FEEDING OR TPN
REGIMEN, WHICH PROBABLY MEETS MOST OF NUTRITIONAL NEEDS.
FRICTION AND SHEAR
POTENTIAL PROBLEM - MOVES FEEBLY OR REQUIRES MINIMUM ASSISTANCE. DURING A MOVE SKIN PROBABLY SLIDES TO SOME EXTENT
AGAINST SHEETS, CHAIR, RESTRAINTS, OR OTHER DEVICES. MAINTAINS RELATIVELY GOOD POSITION IN CHAIR OR BED MOST OF THE
TIME BUT OCCASIONALLY SLIDES DOWN.
TOTAL SCORE (PATIENTS WITH A TOTAL SCORE OF 12 OR LESS ARE CONSIDERED TO BE AT HIGH RISK OF DEVELOPING PRESSURE
ULCERS):
17
BASED ON THESCORE, THE RISK LEVEL FOR THIS PATIENT IS:
LOW
INDICATE THE BRADEN RISK LEVEL PRESENTED:
LOW RISK (15 -18) - IF OTHER MAJOR RISK FACTORS PRESENT, ADVANCE TO NEXT LEVEL OF RISK
A STANDARD MATTRESS IS APPROPRIATE AT THIS TIME.
OK

INCONTINENCE -- INABILITY TO MAKE IT TO THE BATHROOM OR COMMODE IN TIMELY MANNER INCLUDES FREQUENCY, URGENCY, AND/OR

VISUAL IMPAIRMENT -- INCLUDES BUT NOT LIMITED TO, MACULAR DEGENERATION, DIABETIC RETINOPATHIES, VISUAL FIELD LOSS, AGE

YES
POLY PHARMACY (4 OR MORE PRESCRIPTIONS - ANY TYPE) -- ALL PRESCRIPTIONS INCLUDING PRESCRIPTIONS FOR OTC MEDS. DRUGS
HIGHLY ASSOCIATED WITH FALL RISK INCLUDE BUT NOT LIMITED TO, SEDATIVES, ANTI-DEPRESSANTS, TRANQUILIZERS, NARCOTICS,
ANTIHYPERTENSIVES, CARDIAC MEDS, CORTICOSTEROIDS, ANTI-ANXIETY DRUGS, ANTICHOLINERGIC DRUGS, AND HYPOGLYCEMIC DRUGS.
YES
PAIN AFFECTING LEVEL OF FUNCTION -- PAIN OFTEN AFFECTS AN INDIVIDUAL'S DESIRE OR ABILITY TO MOVE, OR PAIN CAN BE_A FACTOR IN
DEPRESSION OR COMPLIANCE WITH SAFETY RECOMMENDATIONS.
NO
COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT -- COULD INCLUDE PATIENTS WITH DEMENTIA, ALZHEIMER'S OR STROKE PATIENTS, OR PATIENTS WHO ARE
CONFUSED, USE POOR JUDGMENT, HAVE DECREASED COMPREHENSION, IMPULSIVITY, MEMORY DEFICITS. CONSIDER PATIENTS ABILITY TO
ADHERE TO THE PLAN OF CARE.
YES

BATH FOR ASSISTANCE OR SUPERVISION.
(QM) (M1840) TOILET TRANSFERRING: CURRENT ABILITY TO GET TO AND FROM THE TOILET OR BEDSIDE COMMODE SAFELY AND TRANSFER
ON AND OFF TOILET/COMMODE.
1-WHEN REMINDED, ASSISTED, OR SUPERVISED BY ANOTHER PERSON, ABLE TO GET TO AND FROM THE TOILET AND TRANSFER.
(QM) (M1845) TOILETING HYGIENE: CURRENT ABILITY TO MAINTAIN PERINEAL HYGIENE SAFELY, ADJUST CLOTHES AND/OR INCONTINENCE
PADS BEFORE AND AFTER USING TOILET, COMMODE, BEDPAN, URINAL. IF MANAGING OSTOMY, INCLUDE CLEANING AREA AROUND STOMA,
BUT NOT MANAGING EQUIPMENT.
2 -SOMEONE MUST HELP THE PATIENT TO MAINTAIN TOILETING HYGIENE AND/OR ADJUST CLOTHING.
(QM) (M1850) TRANSFERRING: CURRENT ABILITY TO MOVE SAFELY FROM BED TO CHAIR, OR ABILITY TO TURN AND POSITION SELF IN BED IF
PATIENT IS BEDFAST.
2 -ABLE TO BEAR WEIGHT AND PIVOT DURING THE TRANSFER PROCESS BUT UNABLE TO TRANSFER SELF
(C1) (QM) (PRA) (M1860) AMBULATION/LOCOMOTION: CURRENT ABILITY TO WALK SAFELY, ONCE INA STANDING POSITION, OR USE A
WHEELCHAIR, ONCE INA SEATED POSITION, ON A VARIETY OF SURFACES.
3 -ABLE TO WALK ONLY WITH THE SUPERVISION OR ASSISTANCE OF ANOTHER PERSON AT ALL TIMES.
(QM) (M1870) FEEDING OR EATING: CURRENT ABILITY TO FEED SELF MEALS AND SNACKS SAFELY. NOTE: THIS REFERS ONLY TO THE
PROCESS OF EATING, CHEWING, AND SWALLOWING, NOT PREPARING THE FOOD TO BE EATEN.
1-ABLE TO FEED SELF INDEPENDENTLY BUT REQUIRES: (A) MEAL SET-UP; OR (B) INTERMITTENT ASSISTANCE OR SUPERVISION FROM
ANOTHER PERSON; OR (C) ALIQUID, PUREED OR GROUND MEAT DIET
(C1) (QM) (M1910) HAS THIS PATIENT HAD A MULTI- FACTOR FALLS RISK ASSESSMENT USING A STANDARDIZED, VALIDATED ASSESSMENT.
TOOL?
2-YES, AND IT DOES INDICATE A RISK FOR FALLS.
(GGO100A) SELF CARE PRIOR FUNCTIONING: EVERYDAY ACTIVITIES: INDICATE THE PATIENT'S USUAL ABILITY WITH EVERYDAY ACTIVITIES
PRIOR TO THE CURRENT ILLNESS, EXACERBATION, OR INJURY. CODE THE PATIENT'S NEED FOR ASSISTANCE WITH BATHING, DRESSING,
USING THE TOILET, OR EATING PRIOR TO THE CURRENT ILLNESS, EXACERBATION, OR INJURY.
2 -NEEDEDSOME HELP - PATIENT NEEDED PARTIAL ASSISTANCE FROM ANOTHER PERSON TO COMPLETE ACTIVITIES.
(GG0100B) INDOOR MOBILITY (AMBULATION) PRIOR FUNCTIONING: EVERYDAY ACTIVITIES: INDICATE THE PATIENT'S USUAL ABILITYWITH
EVERYDAY ACTIVITIES PRIOR TO THE CURRENT ILLNESS, EXACERBATION, OR INJURY. CODE THE PATIENT'S NEED FOR ASSISTANCE WITH
WALKING FROM ROOM TO ROOM (WITH OR WITHOUT A DEVICE SUCH AS CANE, CRUTCH OR WALKER) PRIOR TO THE CURRENT ILLNESS,
EXACERBATION, OR INJURY.
2 -NEEDEDSOME HELP - PATIENT NEEDED PARTIAL ASSISTANCE FROM ANOTHER PERSON TO COMPLETE ACTIVITIES.
(GG0100C) STAIRS PRIOR FUNCTIONING: EVERYDAY ACTIVITIES: INDICATE THE PATIENT'S USUAL ABILITY WITH EVERYDAY ACTIVITIES
PRIOR TO THE CURRENT ILLNESS, EXACERBATION, OR INJURY. CODE THE PATIENT'S NEED FOR ASSISTANCE WITH INTERNAL OR

OR INJURY.

HEMORRHAGE.
79 YEAR OLD FEMALE, LIVES WITH SPOUSE WHO IS PRIMARY CAREGIVER. HE IS WILLING AND ABLE TO PROVIDE CARE FOR PATIENT.
PATIENT WENT TO OUTPATIENT REGISTRATION ON JULY 14TH, FOR PLACEMENT OF PORT FOR FUTURE DIALYSIS USE. PATIENT WAS
AMBULATING WITH ROLLING WALKER IN REGISTRATION AREA, LOST BALANCE AND FELL BACKWARDS. SUSTAINED LACERATION TO
POSTERIOR SCALP, AND BRUISING TO LEFT SIDE OH, SHE DID NOT LOSE CONSCIOUSNESS. PATIENT WAS ADMITTED TO HOSPITAL WITH
DIAGNOSIS SUBDURAL HEMORRHAGE. PORT PLACEMENT WAS PUT ON HOLD.

Client: PRINCE, BETTY M MR No: B3600047293201 Legacy MR No:
Client DOB: 3/1/1941
Insured ID: H43132048 Primary Payor: HUMANA PDGM
Visit Date: [DOB-5339] Visit Number: 2 Visit Type: PA11 - PTA SUBSEQUENT VISIT
: 1. INSTRUCT ON BASIC USE OF ASSISTIVE DEVICES TO ENSURE PATIENT IS SAFE UNTIL NEXT VISIT
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED ON NEED TO USE WALKER AND/ OR ASSISTANCE TO AMBULATE.
2. INSTRUCT PATIENT /CAREGIVER IN SAFE TRANSFER TECHNIQUES USING APPROPRIATE BODY MECHANICS AND / OR EQUIPMENT.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: TASK DESIGN TO IMPROVE PLANNING AND CONTROL FOR SIT TO STAND TRANSFERS FROM RECLINER TO RWINSTRUCTED PATIENT/ CAREGIVER IN:
SAFE SIT TO STAND TECHNIQUE;
PATIENT REQUIRES OCCASIONAL VERBAL/TACTILE CUES FOR HAND PLACEMENT AND AD PLACEMENT -TO KEEP RW WITH HER AS SHE BACKS
UP TO SIT DOWN --NOT TO PARK WALKER TO THE SIDE AND WALK OVER TO CHAIR - FALL RISK
PATIENT REQUIRES SUPERVISION OR TOUCHING ASSISTANCE TO SAFELY PERFORM TRANSFERS.
3. INSTRUCT IN THERAPEUTIC EXERCISES AND HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM. PROVIDE MANUAL THERAPY TECHNIQUE(S) AS NEEDED.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED WITH 1TO 2 VC FOR LE THER EX TO IMPROVE STRENGTH -SEATED LAQ. MIP. AP. HIP ABDUCTION X 10
SEATED UPRIGHT WITH BACK UNSUPPORTED - ZONTAGO 1, 2 , 4X5 REPS
PREVIOUSLY LISTED GIVEN FOR HEP
4. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: CGA AND SETUP FOR GT WITH
ROLLING WALKER; IN HOME OVER EVEN SURFACES -X 100 FEET
PATIENT REQUIRED SUPERVISION OR TOUCHING ASSISTANCE.
2TO3VERBAL/TACTILE CUES NEEDED TO IMPROVE:
HEEL STRIKE;
STEP CLEARANCE;
STEP LENGTH;
POSTURE; TO KEEP AD CLOSE TO BODY WITH TURNS
FATIGUED AFTER GAIT ACTIVITY, REQUIRED REST TO RECOVER; RPE BORG 13/20 DYSPNEA 2/10
NO LOSS OF BALANCE.
PATIENT REQUIRES ADDITIONAL GAIT TRAINING FOR SAFE AMBULATION.
5. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO UPPER/LOWER EXTREMITY ROM AND STRENGTHENING. (INDICATE REPS, SETS, RESISTANCE AND
LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE PROVIDED)
DETAILS/COMMENTS: LISTED ABOVE

Client: PRINCE, BETTY M MR No: B3600047293201 Legacy MR No:

Insured ID: H43132048 Primary Payor: HUMANA PDGM
Visit Date: [DOB-5251] Visit Number: 3 Visit Type: RN11 - RN SUBSEQUENT VISIT
LEAVE BLISTERS INTACT
13. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON PATHOPHYSIOLOGY RELATED TO CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASE. INSTRUCTED ON:
DETAILS/COMMENTS: "CALL US FIRST" AND WHEN TO CALL 911
DEFINITION OF CARDIAC OUTPUT AS AN INADEQUATE AMOUNT OF BLOOD PUMPED FROM THE HEART AND THE RELATED SIGNS / SYMPTOMS
SIGNS / SYMPTOMS OF CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASE INCLUDING UNRELIEVED CHEST PAIN OR PAIN IN THE NECK, JAW, INNER ASPECTS OF
ARMS, SHOULDERS, UPPER ABDOMEN, INCREASED ANXIETY, PROLONGED INDIGESTION, SWEATING, SHORTNESS OF BREATH, INCREASED
EDEMA, CHANGE IN MENTAL STATUS AND INCREASED FATIGUE.
IMPORTANCE OF REPORTING INCREASED INCIDENCE OF CONFUSION.
IMPORTANCE OF REPORTING INCREASED SHORTNESS OF BREATH AND COUGH
14. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON DIETARY RESTRICTIONS THAT PROMOTE CONTROL OF CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASE.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: IMPORTANCE OF LOW SODIUM DIET INCLUDING A DEFINITION OF FOODS HIGH IN SODIUM, HOW TO REVIEW FOOD
LABELS TO IDENTIFY HIGH SODIUM CONTENT FOODS AND SODIUM ALTERNATIVES
15. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON THE USE OF MEDICATIONS TO TREAT CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASE
DETAILS/COMMENTS: MEDICATION REGIMEN INCLUDING CORRECT MEDICATION, DOSAGE, FREQUENCY, TIMES
16. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON THE IMPORTANCE OF DAILY ACTIVITY/EXERCISE WHEN MANAGING CARDIOVASCULAR DISEASE
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PERFORMING ACTIVITIES THAT IMPROVE PERFUSION
NEED FOR FREQUENT REST PERIODS AND ON SETTING REASONABLE ACTIVITY GOALS
IMMEDIATELY STOPPING EXERCISE IF CHEST PAIN EXPERIENCED
17. PROVIDE/INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON WOUND CARE
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED ON CARE OF DRESSING/AREA
INSTRUCTED ON SIGNS / SYMPTOMS OF INFECTION TO WOUND (REDNESS, DRAINAGE, ODOR)
18. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON PATHOPHYSIOLOGY RELATED TO SKIN BREAKDOWN
DETAILS/COMMENTS: DEFINITION OF SKIN BREAKDOWN - CHANGES TO INTACT SKIN, INCLUDING NONBLANCHABLE ERYTHEMA WITHOUT
OVERLYING TISSUE LOSS, ABRASION, MILD ENTRY INTO THE SKIN, AND DEEP AND EXTENSIVE PRESSURE ULCERS.
DEFINITION OF PRESSURE ULCER - DAMAGE TO THE SKIN RESULTING IN SKIN LOSS WITH A CRATER (MILD OR DEEP) AS A RESULT OF
PRESSURE OR FRICTION/SHEAR OF SKIN AGAINST OTHER SURFACES.
PRESSURE ULCERS ARE ALSO CALLED DECUBITUS ULCERS OR BED SORES
PRESSURE ULCERS ARE CAUSED BY UNRELIEVED PRESSURE (COMPRESSING DOWNWARD FORCE ON A BODY AREA) THAT RESULTS IN
DAMAGE TO AN UNDERLYING TISSUE; THIS CAUSES A POOR OR INSUFFICIENT BLOOD SUPPLY TO THE TISSUE. OCCURS MAINLY OVER BONY
PROMINENCES.
SIGNS / SYMPTOMS OF SKIN BREAKDOWN - PALE OR REDDENED AREA, BLISTER, PAIN, ITCHING, BOGGY AREA, LOSS OF TISSUE TO INCLUDE
BOTH LAYERS OF SKIN.
19. PERFORM MULTIFACTOR FALLS RISK ASSESSMENT TO INCLUDE HISTORY OF FALLS, ACCIDENTS, MEDICATION REGIMEN, IMPACT OF
POLYPHARMACY, COGNITIVE STATUS, MOBILITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL STATUS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: POTENTIAL OF VERTIGO AND DIZZINESS WITH SUDDEN POSITION CHANGES
20. PROVIDE INSTRUCTION RELATED TO PATIENT'S RISK FOR FALLS AND SAFETY TO PREVENT FALLS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: KEEP STAIRS AND PATHWAYS CLEAR OF CLUTTER
KEEP A PHONE CLOSE BY AT ALL TIMES

“1. INSTRUCT ON BASIC USE OF ASSISTIVE DEVICES TO ENSURE PATIENT IS SAFE UNTIL NEXT VISIT
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PROVIDED INSTRUCTION IN SAFE USE OF ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
2. INSTRUCT PATIENT /CAREGIVER IN SAFE TRANSFER TECHNIQUES USING APPROPRIATE BODY MECHANICS AND / OR EQUIPMENT.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN SAFE SIT TO STAND TECHNIQUE. PATIENT / CAREGIVER PERFORMS TRANSFERS
AS INSTRUCTED.
3. INSTRUCT IN THERAPEUTIC EXERCISES AND HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM. PROVIDE MANUAL THERAPY TECHNIQUE(S) AS NEEDED.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: THERA EX - 3 VVCS ("SQUAT DOWN HALFWAY, "WALK SIDEWAYS", "WALK BACKWARDS") X 15 REPS/STEPS EACH IN
STANDING POSITION TO IMPROVE STRENGTH, BALANCE AND FLEXIBILITY USING ZONTAGO BALANCE ACTIVITIES #14, 17 AND 18 @ RPE ON
BORG 15/20 AND DYSPNEA SCALE 2/10. EDUC INTERVENTIONS -T/l TO DO ROM EX TO BLE'S IN SITTING/STANDING POSITION x 15REPS EACH,
2X A DAY. T/| TO KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR TO DECREASE RISK OF FALLS. T/. PLB BEFORE, DURING AND AFTER ACTIVITIES. T/| PROPER
STANDING POSTURE TO IMPROVE POSTURAL ALIGNMENT AND SYMMETRY. PATIENT VERBALIZED UNDERSTANDING TO ALL INSTRUCTIONS
TODAY.
4. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: GAIT TRAINING + 3VCS ("LOOK STRAIGHT AHEAD", "PICK UP FEET HIGHER", "BIGGER STEPS") TO IMPROVE POSTURAL
ALIGNMENT, FOOT CLEARANCE AND LIMB ADVANCEMENT USING GAIT TRAINING, 90 FEET x 2 (6MINS WITH 4MINS SEATED REST BREAK NEEDED
TO RECOVER FATIGUE) USING A FWW WITH SBA-CGAX1 ON EVEN/UNEVEN TERRAIN, RPE ON BORG 16/20 AND DYSPNEA SCALE 2/10.
5. ASSIST IN PERFORMANCE OF BALANCE EXERCISES IN STATIC AND OR DYNAMIC POSITIONS.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN POSTURAL AWARENESS, WEIGHT SHIFTING AND REACHING ACTIVITY WITH
UPPER EXTREMITY SUPPORT WITH OCCASIONAL LOSS OF BALANCE NOTED.
6. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO UPPER/LOWER EXTREMITY ROM AND STRENGTHENING. (INDICATE REPS, SETS, RESISTANCE AND
LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE PROVIDED)
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SEE ABOVE
7. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE
PROVIDED):
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SEE ABOVE

INDICATE THERAPY PLAN, INCLUDING THE MAIN FOCUS FOR NEXT VISIT'S TREATMENT:
SKILLED PT SERVICES 2W2 AND 1W7 TO FOCUS ON THERAPEUTIC EXCERCISES TO BLE'S TO INCREASE MUSCLE STRENGTH AND
ENDURANCE, NMR TO IMPROVE BALANCE AND COORDINATION, GAIT TRAINING TO ACHIEVE INDEPENDENT AND SAFE COMMUNITY
MOBILITY, AND HEP/FMP INSTRUCTIONS. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER PARTICIPATED TO PT POC.

YES

WHERE A PHYSICIAN HAS DETERMINED THAT IT IS MEDICALLY CONTRAINDICATED FOR A BENEFICIARY TO LEAVE THE HOME BECAUSE
THE PATIENT HAS A CONDITION THAT MAY MAKE THE PATIENT MORE SUSCEPTIBLE TO CONTRACTING COVID-19
DOCUMENT PATIENT'S CONDITION AND LIMITATIONS AS IT RELATES TO THEIR HOMEBOUND STATUS:
LEAVING HOME IS VERY DIFFICULT ANDTAXING EFFORT DUE TO MUSCLE WEAKNESS, DECREASED ENDURANCE, GAIT INSTABILITY, AND
SAFETY CONCERNS. PATIENT REQUIRES ASSISTANCE OF ANOTHER PERSON AND AN ASSISTIVE DEVICE IN ORDER TO LEAVE PLACE OF
RESIDENCE. PATIENT REQUIRES SEVERAL HOURS OF REST UPON RETURN FROM MEDICAL APPOINTMENT.

“4. INSTRUCT PATIENT /CAREGIVER IN SAFE TRANSFER TECHNIQUES USING APPROPRIATE BODY MECHANICS AND / OR EQUIPMENT.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN SAFE SIT TO STAND TECHNIQUE. PATIENT / CAREGIVER PERFORMS TRANSFERS
AS INSTRUCTED.
2. INSTRUCT IN THERAPEUTIC EXERCISES AND HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: THERA EX - 3 VVCS ("SQUAT DOWN HALFWAY, "WALK SIDEWAYS", "WALK BACKWARDS") X 15 REPS/STEPS EACH IN
STANDING POSITION TO IMPROVE STRENGTH, BALANCE AND FLEXIBILITY USING ZONTAGO BALANCE ACTIVITIES #14, 17 AND 18 @ RPE ON
BORG 16/20 AND DYSPNEA SCALE 2
3/10. EDUC INTERVENTIONS -T/l TO DO ROM EX TO BLE'S IN SITTING/STANDING POSITION x 15REPS EACH, 2X A DAY. T/l TO KEEP PATHWAYS.
CLEAR TO DECREASE RISK OF FALLS. T/| PLB BEFORE, DURING ANDAFTER ACTIVITIES. T/| PROPER STANDING POSTURE TO IMPROVE
POSTURAL ALIGNMENT AND SYMMETRY. PATIENT VERBALIZED UNDERSTANDING TO ALL INSTRUCTIONS TODAY.
3. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: GAIT TRAINING + 4VCS ("LOOK STRAIGHT AHEAD" X 2, "PICK UP FEET HIGHER", "BIGGER STEPS") TO IMPROVE POSTURAL
ALIGNMENT, FOOT CLEARANCE AND LIMB ADVANCEMENT USING GAIT TRAINING, 90 FEET x 2 (6MINS WITH 4MINS SEATED REST BREAK NEEDED
TO RECOVER FATIGUE) USING A FWW WITH SBA-CGAX1 ON EVEN/UNEVEN TERRAIN, RPE ON BORG 17/20 AND DYSPNEA SCALE 2/10.
4. ASSIST IN PERFORMANCE OF BALANCE EXERCISES IN STATIC AND OR DYNAMIC POSITIONS.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN POSTURAL AWARENESS, WEIGHT SHIFTING AND REACHING ACTIVITY WITH
UPPER EXTREMITY SUPPORT WITH OCCASIONAL LOSS OF BALANCE NOTED.
: 1. PATIENT /CAREGIVER DEMONSTRATES SAFE TRANSFERS USING APPROPRIATE BODY MECHANICS AND / OR EQUIPMENT.

WALKS OCCASIONALLY - WALKS OCCASIONALLY DURING DAY, BUT FOR VERY SHORT DISTANCES, WITH OR WITHOUT ASSISTANCE.
SPENDS MAJORITY OF DAY IN BED OR CHAIR.
CARDIOVASCULAR
IE THE PATIENT IS EASILY FATIGUED OR SHOWS DYSPNEA ON EXERTION IS THERE A NEED FOR ENERGY CONSERVATION TRAINING?
NO
BRADEN RISK ASSESSMENT SCALE
MOBILITY -ABILITY TO CHANGE AND CONTROL BODY POSITION
SLIGHTLY LIMITED - MAKES FREQUENT THOUGH SLIGHT CHANGES IN BODY OR EXTREMITY POSITION INDEPENDENTLY.
NUTRITION - USUAL FOOD INTAKE PATTERN
ADEQUATE - EATS OVER HALF OF MOST MEALS. EATS A TOTAL OF 4 SERVINGS OF PROTEIN (MEAT, DAIRY PRODUCTS) PER DAY.
OCCASIONALLY WILL REFUSE A MEAL, BUT WILL USUALLY TAKE A SUPPLEMENT WHEN OFFERED, OR IS ON A TUBE FEEDING OR TPN
REGIMEN, WHICH PROBABLY MEETS MOST OF NUTRITIONAL NEEDS.
ERICTION AND SHEAR
POTENTIAL PROBLEM - MOVES FEEBLY OR REQUIRES MINIMUM ASSISTANCE. DURING A MOVE SKIN PROBABLY SLIDES TO SOME EXTENT
AGAINST SHEETS, CHAIR, RESTRAINTS, OR OTHER DEVICES. MAINTAINS RELATIVELY GOOD POSITION IN CHAIR OR BED MOST OF THE
TIME BUT OCCASIONALLY SLIDES DOWN.
TOTAL SCORE (PATIENTS WITH A TOTAL SCORE OF 12 OR LESS ARE CONSIDERED TO BE AT HIGH RISK OF DEVELOPING PRESSURE
ULCERS):

REGIMEN BY 9.20.20

PATIENT / CAREGIVER WILL VERBALIZE/DEMONSTRATE ABILITY TO PERFORM WOUND CARE. WOUND STATUS WILL IMPROVE AS

Supporting Documentation for Home Health Eligibility:
FOCUS OF CARE (SUPPORTING CLINICAL INFORMATION, PERTINENT MEDICAL HISTORY, ADDITIONAL DIAGNOSIS, LAST REHOSPITALIZATION,
ER, OR URGENCY CARE VISIT):
CARE PLAN ASSESSMENT, PHE, MEDICATION MANAGEMENT AND DISEASE PROCESSES. RELATED TO RECENT FALL AND SUBDURAL
HEMORRHAGE.
79 YEAR OLD FEMALE, LIVES WITH SPOUSE WHO IS PRIMARY CAREGIVER. HE IS WILLING AND ABLE TO PROVIDE CARE FOR PATIENT.
PATIENT WENT TO OUTPATIENT REGISTRATION ON JULY 14TH, FOR PLACEMENT OF PORT FOR FUTURE DIALYSIS USE. PATIENT WAS
AMBULATING WITH ROLLING WALKER IN REGISTRATION AREA, LOST BALANCE AND FELL BACKWARDS. SUSTAINED LACERATION TO
POSTERIOR SCALP, AND BRUISING TO LEFT SIDE OH, SHE DID NOT LOSE CONSCIOUSNESS. PATIENT WAS ADMITTED TO HOSPITAL WITH
DIAGNOSIS SUBDURAL HEMORRHAGE. PORT PLACEMENT WAS PUT ON HOLD.
ADDITIONAL DIAGNOSIS A-FIB HYPERLIPIDEMIA HYPERTENSION CAD HYPOTHYROIDISM BREAST CANCER ANEMIA GOUT GERD CKD 4.
HOSPITALIZATION AT BAPTIST LOUISVILLE 7.14 - 7.16
THE PATIENT IS CONSIDERED HOMEBOUND/CONFINED TO HOME BECAUSE: (MARK ALL THAT APPLY)
ASSISTANCE OF ANOTHER PERSON IN ORDER TO LEAVE PLACE OF RESIDENCE - LEVEL 1, BECAUSE OF ILLNESS OR INJURY, PATIENT
NEEDS AID OF SUPPORTIVE DEVICES -WALKER - LEVEL 1, PATIENT HAS A CONDITION SUCH THAT LEAVING HOME IS MEDICALLY
CONTRAINDICATED - LEVEL 1
DOCUMENT PATIENT'S CONDITION AND LIMITATIONS AS IT RELATES TO THEIR HOMEBOUND STATUS
PATIENT REQUIRES ASSIST OF ONE FOR ALL AMBULATION WITH ROLLING WALKER, REQUIRES CUEING AND TOUCHING ASSIST DUE TO
POOR BALANCE AT TIMES. PATIENT IS HIGH RISK FOR CONTRACTING COVID-19 DUE TO AGE AND DISEASE PROCESSES.
DOES THE PATIENT MEET LEVEL 2 CRITERIA - NORMAL INABILITY TO LEAVE THE HOME EXISTS AND LEAVING HOME REQUIRES A
CONSIDERABLE AND TAXING EFFORT?
WHERE A PHYSICIAN HAS DETERMINED THAT IT IS MEDICALLY CONTRAINDICATED FOR A BENEFICIARY TO LEAVE THE HOME BECAUSE
THE PATIENT HAS A CONDITION THAT MAY MAKE THE PATIENT MORE SUSCEPTIBLE TO CONTRACTING COVID-19

NOTIFY SN IF ANY ABNORMAL WOUND FINDINGS SUCH AS INCREASED
DRAINAGE, FOUL ODOR, OR ACUTE PAIN ONSET. DISCUSSED IMPORTANCE OF
MAINTAINING A CLEAN ATMOSPHERE AND WASHING HANDS BEFORE AND

WOUND HEALING CONCEPTS AND THEORIES APPROPRIATE FOR THIS
PATIENTS LEVEL OF UNDERSTANDING, REVIEWED AND ACKNOWLEDGED.

ASSESSMENT.

Note
Note Status
PATIENT WAS SEEN LAST WEEK AT PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIANS OFFICE. Active
PATIENT SUBMITTED LAB SAMPLES. THE LAB SAMPLE RESULTS WERE SENT
TO HER NEPHROLOGIST DR. RAZA. DR. RAZA INITIATED CEPHALEXIN 500 MG
CAPSULES ONE TWICE A DAY FOR 7 DAYS FOR A POSITIVE UA. PATIENT
STATES SHES HAD NO UTI SXS AND WAS SUPRISED TO GET ABX TX. THIS
MORNING PATIENT HAS BEEN HAVING INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH
AMBULATION RESULTING IN SEVERAL ALMOST FALLS AND TWO ACTUAL FALLS
OVER THE LAST WEEK. PATIENT HAS BRUISING ABOVE HER RIGHT EYE AND
MULTIPLE BRUISING ABOUT HER UPPER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT SKIN IS CLEAN
DRY AND INTACT AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE LAST FALL OCCURRED WHEN
THE PATIENT WAS TRYING TO EXIT A VEHICLE. PATIENT CAREGIVER STATES
THAT HE THINKS HER SHOES MAY HAVE BEEN A LITTLE BIT TOO BIG FOR HER
AND SHE MAY HAVE TRIPPED OVER SHOES. PATIENT ADVISED TO WEAR
STABLE PLATFORM SHOES AND TO MOVE SLOW AND WAIT FOR ASSISTANCE
FOR ALL TRANSFERS TO REDUCE FALL OCCURRENCES. PATIENT AND
CAREGIVERS STATE THAT SHE SEEMS TO JUST LOSE HER BALANCE WITHOUT
WARNING. BG AND BP HAVE REMAINED STABLE. PATIENT AND CG STATE THEY
BELIEVE ALL FALLS ARE RELATED TO WEAKNESS. PATIENT ADMITS TO NOT
FOLLOWING THROUGH WITH THERAPY EXERCISES BETWEEN VISITS. FALL
PRECAUTIONS REVIEWED. PATIENT VERBALIZES AGREEMENT. THERE HAVE
BEEN NO OTHER MEDICATION CHANGES AND PATIENT STATES WITH THE
EXCEPTION OF THE INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH BALANCE THAT SHE
REMAINS STABLE AND IN GOOD SHAPE.
SPOKE WITH CG PRIOR TO VISIT DENIED S/S OFCOVID 19 Active

THIS PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 ANDABLE TO VERBALIZE Active
NEEDS WITHOUT DIFFICULTIES. THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR ACCESS
SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM YESTERDAY WITH DR. DWIVEDI.
DISCHARGE PAPERWORK SAYS LEAVE CURRENT DRESSING ON RT UPPER
ARMIN PLACE UNTIL TOMORROW 11-19-20 AND THEN LEAVE STERI-STRIPS IN
PLACE UNTIL THEY FALL OFF NATURALLY. SKILLED NURSING INTENDS TO
ASSESS FOR DRESSING NEEDS WANTS CURRENT DRESSING IS REMOVED
ANDTHEAREA IS ASSESSED TOMORROW. ALSO PATIENT HAS NEW
PRESCRIPTION FOR PAIN MEDICATION. NEW ORDERS RECEIVED WITH
BRITNEY WITH DR MADERIC. PATIENTS SCHEDULED FOR SKILLED NURSING
RECERTIFICATION SECONDARY TO NEW SURGICAL WOUND AT RIGHT UPPER
ARM. PATIENT REMAINS STABLE AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE DRESSING THAT
IS IN PLACE DOES SHOW SOME DRAINAGE THAT IS BLOODY AND NATURE. THE
DRESSING IS INTACT AND THE EXTREMITY IS NOT SWOLLEN AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES PAIN IS CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT
BUT THE AREA REMAINS TENDER. PATIENT ALSO COMPLAINS OF A SLIGHT
SORE THROAT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS
OBSERVED STATED.

SKILLED NURSE TO OBSERVE/ASSESS INTEGUMENTARY SYSTEM, IDENTIFY CHANGES, AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. SKILLED
NURSE TO TEACH RELATED TO ALTERED SKIN INTEGRITY INCLUDING DISEASE PROCESS, NUTRITION, MEDICATIONS, ACTIVITIES, AND
PERSONAL/ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS. SIGNIFICANT CHANGES IN STATUS WILL BE REPORTED TO PHYSICIAN. NURSING ASSESSMENT TO BE

SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM MULTIFACTOR FALL RISK ASSESSMENT AND IMPLEMENT INTERVENTIONS TO DECREASE RISK OF FALLS. SKILLED
NURSE TO INSTRUCT ON HOME SAFETY, IMPACT OF POLYPHARMACY, ENVIRONMENTAL SAFETY, AND FALL PREVENTION.
SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM OBSERVATION/ASSESSMENT OF GENITOURINARY STATUS AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS OF
DISEASE PROCESS. SKILLED NURSE TO PROVIDE INSTRUCTION REGARDING MANAGEMENT OF DISEASE PROCESS, INCLUDING
PATHOPHYSIOLOGY, NUTRITIONAL/FLUID REQUIREMENTS AND MEDICATION REGIMEN.
SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM/TEACH WOUND CARE TO RT UPPEREXTSURGICALAREA. APPLY TELFA LIKE PAD, COVER WITH ROLLED GAUZE ,
SECURE WITH TAPE USING CLEAN/ASEPTIC TECHNIQUE. CHANGE DRESSING EVERY 48 HOURS AND PRN SOILING/DISLODGEMENT. MAY USE
EQUIVALENT PRODUCTS. ONCE TAUGHT, PATIENT/CAREGIVER TO PERFORM ON DAYS SKILLED NURSE IS NOT IN HOME.
SKILLED NURSE TO PROVIDE INSTRUCT REGARDING INTERVENTION(S) TO PREVENT PRESSURE ULCERS.
SKILLED NURSE TO PROVIDE INSTRUCTIONS RELATED TO MANAGEMENT OF CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO
DEFINITION, RISKS FACTORS, MEASURES TO PREVENT EXACERBATION, SIGNS/SYMPTOMS AND POTENTIAL COMPLICATIONS AND TO REPORT
WEIGHT GAIN OF 3 LBS/DAY OR 5 LBS/WK.
SKILLED NURSE TO PROVIDE MONITORING FOR THE PRESENCE OF SKIN LESIONS ON THE LOWER EXTREMITIES AND
TEACHING/REINFORCEMENT REGARDING PROPER DIABETIC FOOT CARE.
SKILLED NURSE TO REVIEW MEDICATION PROFILE AND RECONCILE MEDICATIONS AS NEEDED. SKILLED NURSE MAY INSTRUCT AND REINFORCE

SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM/TEACH WOUND CARE TO RT UPPEREXTSURGICALAREA. APPLY TELFA LIKE PAD, COVER WITH ROLLED GAUZE ,
SECURE WITH TAPE USING CLEAN/ASEPTIC TECHNIQUE. CHANGE DRESSING EVERY 48 HOURS AND PRN SOILING/DISLODGEMENT. MAY USE
EQUIVALENT PRODUCTS. ONCE TAUGHT, PATIENT/CAREGIVER TO PERFORM ON DAYS SKILLED NURSE IS NOT IN HOME.
SKILLED NURSE TO PROVIDE INSTRUCT REGARDING INTERVENTION(S) TO PREVENT PRESSURE ULCERS.
SKILLED NURSE TO PROVIDE INSTRUCTIONS RELATED TO MANAGEMENT OF CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO

Client Coordination Note Report
Client: PRINCE, BETTY M Insured ID: H43132048 Primary Payor: HUMANA PDGM
MR No: B3600047293201
Legacy MR No:
DOB [DOB-1]
Primary Physician Phone Fax
MADERIC, SUSAN F. MD [PHONE-1227] [PHONE-1228]
Note Date Note Type Entered By Note Status
[DOB-4584] NARRATIVE BRIAN WOOSLEY, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
PATIENT WAS SEEN LAST WEEK AT PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIANS OFFICE. PATIENT SUBMITTED LAB
SAMPLES. THE LAB SAMPLE RESULTS WERE SENT TO HER NEPHROLOGIST DR. RAZA. DR. RAZA
INITIATED CEPHALEXIN 500 MG CAPSULES ONE TWICE A DAY FOR 7 DAYS FOR A POSITIVE UA.
PATIENT STATES SHES HAD NO UTI SXS AND WAS SUPRISED TO GET ABX TX. THIS MORNING
PATIENT HAS BEEN HAVING INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH AMBULATION RESULTING IN SEVERAL
ALMOST FALLS AND TWO ACTUAL FALLS OVER THE LAST WEEK. PATIENT HAS BRUISING ABOVE
HER RIGHT EYE AND MULTIPLE BRUISING ABOUT HER UPPER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT SKIN IS
CLEAN DRY AND INTACT AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE LAST FALL OCCURRED WHEN THE PATIENT
WAS TRYING TO EXIT A VEHICLE. PATIENT CAREGIVER STATES THAT HE THINKS HER SHOES MAY
HAVE BEEN A LITTLE BIT TOO BIG FOR HER AND SHE MAY HAVE TRIPPED OVER SHOES. PATIENT
ADVISED TO WEAR STABLE PLATFORM SHOES AND TO MOVE SLOW AND WAIT FOR ASSISTANCE
FOR ALL TRANSFERS TO REDUCE FALL OCCURRENCES. PATIENT AND CAREGIVERS STATE THAT
SHE SEEMS TO JUST LOSE HER BALANCE WITHOUT WARNING. BG AND BP HAVE REMAINED
STABLE. PATIENT AND CG STATE THEY BELIEVE ALL FALLS ARE RELATED TO WEAKNESS.
PATIENT ADMITS TO NOT FOLLOWING THROUGH WITH THERAPY EXERCISES BETWEEN VISITS.
FALL PRECAUTIONS REVIEWED. PATIENT VERBALIZES AGREEMENT. THERE HAVE BEEN NO
OTHER MEDICATION CHANGES AND PATIENT STATES WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE INCREASED
DIFFICULTY WITH BALANCE THAT SHE REMAINS STABLE AND IN GOOD SHAPE.

Note
THIS PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3AND ABLE TO VERBALIZE NEEDS WITHOUT
DIFFICULTIES. PATIENTS BLOOD SUGARS HAVE STABILIZED. PATIENTS BLOOD PRESSURE
REMAINS LOW BUT PATIENT IS STABLE. PATIENT SKIN IS CLEAN DRY AND INTACT AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED OR
STATED AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT FEELS THAT SKILLED NURSING IS NO LONGER NEEDED
AT THIS TIME AND IS IN FAVOR OF CONTINUING PHYSICAL THERAPY AS PER ORDERS. SKILLED
NURSING INTENDS TO DISCIPLINE DISCHARGE THIS PATIENT NEXT SKILLED NURSING VISIT. WE'LL
DISCUSS THIS PATIENT'S CASE AT CASE CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR TOMORROW. PATIENT
STATES UTI SYMPTOMS HAVE RESOLVED AND SHE HAS COMPLETED HER ANTIBIOTIC TREATMENT
AT THIS TIME. PATIENT DENIES ANY FURTHER UTI LIKE SYMPTOMS AT THIS ASSESSMENT.

THIS PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3AND ABLE TO VERBALIZE NEEDS WITHOUT
DIFFICULTIES. THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR ACCESS SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM
YESTERDAY WITH DR. DWIVEDI. DISCHARGE PAPERWORK SAYS LEAVE CURRENT DRESSING ON
RT UPPER ARM IN PLACE UNTIL TOMORROW 11-19-20 AND THEN LEAVE STERI-STRIPS IN PLACE
UNTIL THEY FALL OFF NATURALLY. SKILLED NURSING INTENDS TO ASSESS FOR DRESSING
NEEDS WANTS CURRENT DRESSING IS REMOVED AND THE AREA IS ASSESSED TOMORROW.
ALSO PATIENT HAS NEW PRESCRIPTION FOR PAIN MEDICATION. NEW ORDERS RECEIVED WITH
BRITNEY WITH DR MADERIC. PATIENTS SCHEDULED FOR SKILLED NURSING RECERTIFICATION
SECONDARY TO NEW SURGICAL WOUND AT RIGHT UPPER ARM. PATIENT REMAINS STABLE AT
THIS ASSESSMENT. THE DRESSING THAT IS IN PLACE DOES SHOW SOME DRAINAGE THAT IS
BLOODY AND NATURE. THE DRESSING IS INTACT AND THE EXTREMITY IS NOT SWOLLEN AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES PAIN IS CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT BUT THE AREA
REMAINS TENDER. PATIENT ALSO COMPLAINS OF A SLIGHT SORE THROAT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS
AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED STATED.

Note
PATIENT WAS SEEN LAST WEEK AT PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIANS OFFICE. PATIENT SUBMITTED LAB SAMPLES. THE LAB SAMPLE RESULTS WERE
SENT TO HER NEPHROLOGIST DR. RAZA. DR. RAZA INITIATED CEPHALEXIN 500 MG CAPSULES ONE TWICE A DAY FOR 7 DAYS FOR A POSITIVE UA.
PATIENT STATES SHES HAD NO UTI SXS AND WAS SUPRISED TO GET ABXTX. THIS MORNING PATIENT HAS BEEN HAVING INCREASED DIFFICULTY
WITH AMBULATION RESULTING IN SEVERAL ALMOST FALLS AND TWO ACTUAL FALLS OVER THE LAST WEEK. PATIENT HAS BRUISING ABOVE HER
RIGHT EYE AND MULTIPLE BRUISING ABOUT HER UPPER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT SKIN IS CLEAN DRY AND INTACT AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE LAST
FALL OCCURRED WHEN THE PATIENT WAS TRYING TO EXIT A VEHICLE. PATIENT CAREGIVER STATES THAT HE THINKS HER SHOES MAY HAVE
BEEN ALITTLE BIT TOO BIG FOR HER AND SHE MAY HAVE TRIPPED OVER SHOES. PATIENT ADVISED TO WEARSTABLE PLATFORM SHOES AND TO
MOVE SLOW ANDWAIT FOR ASSISTANCE FOR ALL TRANSFERS TO REDUCE FALL OCCURRENCES. PATIENT AND CAREGIVERS STATE THATSHE
SEEMS TO JUST LOSE HER BALANCE WITHOUT WARNING. BG AND BP HAVE REMAINED STABLE. PATIENT AND CG STATE THEY BELIEVE ALL FALLS
ARE RELATED TO WEAKNESS. PATIENT ADMITS TO NOT FOLLOWING THROUGH WITH THERAPY EXERCISES BETWEEN VISITS. FALL
PRECAUTIONS REVIEWED. PATIENT VERBALIZES AGREEMENT. THERE HAVE BEEN NO OTHER MEDICATION CHANGES AND PATIENT STATES WITH
THE EXCEPTION OF THE INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH BALANCE THAT SHE REMAINS STABLE AND IN GOOD SHAPE.

[DOB-4427] NARRATIVE BRIAN WOOSLEY, RN
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
THIS PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 AND ABLE TO VERBALIZE NEEDS WITHOUT DIFFICULTIES. THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR ACCESS
SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM YESTERDAY WITH DR. DWIVEDI. DISCHARGE PAPERWORK SAYS LEAVE CURRENT DRESSING ON RT UPPER
ARM IN PLACE UNTIL TOMORROW 11-19-20 ANDTHEN LEAVE STERI-STRIPS IN PLACE UNTIL THEY FALL OFF NATURALLY. SKILLED NURSING
INTENDS TO ASSESS FOR DRESSING NEEDS WANTS CURRENT DRESSING IS REMOVED AND THE AREA IS ASSESSED TOMORROW. ALSO PATIENT
HAS NEW PRESCRIPTION FOR PAIN MEDICATION. NEW ORDERS RECEIVED WITH BRITNEY WITH DR MADERIC. PATIENTS SCHEDULED FOR
SKILLED NURSING RECERTIFICATION SECONDARY TO NEW SURGICAL WOUND AT RIGHT UPPER ARM. PATIENT REMAINS STABLE AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. THE DRESSING THAT IS IN PLACE DOES SHOW SOME DRAINAGE THAT IS BLOODY AND NATURE. THE DRESSING IS INTACT AND
THE EXTREMITY IS NOT SWOLLEN AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES PAIN IS CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT BUT THE AREA REMAINS
TENDER. PATIENT ALSO COMPLAINS OF A SLIGHT SORE THROAT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED
STATED.
PHYSICIAN CONTACTED AND APPROVED ORDERS IN PLAN, NAME OF CONTACT PERSON BRITTANY WITH DR. MADERIC

SN FOR WOUND ASSESSMENT AND CARE, CP ASSESS, MEDICATION REGIMEN REVIEW, MEDICATION ADHERENCE PROMOTION, MEDICATION

RECENT VASCULAR ACCESS PROCEDURE TO RT UPPER EXT. 1TIME PER WEEK FOR 3 WEEKS.
THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR ACCESS SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM 11-17-20 WITH DR. DWIVEDI RELATED TO ESRD PREPARATION.

DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED WITH 1TO 2 VERBAL / VISUAL CUES WITH SBA - STANDING LE EXERCISES X 20REPS: TO IMPROVE
STRENGTH -
- HEEL/TOE RAISES
- HIP ABDUCTION
- HIP FLEXION
- HIP EXTENSION
- HAM CURLS
- MARCHING IN PLACE
- MINI SQUATS
RPE BORG 13/20
2. PROVIDE MUSCLE RE EDUCATION THERAPY TO INCREASE NEUROMUSCULAR FUNCTION.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SETUP WITH CGA AND 2 TO 3 VC FOR NMR USING SENSORY INTEGRATION WITH STANDING OVER AIREX X5 MINUTES
EO /EC 2 HANDS /1 HAND/ NO HANDS/WITH NORMAL AND NARROW BOS -
X 308 EACH
HELD - MCTSIB 4X 108.
ZONTAGO WARMUPS 1AND 2X5 REPS
RPE BORG 14/20
3. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SETUP WITH 3 TO 4 VERBAL CUES TO IMPROVE STEPPING STRATEGIES FOR STEP CLEARANCE, OBSTACLE
NEGOTIATION , DIRECTIONAL CHANGES, AND BALANCE WITH WEIGHT SHIFTING - USING GT STEPPING OVER OBJECTS PLACED ALONG
PATHWAY WITH RW X 200 FEET- REQUIRED VC TO STEP OVER NOT AROUND THE OBJECTS - RPE BORG 13/20 DYSPNEA 0
4. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT INSTRUCTED TO:
KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE; PT IS TRYING TO WALK SOME WITHOUT THEWALKER - PT EDUCATION TO USE AT ALL TIMES - HIGH FALL
RISK
WEAR NON-SLIP FOOTWEAR; KEEP PATHWAYS WELL LIT
5. INSTRUCT PATIENT IN BALANCE TRAINING ACTIVITIES TO REDUCE FALL RISK
DETAILS/COMMENTS: LISTED ABOVE
6. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE
PROVIDED):
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SUPERVISION FOR TRANSFERS , GT WITH RW ,AND CLOSE SBA/CGA FOR STANDING BALANCE

DETAILS/COMMENTS: SUPERVISION WITH 1TO 2 VC FOR APPROPRIATE TECHNIQUE FOR LE THER EXIN STANDING- EXERCISES X 7 MINUTES -
RW SUPPORT
STANDING LE EXERCISES X 20 REPS:
- HEEL/TOE RAISES
- HIP ABDUCTION
- HIP FLEXION
- HIP EXTENSION
- HAM CURLS
- MARCHING IN PLACE
- MINI SQUATS
RPE
DYSPNEA 2/10
BORG 13/20

DETAILS/COMMENTS: TASK DESIGN WITH CLOSE SBA AND 2 TO 3 VC TO IMPROVE STANDING BALANCE -STOOD UP TO 7 MINUTES -
ZONTAGO WARMUPS #1, #2 ,#4X5 REPS NO HANDS
SEMI TANDEM 3X10S HOLD NO HANDS ,TANDEM 1HAND SUPPORT 3 REPS X 10S HOLD,
SIT TO STAND X5 REPS NO HANDS X 2 TRIALS
FOR 26S AND 218- RPE BORG 14/20

DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT REQUIRED SUPERVISION OR TOUCHING ASSISTANCE FOR GT WITH RW 2TO3

STEP CLEARANCE;
STEP WIDTH;
POSTURE;
NO LOSS OF BALANCE FOR HOUSEHOLD DISTANCES IN HOME
4. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT INSTRUCTED TO:
KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE;
WEAR NON-SLIP FOOTWEAR;
5. INSTRUCT PATIENT IN BALANCE TRAINING ACTIVITIES TO REDUCE FALL RISK
DETAILS/COMMENTS: LISTED ABOVE
6. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE

DETAILS/COMMENTS: SUPERVISION FOR GT AND TRANSFERS , CLOSE SBA FOR BALANCE ACTIVITIES IN STANDING

“1. INSTRUCT IN THERAPEUTIC EXERCISES AND HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SUPERVISION WITH 1VC FOR LE THER EXIN STANDING TO IMPROVE STRENGTH -
STANDING LE EXERCISES X 20 REPS: RW FOR BUE SUPPORT -
- HEEL/TOE RAISES
- HIP ABDUCTION
- HIP FLEXION
- HIP EXTENSION
- HAM CURLS
- MARCHING IN PLACE
- MINI SQUATS
RPE BORG 13/20 DYSPNEA 0
SETUP WITH SUPERVISION AND 1TO 2 VC FOR 5XSIT TO STANDS NO HANDS TO IMPROVE POWER WITH TRANSFERS AND POSTURAL
ADJUSTMENTS WITH TRANSITIONAL MOVEMENTS -X 2 TRIALS - 24S AND 27S RPE BORG 14/20
2. PROVIDE MUSCLE RE EDUCATION THERAPY TO INCREASE NEUROMUSCULAR FUNCTION.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SETUP WITH VERY CLOSE SBA /CGA TO IMPROVE BALANCE WITH SENSORY INTEGRATION - PT STOOD UP TO 11M30S
TOTAL BEFORE REQUIRING A SEATED RECOVERY -
EC NO HANDS FIRM SURFACE X 308 , ZONTAGO WARMUPS 1, 2, 4X5 REPS NO HANDS,
OVER AIREXX3M30S WITH NARROW BOS EC 2 FINGERS X 308 , 1FINGER X 30S , NO HANDS EC X 108.
RPE BORG 14/20 DYSPNEA 0
3. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SUPERVISION WITH 2 TO 3 VC FOR GT WITH ROLLATOR IN HOME WITH FOCUS ON DIRECTIONAL CHANGES AND
SENSORY INTEGRATION TO REDUCE FALL RISK WITH GAIT - X 20 FEET X 6 REPS WITH EACH DIRECTION - FORWARD WITH HEAD TURNS ,
RETRO, 180 DEGREE TURNS TO LAND R - NO LOB. RPE BORG 13/20 DYSPNEA 0
4. INSTRUCT IN PROPER SKIN CARE FOR THE DIABETIC PATIENT, INCLUDING PROTECTIVE FOOTWEAR, FOOT CARE AND INSPECTION.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: WEARSHOES THATSUPPORT AND FIT PROPERLY.
WEARSOCKS THAT ARE CLEAN AND FIT PROPERLY.
5. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT INSTRUCTED TO:
KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE; ROLLATOR OR RW AT ALL TIMES - DO NOT ATTEMPT TO WALK WITHOUT AD
WEAR NON-SLIP FOOTWEAR;
6. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE
PROVIDED):
DETAILS/COMMENTS: LISTED ABOVE

8. REACHING FORWARD WITH OUTSTRETCHED ARM WHILE STANDING
INSTRUCTIONS: LIFT ARM TO 90 DEGREES. STRETCH OUT YOUR FINGERS AND REACH FORWARD AS FAR AS YOU CAN. (EXAMINER PLACES
A RULER AT END OF FINGERTIPS WHEN ARM IS AT 90 DEGREES. FINGERS SHOULD NOT TOUCH THE RULER WHILE REACHING FORWARD.
THE RECORDED MEASURE IS THE DISTANCE FORWARD THATTHE FINGERS REACHES WHILE THE SUBJECT IS IN THE MOST FORWARD
LEAN POSITION. WHEN POSSIBLE.ASK SUBJECT TO USE BOTH ARMS WHEN REACHING TO AVOID ROTATION OF THE TRUNK.)
1-REACHES FORWARD BUT NEEDS SUPERVISION
9g.PICK UP OBJECT FROM THE FLOOR FROM A STANDING POSITION

0 - UNABLE TO TRY/NEEDS ASSIST TO KEEP FROM LOSING BALANCE OR FALLING
10. TURNING TO LOOK BEHIND OVER LEFT AND RIGHT SHOULDERS WHILE STANDING
INSTRUCTIONS: TURN TO LOOK DIRECTLY BEHIND YOU OVER TOWARD LEFT SHOULDER. REPEAT TO THE RIGHT.
(EXAMINER MAY PICK AN OBJECT TO LOOK AT DIRECTLY BEHIND THE SUBJECT TO ENCOURAGE A BETTER TWIST TURN.)
1- NEEDS SUPERVISION WHEN TURNING
11. TURN 360 DEGREES
INSTRUCTIONS: TURN COMPLETELY AROUND IN A FULL CIRCLE. PAUSE. THEN TURN A FULL CIRCLE IN THE OTHER
DIRECTION.
1- NEEDS CLOSE SUPERVISION OR VERBAL CUEING

«CONTINUE PER POC
PATIENT WAS SEEN LAST WEEK AT PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIANS OFFICE. PATIENT SUBMITTED LAB SAMPLES. THE LAB SAMPLE RESULTS WERE
SENT TO HER NEPHROLOGIST DR. RAZA. DR. RAZA INITIATED CEPHALEXIN 500 MG CAPSULES ONE TWICE A DAY FOR 7 DAYS FOR A POSITIVE
UA. PATIENT STATES SHES HAD NO UTI SXS AND WAS SUPRISED TO GET ABX TX. THIS MORNING PATIENT HAS BEEN HAVING INCREASED
DIFFICULTY WITH AMBULATION RESULTING IN SEVERAL ALMOST FALLS AND TWO ACTUAL FALLS OVER THE LAST WEEK. PATIENT HAS
BRUISING ABOVE HER RIGHT EYE AND MULTIPLE BRUISING ABOUT HER UPPER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT SKIN IS CLEAN DRY AND INTACT AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. THE LAST FALL OCCURRED WHEN THE PATIENT WAS TRYING TO EXIT A VEHICLE. PATIENT CAREGIVER STATES THAT HE
THINKS HER SHOES MAY HAVE BEEN A LITTLE BIT TOO BIG FOR HER AND SHE MAY HAVE TRIPPED OVER SHOES. PATIENT ADVISED TO WEAR
STABLE PLATFORM SHOES ANDTO MOVE SLOW AND WAIT FOR ASSISTANCE FOR ALL TRANSFERS TO REDUCE FALL OCCURRENCES. PATIENT
AND CAREGIVERS STATE THAT SHE SEEMS TO JUST LOSE HER BALANCE WITHOUT WARNING. BG AND BP HAVE REMAINED STABLE. PATIENT
AND CG STATE THEY BELIEVE ALL FALLS ARE RELATED TO WEAKNESS. PATIENT ADMITS TO NOT FOLLOWING THROUGH WITH THERAPY
EXERCISES BETWEEN VISITS. FALL PRECAUTIONS REVIEWED. PATIENT VERBALIZES AGREEMENT. THERE HAVE BEEN NO OTHER MEDICATION
CHANGES AND PATIENT STATES WITH THE EXCEPTION OF THE INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH BALANCE THATSHE REMAINS STABLE AND IN
GOOD SHAPE.
Patient Goal
NO MORE FALLS

AND WHEN TO REPORT PROBLEMS THAT MAY OCCUR
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED ON HOW AND WHEN TO REPORT PROBLEMS THAT MAY OCCUR DUE TO HIGH-RISK MEDICATIONS
2. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER IN MEASURES TO PREVENT INFECTION OR CONTAMINATION.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON MEASURES NECESSARY TO PREVENT INFECTION OR CONTAMINATION.
INSTRUCTED PATIENT/CAREGIVER IN ALL ASPECTS OF EFFECTIVE HAND WASHING TECHNIQUES, TECHNIQUES TO AVOID BODY
FLUIDS/EXCRETIONS.
INSTRUCTED PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON PROPER CARE AND DISPOSAL OF BODY FLUIDS/EXCRETIONS, SOILED DRESSINGS, SUPPLIES, WASHING
OF LINENS, USE OF BLEACH ETC.
3. ASSESS AND EVALUATE CO-MORBID CONDITIONS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: REVIEWED AND INSTRUCTED ON RECENT EXACERBATION OF CO-MORBID CONDITIONS
WHEN TO REPORT TO NURSE/PHYSICIAN
DIETARY RESTRICTIONS THAT PROMOTE CONTROL OF CARDIAC DISEASE
MEDICATION REGIMEN, SIDE EFFECTS/DESIRED EFFECTS, POTENTIAL INTERACTIONS AND REFILL PROCESS
BALANCING ACTIVITIES AND NEED FOR FREQUENT REST PERIODS
ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS
DISEASE PROCESS
4. PROVIDE SKILLED ASSESSMENT AND BASIC INSTRUCTION FOR PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON FACTORS TO PREVENT PRESSURE ULCERS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SIGNS / SYMPTOMS OF SKIN BREAKDOWN - PALE OR REDDENED AREA, BLISTER, PAIN, ITCHING, BOGGY AREA, LOSS OF
TISSUE TO INCLUDE BOTH LAYERS OF SKIN.
IF PATIENT IS RESTRICTED TO BED IMPLEMENT A TURNING SCHEDULE WHICH RESTRICTS THE PATIENT'S TIME IN ONE POSITION FOR 2
HOURS OR LESS
IMPLEMENT A SKIN CARE REGIMEN THAT INCLUDES: KEEPING THE SKIN CLEAN AND APPROPRIATELY MOISTUREIZED, CLEANSING THE SKIN
PROMPTLY AFTER EPISODES OF INCONTINENCE, AVOIDING THE USE OF HARSH SOAPS AND CLEANERS, PROTECTING THE SKIN FROM
MOISTURE WIHT A BARRIER PRODUCT.
ENCOURAGE ADEQUATE NUTRITION AND HYDRATION (GOOD CALORIC INTAKE AND INCLUDE PROTEIN)
LEAVE BLISTERS INTACT
5. INSTRUCT ON CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE TO INCLUDE DISEASE PROCESS, SIGNS / SYMPTOMS, COMPLICATIONS AND MANAGEMENT.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT/CAREGIVER RE: DEFINITION OF CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE: HEART IS UNABLE TO PUMP AN
ADEQUATE SUPPLY OF BLOOD TO MEET THE OXYGEN AND NUTRITIONAL NEEDS OF THE BODY. THE HEART DOESN'T PUMP WELLAND CAUSES
A BACKUP OF BLOOD IN THE VEINS. A BACKUP OF BLOOD IN THE VEINS OF THE LUNGS CAUSES SHORTNESS OF BREATH. A BACKUP OF
BLOOD IN THE VEINS OF THE LEGS CAUSES EDEMA (SWELLING).
INSTRUCTED PATIENT /CAREGIVER ON COMMON SYMPTOMS OF HEART FAILURE INCLUDING: SHORTNESS OF BREATH DURING PHYSICAL
ACTIVITY OR EVEN WHILE LYING IN BED, WAKING UP DUE TO SHORTNESS OF BREATH, FREQUENT COUGH OR WHEEZING, BLOATING,
SWOLLEN FEET/LEGS/ANKLES, THE NEED TO URINATE MORE FREQUENTLY DURING THE NIGHT, ASUDDEN WEIGHTGAIN, NAUSEA OR LOSS OF
APPETITE, FATIGUE OR WEAKNESS, CONFUSION OR DECREASED ALERTNESS, RAPID OR IRREGULAR HEART BEAT, AND SWOLLEN NECK
VEINS.
INSTRUCTED PATIENT /CAREGIVER(S) TO IMMEDIATELY REPORT THE FOLLOWING SIGNS / SYMPTOMS OF CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE
EXACERBATION: CHEST PAIN, INCREASE IN SHORTNESS OF BREATH, A CHANGE IN MENTAL STATUS, WORSENING LOWER EXTREMITY EDEMA,
AND/OR A WEIGHT GAIN OF 2-4 POUNDS.
INSTRUCTED PATIENT /CAREGIVER TO KEEP WEIGHT LOG DAILY AND TO USE THE SAME SCALES WHEN OBTAINING WEIGHT. CALL RN FOR
Powered by Homecare Homebase™ Page 7 of 10

INSTRUCTED PATIENT /CAREGIVER THAT YOUR HEALTHCARE PROVIDER MAY ALSO PRESCRIBE A DIURETIC: THIS MEDICATION IS TO RID
YOUR BODY OF EXTRA FLUID (OFTEN USED FOR PEOPLE WITH HEART FAILURE). POSSIBLE SIDE EFFECTS INCLUDE WEAKNESS, MUSCLE
CRAMPS, DRY MOUTH, AND DIZZINESS.
6. PROVIDE INSTRUCTION RELATED TO POSSIBLE COMPLICATIONS OF CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: DAMAGE TO ORGANS, SUCH AS: LIVER, KIDNEY, OR BRAIN
ACUTE PULMONARY EDEMA
7. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON PATHOPHYSIOLOGY/UNDERLYING CAUSES OF GENITOURINARY DISEASE
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SIGNS / SYMPTOMS OF EXACERBATION INCLUDING INCREASED/DECREASED URINARY OUTPUT, PAIN ON URINATION,
INCREASED FREQUENCY, CLOUDY, FOUL SMELLING OR BLOODY URINE, FLANK PAIN
INTERVENTIONS TO MINIMIZE SYMPTOMOLOGY INCLUDING MEDICATIONS, HEAT/COLD, DIETARY INTAKE AND FLUID INTAKE AND PERSONAL
HYGIENE
EFFECTS OF URINARY INCONTINENCE ON SKIN INTEGRITY AND INTERVENTIONS TO PROTECT SKIN INTEGRITY INCLUDING USE OF
INCONTINENT SUPPLIES.
8. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON THE USE OF MEDICATIONS TO TREAT GENITOURINARY DISEASE
DETAILS/COMMENTS: MEDICATION REGIMEN INCLUDING CORRECT MEDICATION, DOSAGE, FREQUENCY, TIMES
AVOIDANCE OF OVER THE COUNTER MEDICATIONS WITHOUT A PHYSICIAN'S APPROVAL
9. PERFORM MULTIFACTOR FALLS RISK ASSESSMENT TO INCLUDE HISTORY OF FALLS, ACCIDENTS, MEDICATION REGIMEN, IMPACT OF
POLYPHARMACY, COGNITIVE STATUS, MOBILITY AND ENVIRONMENTAL STATUS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: POTENTIAL OF VERTIGO AND DIZZINESS WITH SUDDEN POSITION CHANGES
10. PROVIDE INSTRUCTION RELATED TO PATIENT'S RISK FOR FALLS AND SAFETY TO PREVENT FALLS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: KEEP STAIRS AND PATHWAYS CLEAR OF CLUTTER
KEEP A PHONE CLOSE BY AT ALL TIMES
DO NOT WAIT UNTIL THE LAST MOMENT TO START A TRIP TO THE BATHROOM.
STORE FOOD, DISHES AND COOKING EQUIPMENT AT EASY TO REACH, WAIST HIGH LEVELS
DO NOT TRYTO CARRY TOO MANY ITEMS AT ONE TIME.
KEEP ASSISTIVE DEVICES CLOSE AT HAND FOR USE WITH MOBILITY.
11. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON THE USE OF MEDICATIONS TO TREAT DISEASE PROCESSES
DETAILS/COMMENTS: MEDICATION REGIMEN INCLUDING CORRECT MEDICATION, DOSAGE, FREQUENCY, TIMES
INSTRUCTED ON HOW AND WHEN TO REPORT PROBLEMS THAT MAY OCCUR DUE TO HIGH-RISK MEDICATIONS

DETAILS/COMMENTS: REVIEWED HEP FOR LE THER EXAND FREQUENCY FOR HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM; DAILY
INSTRUCTED WITH 1TO 2 VC -
SEATED LE EXERCISES X 20REPS:
-ANKLE PUMPS
- SITTING MARCHES
- LAQ
- HIP ABD/ADD
CLOSE SBA WITH ROLLATOR FOR BUE SUPPORT -
STANDING LE EXERCISES X20REPS:
- HEEL/TOE RAISES
- HIP ABDUCTION
- MARCHING IN PLACEMINI SQUATS
RPE BORG 13/20 DYSPNEA 0
2. PROVIDE MUSCLE RE EDUCATION THERAPY TO INCREASE NEUROMUSCULAR FUNCTION.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: TASK DESIGN WITH VERY CLOSE SBA/ CGA -
TO IMPROVE BALANCE AND POWER WITH TRANSFERS - SIT TO STAND 5X WITH BUES WITHIN 45S FROM RECLINER ,
STANDING SEMI TANDEM 3 XS X 10S EACH ALTERNATING R FOOT FORWARD/ L FOOT FORWARD - HELD POSITION NO HANDS , OVER FIRM
SURFACE - NARROW BOS EC X 30S NO HANDS, ZONTAGO WARMUPS 1AND 2X5 REPS NO HANDS.
RPE BORG 14/20 DYSPNEA 0
3. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.

MULTIPLE DIRECTIONS - FORWARD GAIT WITH HEAD TURNS TOLANDR, RETRO STEPPING , AND 180 DEGREE TURNS - TIME UP X 6 MINUTES
WITH GAIT BEFORE PT REQUESTED TO SIT DOWN - RPE BORG 13/20 DYSPNEA0
4. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.

KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE; ROLLATOR OR RW
WEAR NON-SLIP FOOTWEAR; HAVE BUE SUPPORT WITH STANDING ACTIVITIES

THIS PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 AND ABLE TO VERBALIZE NEEDS WITHOUT DIFFICULTIES. THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR
ACCESS SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM YESTERDAY WITH DR. DWIVEDI. DISCHARGE PAPERWORK SAYS LEAVE CURRENT DRESSING
ON RT UPPER ARM IN PLACE UNTIL TOMORROW 11-19-20 AND THEN LEAVE STERI-STRIPS IN PLACE UNTIL THEY FALL OFF NATURALLY. SKILLED
NURSING INTENDS TO ASSESS FOR DRESSING NEEDS WANTS CURRENT DRESSING IS REMOVED AND THE AREA IS ASSESSED TOMORROW.
ALSO PATIENT HAS NEW PRESCRIPTION FOR PAIN MEDICATION. NEW ORDERS RECEIVED WITH BRITNEY WITH DR MADERIC. PATIENTS
SCHEDULED FOR SKILLED NURSING RECERTIFICATION SECONDARY TO NEW SURGICAL WOUND AT RIGHT UPPER ARM. PATIENT REMAINS
STABLE AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE DRESSING THAT IS IN PLACE DOES SHOW SOME DRAINAGE THAT IS BLOODY AND NATURE. THE DRESSING
IS INTACT ANDTHE EXTREMITY IS NOT SWOLLEN AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES PAIN IS CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT BUT
THE AREA REMAINS TENDER. PATIENT ALSO COMPLAINS OF A SLIGHT SORE THROAT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE
DISTRESS OBSERVED STATED.
PHYSICIAN CONTACTED AND APPROVED ORDERS IN PLAN, NAME OF CONTACT PERSON BRITTANY WITH DR. MADERIC
Patient Goal
NO MORE FALLS

NOTIFY SN IF ANY ABNORMAL WOUND FINDINGS SUCH AS INCREASED

MAINTAINING A CLEAN ATMOSPHERE AND WASHING HANDS BEFORE AND

OF INFECTION. PATIENT ADVISED ON HIGH PROTEIN DIET CHOICES. BASIC
WOUND HEALING CONCEPTS AND THEORIES APPROPRIATE FOR THIS
PATIENTS LEVEL OF UNDERSTANDING, REVIEWED AND ACKNOWLEDGED.

PATIENT WAS SEEN LAST WEEK AT PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIANS OFFICE. Active
PATIENT SUBMITTED LAB SAMPLES. THE LAB SAMPLE RESULTS WERE SENT
TO HER NEPHROLOGIST DR. RAZA. DR. RAZA INITIATED CEPHALEXIN 500 MG
CAPSULES ONE TWICE A DAY FOR 7 DAYS FOR A POSITIVE UA. PATIENT
STATES SHES HAD NO UTI SXS AND WAS SUPRISED TO GET ABX TX. THIS
MORNING PATIENT HAS BEEN HAVING INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH
AMBULATION RESULTING IN SEVERAL ALMOST FALLS AND TWO ACTUAL FALLS
OVER THE LAST WEEK. PATIENT HAS BRUISING ABOVE HER RIGHT EYE AND
MULTIPLE BRUISING ABOUT HER UPPER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT SKIN IS CLEAN
DRY AND INTACT AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE LAST FALL OCCURRED WHEN
THE PATIENT WAS TRYING TO EXIT A VEHICLE. PATIENT CAREGIVER STATES
THAT HE THINKS HER SHOES MAY HAVE BEEN A LITTLE BIT TOO BIG FOR HER
AND SHE MAY HAVE TRIPPED OVER SHOES. PATIENT ADVISED TO WEAR
STABLE PLATFORM SHOES AND TO MOVE SLOW AND WAIT FOR ASSISTANCE
FOR ALL TRANSFERS TO REDUCE FALL OCCURRENCES. PATIENT AND
CAREGIVERS STATE THAT SHE SEEMS TO JUST LOSE HER BALANCE WITHOUT
WARNING. BG AND BP HAVE REMAINED STABLE. PATIENT AND CG STATE THEY
BELIEVE ALL FALLS ARE RELATED TO WEAKNESS. PATIENT ADMITS TO NOT
FOLLOWING THROUGH WITH THERAPY EXERCISES BETWEEN VISITS. FALL
PRECAUTIONS REVIEWED. PATIENT VERBALIZES AGREEMENT. THERE HAVE
BEEN NO OTHER MEDICATION CHANGES AND PATIENT STATES WITH THE
EXCEPTION OF THE INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH BALANCE THAT SHE
REMAINS STABLE AND IN GOOD SHAPE.
SPOKE WITH CG PRIOR TO VISIT DENIED S/S OFCOVID 19 Active

THIS PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 ANDABLE TO VERBALIZE Active
NEEDS WITHOUT DIFFICULTIES. THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR ACCESS
SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM YESTERDAY WITH DR. DWIVEDI.
DISCHARGE PAPERWORK SAYS LEAVE CURRENT DRESSING ON RT UPPER
ARMIN PLACE UNTIL TOMORROW 11-19-20 AND THEN LEAVE STERI-STRIPS IN
PLACE UNTIL THEY FALL OFF NATURALLY. SKILLED NURSING INTENDS TO
ASSESS FOR DRESSING NEEDS WANTS CURRENT DRESSING IS REMOVED
ANDTHEAREA IS ASSESSED TOMORROW. ALSO PATIENT HAS NEW
PRESCRIPTION FOR PAIN MEDICATION. NEW ORDERS RECEIVED WITH
BRITNEY WITH DR MADERIC. PATIENTS SCHEDULED FOR SKILLED NURSING
RECERTIFICATION SECONDARY TO NEW SURGICAL WOUND AT RIGHT UPPER
ARM. PATIENT REMAINS STABLE AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE DRESSING THAT
IS IN PLACE DOES SHOW SOME DRAINAGE THAT IS BLOODY AND NATURE. THE
DRESSING IS INTACT AND THE EXTREMITY IS NOT SWOLLEN AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES PAIN IS CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT
BUT THE AREA REMAINS TENDER. PATIENT ALSO COMPLAINS OF A SLIGHT
SORE THROAT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS
OBSERVED STATED.

SKILLED NURSE TO EVALUATE AND DEVELOP PLAN OF CARE TO BE COUNTERSIGNED BY PHYSICIAN. SKILLED NURSE TO ASSESS/EVALUATE COMORBID CONDITIONS INCLUDING WOUND CARE AND ASSESSMENT ANDOTHER CONDITIONS THAT PRESENT THEMSELVES DURING THE COURSE
OF THIS EPISODE TO IDENTIFY CHANGES AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. NURSING ASSESSMENT TO BE PERFORMED BY RN: LPN

SKILLED NURSE TO OBSERVE/ASSESS INTEGUMENTARY SYSTEM, IDENTIFY CHANGES, AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. SKILLED
NURSE TO TEACH RELATED TO ALTERED SKIN INTEGRITY INCLUDING DISEASE PROCESS, NUTRITION, MEDICATIONS, ACTIVITIES, AND

PERFORMED BY RN. LPN TO OBSERVE AND REPORT CHANGES TO THE RN.
SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM/TEACH WOUND CARE TO RT UPPEREXTSURGICALAREA. APPLY TELFA LIKE PAD, COVER WITH ROLLED GAUZE ,
SECURE WITH TAPE USING CLEAN/ASEPTIC TECHNIQUE. CHANGE DRESSING EVERY 48 HOURS AND PRN SOILING/DISLODGEMENT. MAY USE
EQUIVALENT PRODUCTS. ONCE TAUGHT, PATIENT/CAREGIVER TO PERFORM ON DAYS SKILLED NURSE IS NOT IN HOME.
SKILLED NURSE TO REVIEW MEDICATION PROFILE/RECONCILE MEDICATIONS, INSTRUCT/REINFORCE MEDICATION MANAGEMENT

[DOB-3335] NARRATIVE ONEIDA ROSE, LPN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
SN VISIT FOR CP AND WOUND ASSESS. ALL VSS FOR PT. BP TAKEN IN LLE AS PER ORDER. PT HAS
22CM LONG INCISION SITE TO RUE FROM RECENT FISTULA SURGERY FOR POTENTIAL DIALYSIS.
STERI STRIPS IN PLACE SOME WITH DRIED BLOOD BUT INTACT WITH NO S/S INFECTION. WOUND
CARE INSTRUCTIONS SAYS TO LEAVE OPEN TO AIR AND KEEP CLEAN/DRY. PT REFUSED SN TO
PUT DRESSING ON STERI STRIPS. NO REDDNESS OR WARMTH OR INCREASED PAIN OR S/S
INFECTION.

[DOB-3331] NARRATIVE AMY GUTIERREZ, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
ARRIVED AT PATIENT'S HOME TO FIND PATIENT SITTING IN LIVING ROOM WATCHING T.V. RIGHT
ARM INCISION OPEN TO AIR. FEW SCABS STILL IN PLACE TO PROXIMAL END OF INCISION. ALL
STERI-STRIPS ARE OFF NOW. NOTED TINY DOT OF VERY LIGHT SEROSANGUINOUS DRAINAGE AT
PROXIMAL END OF INCISION IN CROOK OF ELBOW. NO SIGNS OF INFECTION NOTED. INSTRUCTED
ON GENTLY DABBING INCISION WITH GOLD DIAL SOAP, RINSING, AND PATTING DRY DAILY AND TO
APPLY BAND-AID OVER THAT SPOT ONLY. PT/CG VERBALIZED UNDERSTANDING. PATIENT SEES
THE SURGEON WHO PLACED THE AV FISTULA THIS WEEK FOR FOLLOW-UP. HAS POSITIVE
THRILL/BRUIT. VSS AND WITHIN PARAMETERS. LUNGS CTA. NO EDEMA NOTED. PATIENT TO BE
DISCHARGED FROM SN ON 12-7; PT/CG IN AGREEMENT.

[DOB-3298] NARRATIVE BRIAN WOOSLEY, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
SN FOR WOUND ASSESSMENT AND CARE, CP ASSESS, MEDICATION REGIMEN REVIEW,
MEDICATION ADHERENCE PROMOTION, MEDICATION TEACHING AND IN-DEPTH DISEASE
PROCESS REVIEW AND EDUCATION. SURGICAL AREA CONSIDERED HEALED AS OF THIS
ASSESSMENT. THE SURGERY WAS TO PLACE A ARTERIAL VENOUS FISTULA FOR HEMODIALYSIS.
B&T EASILY PALPABLE. THERE ARE NO SIGNS OR SYMPTOMS OF INFECTION. PATIENT TO FU
WITH VASCULAR SURGEON IN 5-6 WEEKS. PATIENT WITH ALL GOALS MET AS OF THIS
ASSESSMENT. SKILLED NURSING COMPLETING DISCIPLINE DISCHARGE AS OF THIS VISIT. VITAL
SIGNS REMAIN STABLE. PATIENT IS INDEPENDENT WITH MEDICATIONS AND DIABETIC
MANAGEMENT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVE THE
STATED AT THIS ASSESSMENT.

[DOB-3144] NARRATIVE ONEIDA ROSE, LPN
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
SN VISIT FOR CP AND WOUNDASSESS. ALL VSS FOR PT. BP TAKEN IN LLE AS PER ORDER. PT HAS 22CM LONG INCISION SITE TO RUE FROM
RECENT FISTULA SURGERY FOR POTENTIAL DIALYSIS. STERI STRIPS IN PLACE SOME WITH DRIED BLOOD BUT INTACT WITH NO S/S INFECTION.
WOUND CARE INSTRUCTIONS SAYS TO LEAVE OPEN TO AIR AND KEEP CLEAN/DRY. PT REFUSED SN TO PUT DRESSING ON STERI STRIPS. NO
REDDNESS OR WARMTH OR INCREASED PAIN OR S/S INFECTION.
Note Date Note Type Entered By
[DOB-3143] NARRATIVE AMY GUTIERREZ, RN
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
ARRIVED AT PATIENT'S HOME TO FIND PATIENT SITTING IN LIVING ROOM WATCHING T.V. RIGHT ARM INCISION OPEN TO AIR. FEW SCABS STILL IN
PLACE TO PROXIMAL END OF INCISION. ALL STERI-STRIPS ARE OFF NOW. NOTED TINY DOT OF VERY LIGHT SEROSANGUINOUS DRAINAGE AT
PROXIMAL END OF INCISION IN CROOK OF ELBOW. NO SIGNS OF INFECTION NOTED. INSTRUCTED ON GENTLY DABBING INCISION WITH GOLD
DIAL SOAP, RINSING, AND PATTING DRY DAILY AND TO APPLY BAND-AID OVER THAT SPOT ONLY. PT/CG VERBALIZED UNDERSTANDING. PATIENT
SEES THE SURGEON WHO PLACED THE AV FISTULA THIS WEEK FOR FOLLOW-UP. HAS POSITIVE THRILL/BRUIT. VSS AND WITHIN PARAMETERS.
LUNGS CTA. NO EDEMA NOTED. PATIENT TO BE DISCHARGED FROM SN ON 12-7; PT/CG IN AGREEMENT.

Note
SN FOR WOUND ASSESSMENT AND CARE, CP ASSESS, MEDICATION REGIMEN REVIEW, MEDICATION ADHERENCE PROMOTION, MEDICATION
TEACHING AND IN-DEPTH DISEASE PROCESS REVIEW AND EDUCATION. SURGICAL AREA CONSIDERED HEALED AS OF THIS ASSESSMENT. THE
SURGERY WAS TO PLACE A ARTERIAL VENOUS FISTULA FOR HEMODIALYSIS. B&T EASILY PALPABLE. THERE ARE NO SIGNS OR SYMPTOMS OF
INFECTION. PATIENT TO FU WITH VASCULAR SURGEON IN 5-6 WEEKS. PATIENT WITH ALL GOALS MET AS OF THIS ASSESSMENT. SKILLED
NURSING COMPLETING DISCIPLINE DISCHARGE AS OF THIS VISIT. VITAL SIGNS REMAIN STABLE. PATIENT IS INDEPENDENT WITH MEDICATIONS
AND DIABETIC MANAGEMENT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVE THE STATED AT THIS ASSESSMENT.

CONTROL METHODS.
2. INSTRUCT IN THERAPEUTIC EXERCISES AND HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: TASK DESIGN WITH CLOSE CGA AND 2 TO 3 VC FOR LE THER EXIN STANDING X5 MINUTES- MIP X15 REPS , HIP
ABDUCTION X 20 , UP DOWN ON TOES X 10
STOOD TOTAL 9M45S
-, ZONTAGO WARMUPS 1AND 2 X5 REPS
INSTRUCTED WITH 1TO 2 VC FOR LE THER EX IN SUPINE- SLR X 20, BENT KNEE TO CHEST X20, SEATED LAQ X 20
RPE BORG 15/20
3. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: SUPERVISION WITH 1TO 2 VC FOR UPRIGHT POSTURE ,TAKE BIG STEPS WITH INCREASED STEP LENGTH ANDWIDTH
USING GT X 4M45S WITH A WALKER IN HOME OVER EVEN SURFACES WITH 180 DEGREE TURNS - PT REQUESTED TO SIT DOWN - RE DYSPNEA
2 /10- BORG 13/20.
4. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT INSTRUCTED TO:
KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE;
WEAR NON-SLIP FOOTWEAR;
5. INSTRUCT PATIENT IN BALANCE TRAINING ACTIVITIES TO REDUCE FALL RISK
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT/ CAREGIVER IN:
STATIC STANDING;
DYNAMIC STANDING;
HARD SURFACE;
FEET TOGETHER;
FEET APART;
EYES OPEN;
EYES CLOSED;
POSTURAL AWARENESS;
WITH 1AND 2 UPPER EXTREMITY SUPPORT;
ZONTAGO WARMUPS 1AND 2X5 REPS EACH
6. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE
PROVIDED):

DETAILS/COMMENTS: SUPERVISION WITH 2 TO 3 VC TO LOOK UP AHEAD, KEEP AD CLOSE TO BODY , AND TAKE BIG STEPS USING GT WITH
ROLLATOR OVER EVEN SURFACES IN HOME WITH MULTIPLE 180 DEGREE TURNS -X5 MINUTES . RPE DYSPNEAO
3. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT AND CG INSTRUCTED TO:
KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE;
WEAR NON-SLIP FOOTWEAR; LOCK ROLLATOR BRAKES WITH TRANSFERS , TO STEP ALL THE WAY BACK TO CHAIR SO SHE CAN FEEL ON
BACK OF LEGS PRIOR TO DESCENDING FOR IMPROVED ACCURACY
4. INSTRUCT PATIENT IN BALANCE TRAINING ACTIVITIES TO REDUCE FALL RISK
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED PATIENT/ CAREGIVER IN:
STATIC STANDING;
DYNAMIC STANDING;
HARD SURFACE;
FEET APART;
EYES OPEN;
POSTURAL AWARENESS;
WITH UPPER EXTREMITY SUPPORT; OF ROLLATOR
ZONTAGO WARMUPS 1AND 2X5 REPS
5. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE
PROVIDED):
DETAILS/COMMENTS: TRANSFER TRAINING WITH CGA - 2TO3 VC TO STEP ALL THE WAY BACK SO SHE CAN FEEL CHAIR ON BACK OF LEGS,
REACH BACK ,AND DESCEND SLOWLYHAND PLACEMENT FOR ASCENDING FOR IMPROVED CONTROL - FOR CG NOT TO PULL HER UP OUT OF CHAIR -ALLOW THE PT TO USE UES

3. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.

KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE;

TRANSFERS

STATIC STANDING;
DYNAMIC STANDING;
HARD SURFACE;
FEET APART;
EYES OPEN;
EYES CLOSED;
POSTURAL AWARENESS; 2 HAND SUPPORT USING ZONTAGO 1AND 2 X5 REPS
5. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE
PROVIDED):

“1. INSTRUCT IN THERAPEUTIC EXERCISES AND HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED WITH 1TO 2 VC WITH CLOSE SBA/CGA TO IMPROVE LE STRENTH USING STANDING LE EXERCISES X 20
REPS: ROLLATOR FOR BUE SUPPORT -
- HEEL/TOE RAISES
- HIP ABDUCTION
- HIP FLEXION
- HIP EXTENSION
- HAM CURLS
- MARCHING IN PLACE
RPE BORG 14/20
2. GAIT TRAINING PROVIDED WITHAWITHOUT ASSISTIVE DEVICES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: THE PT HAD A DIFFERENT ROLLATOR TO TRIAL - ADJUSTED ASSISTIVE DEVICE TO APPROPRIATE HEIGHT TO IMPROVE
SAFETY, POSTURE, AND ENDURANCE.
PATIENT REPORTS IMPROVED COMFORT AFTER DEVICE ADJUSTMENT. THE NEW ROLLATOR IS WIDER ANDTHE PT IS ABLE TO SIT ON THE
SEAT FOR REST BREAKS.
SUPERVISION / CGA FOR GT IN HOME WITH NEW ROLLATOR = VC FOR OBSTACLE NEGOTIATION AND TO ENSURE AD FIT THROUGH
DOORWAYS - USING GT IN HOME X 4 MINUTES FOR HOUSE HOLD DISTANCES WITHIN LIVING ROOM AND KITCHEN.
3. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT INSTRUCTED TO:
KEEP PATHWAYS CLEAR;
ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE;
WEAR NON-SLIP FOOTWEAR; NON SKID STRIP IN FRONT OF RECLINER TO REDUCE FEET FROM SLIDING WITH TRANSFERS
4. PROVIDE SKILLED THERAPY RELATED TO TRANSFERS, BED MOBILITY, AMBULATION AND/OR BALANCE. (INDICATE LEVEL OF ASSISTANCE
PROVIDED):
DETAILS/COMMENTS: TASK DESIGN WITH SUPERVISION AND 2 TO 3 VC TO IMPROVE SAFETY AND ACCURACY WITH STAND <> SIT TRANSFERS
FROM ROLLATOR TO RECLINER - VC TO STEP ALL THE WAY BACK UNTIL SHE CAN FEEL SEAT ON BACK OF BOTH LEGS, REACH BACK FOR
ARMREST WITH BUES , SLOWLY SIT DOWN AND KEEP BOTH FEET PULLED BACK AND ON FLOOR -
PT IS FLOPPING INTO RECLINER AND ALLOWING FEET TO COME OFF FLOOR - FALL RISK . SUGGESTED NON SKID STRIPS , ANDTHEY HAVE A
DIFFERENT RECLINER THAT THE PT MAY BE ABLE TO TRANSFER EASIER FROM - PTA SUGGESED THEY TRIAL IT
{

BRADEN RISK ASSESSMENT SCALE
SENSORY PERCEPTION -ABILITY TO RESPOND MEANINGFULLY TO PRESSURE-RELATED DISCOMFORT
NO IMPAIRMENT - RESPONDS TO VERBAL COMMANDS. HAS NO SENSORY DEFICIT WHICH WOULD LIMIT ABILITY TO FEEL OR VOICE PAIN
OR DISCOMFORT.
MOISTURE - DEGREE TO WHICH SKIN IS EXPOSED TO MOISTURE
RARELY MOIST - SKIN IS USUALLY DRY. LINEN ONLY REQUIRES CHANGING AT ROUTINE INTERVALS.
ACTIVITY - DEGREE OF PHYSICAL ACTIVITY
WALKS FREQUENTLY -WALKS OUTSIDE BEDROOM TWICE A DAY AND INSIDE ROOM AT LEAST ONCE EVERY TWO HOURS DURING WAKING
HOURS.
MOBILITY -ABILITY TO CHANGE AND CONTROL BODY POSITION
NO LIMITATIONS - MAKES MAJOR ANDFREQUENT CHANGES IN POSITION WITHOUT ASSISTANCE.
NUTRITION - USUAL FOOD INTAKE PATTERN
ADEQUATE - EATS OVER HALF OF MOST MEALS. EATS A TOTAL OF 4 SERVINGS OF PROTEIN (MEAT, DAIRY PRODUCTS) PER DAY.
OCCASIONALLY WILL REFUSE A MEAL, BUT WILL USUALLY TAKE A SUPPLEMENT WHEN OFFERED, OR IS ON A TUBE FEEDING OR TPN
REGIMEN, WHICH PROBABLY MEETS MOST OF NUTRITIONAL NEEDS.

8. REACHING FORWARD WITH OUTSTRETCHED ARM WHILE STANDING
INSTRUCTIONS: LIFT ARM TO 90 DEGREES. STRETCH OUT YOUR FINGERS AND REACH FORWARD AS FAR AS YOU CAN. (EXAMINER PLACES
A RULER AT END OF FINGERTIPS WHEN ARM IS AT 90 DEGREES. FINGERS SHOULD NOT TOUCH THE RULER WHILE REACHING FORWARD.
THE RECORDED MEASURE IS THE DISTANCE FORWARD THATTHE FINGERS REACHES WHILE THE SUBJECT IS IN THE MOST FORWARD
LEAN POSITION. WHEN POSSIBLE,ASK SUBJECT TO USE BOTH ARMS WHEN REACHING TO AVOID ROTATION OF THE TRUNK.)

13. STANDING UNSUPPORTED ONE FOOT IN FRONT
INSTRUCTIONS: (DEMONSTRATE TO SUBJECT) PLACE ONE FOOT DIRECTLY IN FRONT OF THE OTHER. IF YOU
FEEL THAT YOU CANNOT PLACE YOUR FOOT DIRECTLY IN FRONT, TRYTO STEP FAR ENOUGH AHEAD THAT THE HEEL OF YOUR FORWARD
FOOT IS AHEAD OF THE TOES OF THE OTHER FOOT.
(TO SCORE 3 POINTS,THE LENGTH OF THE STEP SHOULD EXCEED THE LENGTH OF THE OTHER FOOT AND THEWIDTH OF THE STANCE
SHOULD APPROXIMATE THE SUBJECT'S NORMAL STRIDE WIDTH.)

PETS, HARD TO REACH ITEMS, FLOOR SURFACES THAT ARE UNEVEN OR CLUTTERED, OR OUTDOOR ENTRY AND EXITS.
YES
POLY PHARMACY (4 OR MORE PRESCRIPTIONS - ANY TYPE) -- ALL PRESCRIPTIONS INCLUDING PRESCRIPTIONS FOR OTC MEDS. DRUGS
HIGHLYASSOCIATED WITH FALL RISK INCLUDE BUT NOT LIMITED TO, SEDATIVES. ANTI-DEPRESSANTS, TRANQUILIZERS, NARCOTICS
ANTIHYPERTENSIVES, CARDIAC MEDS, CORTICOSTEROIDS, ANTI-ANXIETY DRUGS, ANTICHOLINERGIC DRUGS, AND HYPOGLYCEMIC DRUGS.
YES
PAIN AFFECTING LEVEL OF FUNCTION -- PAIN OFTEN AFFECTS AN INDIVIDUAL'S DESIRE OR ABILITY TO MOVE, OR PAIN CAN BE_A FACTOR IN
DEPRESSION OR COMPLIANCE WITH SAFETY RECOMMENDATIONS.
NO
COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT -- COULD INCLUDE PATIENTS WITH DEMENTIA, ALZHEIMER'S OR STROKE PATIENTS, OR PATIENTS WHO ARE
CONFUSED, USE POOR JUDGMENT, HAVE DECREASED COMPREHENSION, IMPULSIVITY, MEMORY DEFICITS. CONSIDER PATIENTS ABILITY TO
ADHERE TO THE PLAN OF CARE.
NO

Orders of Discipline and Treatments:
SKILLED NURSE TO EVALUATE AND DEVELOP PLAN OF CARE TO BE COUNTERSIGNED BY PHYSICIAN. SKILLED NURSE TO
ASSESS/EVALUATE CO-MORBID CONDITIONS INCLUDING WOUND CARE AND ASSESSMENT AND OTHER CONDITIONS THAT PRESENT
THEMSELVES DURING THE COURSE OF THIS EPISODE TO IDENTIFY CHANGES AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. NURSING
ASSESSMENT TO BE PERFORMED BY RN: LPN TO OBSERVE AND REPORT CHANGES TO THE RN.
SKILLED NURSE TO OBSERVE/ASSESS INTEGUMENTARY SYSTEM, IDENTIFY CHANGES, AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS.
SKILLED NURSE TO TEACH RELATED TO ALTERED SKIN INTEGRITY INCLUDING DISEASE PROCESS, NUTRITION, MEDICATIONS, ACTIVITIES,
AND PERSONAL/ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS. SIGNIFICANT CHANGES IN STATUS WILL BE REPORTED TO PHYSICIAN. NURSING
ASSESSMENT TO BE PERFORMED BY RN. LPN TO OBSERVE AND REPORT CHANGES TO THE RN.
SKILLED NURSE TO REVIEW MEDICATION PROFILE/RECONCILE MEDICATIONS, INSTRUCT/REINFORCE MEDICATION MANAGEMENT
SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM/TEACH WOUND CARE TO RT UPPER EXTSURGICAL AREA. APPLY TELFA LIKE PAD, COVER WITH ROLLED
GAUZE , SECURE WITH TAPE USING CLEAN/ASEPTIC TECHNIQUE. CHANGE DRESSING EVERY 48 HOURS AND PRN
SOILING/DISLODGEMENT. MAY USE EQUIVALENT PRODUCTS. ONCE TAUGHT, PATIENT/CAREGIVER TO PERFORM ON DAYS SKILLED NURSE
IS NOT IN HOME.
| recertify that this patient is confined to his/her home and needs intermittent skilled nursing care, physical therapy and/or speech therapy or continues to need

Goals/Rehabilitation Potential/Discharge Plans:
A PLAN OF CARE WILL BE ESTABLISHED THAT MEETS THE PATIENT'S NURSING NEEDS AND COUNTERSIGNED BY PHYSICIAN.
CHANGES IN SKIN INTEGRITY WILL BE IDENTIFIED PROMPTLY AND INTERVENTIONS INITIATED TO MINIMIZE RISKS. PATIENT/CAREGIVER WILL
VERBALIZE/DEMONSTRATE AN ABILITY TO MANAGE SKIN HEALTH AS EVIDENCED BY DECREASED SYMPTOMS AND NO UNPLANNED
HOSPITALIZATIONS BY 1-18-21
PATIENT ADHERES TO MEDICATION REGIMEN. PATIENT/CAREGIVER VERBALIZES/DEMONSTRATES MEDICATION MANAGEMENT INCLUDING
INTENDED BENEFITS AND POTENTIAL INTERACTIONS/SIDE EFFECTS BY 1-18-21
PATIENT/ CAREGIVER VERBALIZES/DEMONSTRATES WOUND MANAGEMENT WITHOUT COMPLICATIONS AS EVIDENCED BY IMPROVED
WOUND STATUS AND NO UNPLANNED HOSPITALIZATIONS BY 1-18-21
A PHYSICAL THERAPY PLAN OF CARE WILL BE ORDERED BY PHYSICIAN AND PROVIDED BY PHYSICAL THERAPY. ALL GOALS TO BE MET BY
END OF CURRENTLY APPROVED PLAN OF CARE.
PATIENT WILL DEMONSTRATE IMPROVED FUNCTION IN RESPONSE TO SPECIFIC EXERCISE(S) AS EVIDENCED BY INCREASED
INDEPENDENCE IN ACTIVITIES OF DAILY LIVING BY 1/18.
PATIENT WILL DEMONSTRATE/VERBALIZE KNOWLEDGE OF INTERVENTIONS TO REDUCE FALL RISK AND IMPROVE FUNCTIONAL MOBILITY AS
EVIDENCED BY NO FALLS BY 1/18.
PATIENT WILL DEMONSTRATE SAFE GAIT TECHNIQUE WITH ROLLATOR AS NEEDED TO IMPROVE FUNCTIONAL MOBILITY AND MINIMIZE RISK
OF INJURY BY 1/18.
PATIENT / CAREGIVER WILL VERBALIZE EFFECTIVE PAIN CONTROL AND UNDERSTAND BOTH PHARMACOLOGIC AND NON-PHARMACOLOGIC
PAIN CONTROL METHODS BY 1/18.

GOOD/MARKED IMPROVEMENT IN FUNCTIONAL STATUS IS EXPECTED

PATIENT WAS SEEN LAST WEEK AT PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIANS OFFICE. Active
PATIENT SUBMITTED LAB SAMPLES. THE LAB SAMPLE RESULTS WERE SENT
TO HER NEPHROLOGIST DR. RAZA. DR. RAZA INITIATED CEPHALEXIN 500 MG
CAPSULES ONE TWICE A DAY FOR 7 DAYS FOR A POSITIVE UA. PATIENT
STATES SHES HAD NO UTI SXS AND WAS SUPRISED TO GET ABX TX. THIS
MORNING PATIENT HAS BEEN HAVING INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH
AMBULATION RESULTING IN SEVERAL ALMOST FALLS AND TWO ACTUAL FALLS
OVER THE LAST WEEK. PATIENT HAS BRUISING ABOVE HER RIGHT EYE AND
MULTIPLE BRUISING ABOUT HER UPPER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT SKIN IS CLEAN
DRY AND INTACT AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE LAST FALL OCCURRED WHEN
THE PATIENT WAS TRYING TO EXIT A VEHICLE. PATIENT CAREGIVER STATES
THAT HE THINKS HER SHOES MAY HAVE BEEN A LITTLE BIT TOO BIG FOR HER
AND SHE MAY HAVE TRIPPED OVER SHOES. PATIENT ADVISED TO WEAR
STABLE PLATFORM SHOES AND TO MOVE SLOW AND WAIT FOR ASSISTANCE
FOR ALL TRANSFERS TO REDUCE FALL OCCURRENCES. PATIENT AND
CAREGIVERS STATE THAT SHE SEEMS TO JUST LOSE HER BALANCE WITHOUT
WARNING. BG AND BP HAVE REMAINED STABLE. PATIENT AND CG STATE THEY
BELIEVE ALL FALLS ARE RELATED TO WEAKNESS. PATIENT ADMITS TO NOT
FOLLOWING THROUGH WITH THERAPY EXERCISES BETWEEN VISITS. FALL
PRECAUTIONS REVIEWED. PATIENT VERBALIZES AGREEMENT. THERE HAVE
BEEN NO OTHER MEDICATION CHANGES AND PATIENT STATES WITH THE
EXCEPTION OF THE INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH BALANCE THAT SHE
REMAINS STABLE AND IN GOOD SHAPE.
