THIS PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 ANDABLE TO VERBALIZE Active
NEEDS WITHOUT DIFFICULTIES. THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR ACCESS
SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM YESTERDAY WITH DR. DWIVEDI.
DISCHARGE PAPERWORK SAYS LEAVE CURRENT DRESSING ON RT UPPER
ARMIN PLACE UNTIL TOMORROW 11-19-20 AND THEN LEAVE STERI-STRIPS IN
PLACE UNTIL THEY FALL OFF NATURALLY. SKILLED NURSING INTENDS TO
ASSESS FOR DRESSING NEEDS WANTS CURRENT DRESSING IS REMOVED
ANDTHEAREA IS ASSESSED TOMORROW. ALSO PATIENT HAS NEW
PRESCRIPTION FOR PAIN MEDICATION. NEW ORDERS RECEIVED WITH
BRITNEY WITH DR MADERIC. PATIENTS SCHEDULED FOR SKILLED NURSING
RECERTIFICATION SECONDARY TO NEW SURGICAL WOUND AT RIGHT UPPER
ARM. PATIENT REMAINS STABLE AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE DRESSING THAT
IS IN PLACE DOES SHOW SOME DRAINAGE THAT IS BLOODY AND NATURE. THE
DRESSING IS INTACT AND THE EXTREMITY IS NOT SWOLLEN AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES PAIN IS CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT
BUT THE AREA REMAINS TENDER. PATIENT ALSO COMPLAINS OF A SLIGHT
SORE THROAT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS
OBSERVED STATED.

2/2/2021 9:44:42 PM MARLENA WELTY, RN SKILLED NURSING ADD-ON ASSESSMENT PERFORMED AND PATIENT HAS Active
BILATERAL BUTTOCKS STAGE 2 PRESSURE ULCERS THAT WILL REQUIRE
TEACHING AND INTERVENTION AND MANAGEMENT. PATIENT HAS ALSO BEEN
HAVING FLUCTUATIONS IN HER BLOOD SUGAR AND COULD BENEFIT FROM
DIABETIC TEACHING AND EDUCATION. PATIENT STARTED ON KIDNEY DIALYSIS
TODAY SECONDARY TO END STAGE RENAL DISEASE AND IS ALSO HAVING
PROBLEMS WITH ANEMIA AT THIS TIME IN WHICH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER
COULD BOTH BENEFIT FROM EDUCATION REGARDING AS WELL AS BASIC
SYSTEMS ASSESSMENT. PER CAREGIVER PATIENT HAS A HARD TIME WHEN
SHE HAS BOWEL MOVEMENTS BECAUSE THEY ARE THICKAND HARD TO
CLEAN OFF HER PERI AREA WHICH OFTEN CAUSES HER SKIN BREAKDOWN ON
HER BOTTOM TO WORSEN. UPON ASSESSMENT PATIENT IS TAKING PO
FERROUS SULFATE FOR ANEMIA IN WHICH | EDUCATED THAT COULD CAUSE
THICKTARRY STOOLS THAT ARE HARD TO CLEAN. | ENCOURAGED PATIENT
ANDCAREGIVER TO USE MOIST WIPES WITH PERI WASH CLEANSER TO
CLEAN THE AREA AND TO MAKE SURE SHE IS HYDRATING WITH WATER AS
WELL AS TAKING AN OVER THE COUNTER STOOL SOFTENER AS SUGGESTED
BY MD TO PREVENT INCREASED STOOL THICKNESS. | ALSO EDUCATED ON
THE IMPORTANCE OF PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES AND PATIENT IS USING
HER PRESSURE CUSHION WHILE SITTING IN CHAIR BUT PATIENT ALSO
SLEEPS IN THE CHAIR BECAUSE OF HER NEED TO HAVE HER HEAD ELEVATED
WHEN SLEEPING. | EDUCATED ON SKIN BREAKDOWN PRECAUTIONS AND
PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES INCLUDING NEED TO LIFT HER BUTT OFF AND
NOT SCOOT WHEN CHANGING POSITIONS TO PREVENT SHEARING AND
FRICTION AS WELL AS TRYING TO STAND EVERY HOUR WHILE AWAKE TO
RELIEVE SOME OF THE PRESSURE FROM HER BUTTOCKS. SN EDUCATED ON
CAUSE OF PRESSURE SORES AND TREATMENT TO HEAL THEM WHICH
INCLUDES DECREASING THE PRESSURE AND KEEPING THE SKIN AS CLEAN
AND DRY AS POSSIBLE. |SHOWED CAREGIVER HOW TO APPLY OINTMENT TO
OPEN WOUNDS AND COVER WITH FOAM BORDER GAUZE. CAREGIVER
AGREES HE WILL PICK UP SOME CALMOSEPTINE OINTMENT FROM THE
PHARMACY AND BEGAN USING THIS AT THIS TIME. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND
CAREGIVER ON NEED TO PHONE NURSE IF WOUNDS BEGAN TO DETERIORATE
OR THERE WERE ANY NEW OPEN AREAS NOTED. PATIENT DOES REQUIRE
FREQUENT REPOSITIONING OF THE BODY IN WAYS NOT FEASIBLE BY AN
ORDINARY BED INCLUDING ELEVATION OF HER HEAD AND FEET SECONDARY
TO ORTHOPNEA CAUSED BY CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE
REQUIRINGHOSPITAL BED. PATIENT HAS NEW ONSET SKIN BREAKDOWN TO
HER BUTTOCKS IN WHICH WILL REQUIRE NEED OF GEL OVERLAY OR DRY
FOAM MATTRESS AS WELL TO PREVENT DETERIORATION OF CURRENT
WOUNDS. PATIENT HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE, UNSTEADY GAIT, AND
WEAKNESS ALL REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE AND WALKER TO
SAFELY LEAVE HOME WITH FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER MAKING
HER HOMEBOUND.

3/12/2021 10:13:36 PM MARLENA WELTY, RN NEW ORDERS WERE RECEIVED FOR UNNABOOT PLACEMENT/ WOUND CARE Active
SECONDARY TO NEW ONSET OPEN WOUND TO LEFT LOWER LEG WITH
EDEMA. PATIENT WAS SEEN BY PCP IN HER OFFICE YESTERDAY WITH
CEPHALEXIN ANTIBIOTIC ADDED TO PREVENT CELLULITIS. UPON ARRIVAL
WOUND PICTURES AND MEASUREMENTS WERE TAKEN ANDWOUND CARE
PERFORMED PER MD ORDER AND PATIENT TOLERATED WELL. ORDER WAS
OBTAINED TO APPLY XERORFORM OR PETROLEUM GAUZE OVER WOUND
BEDS PRIOR TO APPLYING UNNABOOT ANDTOES WERE WRAPPED AS WELL
DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE WOUNDS ON THE TOES. PATIENT WAS
EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF CIRCULATORY COMPROMISE TO REPORT AND HAS
GOOD PEDAL PULSES NOTED AT THIS TIME. PATIENT DID HAVE A DOPPLER
OBTAINED YESTERDAY PRIOR TO RETURNING HOME IN WHICH THEY SAID
WAS NEGATIVE. PATIENTS BLOOD PRESSURE WAS CHECK THE FIRST TIME
AND READ 88/31 AND THEN WAS RECHECKED AND WAS STILL CONSIDERABLY
LOW SO DR. MADERIC OFFICE WAS CALLED AND PATIENT WAS ADVISED TO
HOLD HER EVENING DOSE OF PO FUROSEMIDE AND TO TAKE A MIDODRINE
TABLET TO INCREASE HER BLOOD PRESSURE READING. PATIENT HADA
REPORTED FALL YESTERDAY IN WHICH | MADE AN INCIDENT REPORT AND
AFTER TALKING WITH BOTH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER THEY WOULD PREFER
PHYSICAL THERAPY TO COME FOR PATIENTS LEG STRENGTHENING INSTEAD
OF OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY AT THIS TIME SO NEW ORDER OBTAINED FOR
NEXT WEEK PT EVALUATION WHICH IS IN THE 5-DAY RECERTIFICATION
WINDOW AND | WILL NOTIFY OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY OF PATIENTS
REQUEST FOR PT ONLY. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER WERE BOTH EDUCATED
ON SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION TO IMMEDIATELY REPORT AND NEED TO
MONITOR HER SKIN THOROUGHLY AS SHE HAS BRUISES COVERING BOTH
ARMS BUT LARGE HEMATOMAS WITH DRIED SKIN TEARS TO HER RIGHT ARM
AS WELL. PATIENT IS ASHEN IN APPEARANCE AND HAS HAD A STEADY
DECLINE SINCE STARTING DIALYSIS TREATMENTS IN WHICH | DISCUSSED
WITH BOTH PATIENT, CAREGIVER, AND MISSY AT DR. MADERICS OFFICE.
PATIENT HAS CONSIDERED NOT DOING DIALYSIS ANYMORE BUT
UNDERSTANDS THE CONSEQUENCES OF THIS. | DID ENCOURAGE HER TO
DISCUSS HER FEELINGS WITH NEPHROLOGIST WHEN SHE IS SEEN BY HIM
NEXT WEEK.PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED WITH DECREASED
ENDURANCE AND FATIGUES AND GETS SOA WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE
EXERTION REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND CG
ASSISTANCE WITH WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER
HOMEBOUND.

REQUIRE CONTINUED SKILLED NURSING SERVICES FOR NEW ONSET
WOUNDS TO RIGHT LEG AND PREVIOUSLY NOTED LEFT LEG ANDTOE
WOUNDS ALL OF WHICH REQUIRE WOUND CARE TEACHING AND
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT HAS BEEN DECLINING RAPIDLY OVER THE PAST FEW
WEEKS IN WHICH SN MADE MD AWARE. SHE HAS BEEN UNABLE TO COMPLETE
A FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT FOR THE PAST SEVERAL VISITS SECONDARY TO
HER HYPOTENSION AND HAS HAD MULTIPLE FALLS IN A SHORT PERIOD OF
TIME FROM HER LEGS BEING SO WEAK. SHE IS HOLDING FLUID IN BOTH HER
LOWER EXTREMITIES AND SOME IN HER ABDOMEN AND IS NO LONGER
URINATING INDEPENDENTLY AT ALL. PATIENT HAS ECCHYMOSIS TO HER
ENTIRE RIGHT ARM, SOME LEFT ARM, AND MINIMALLY TO HER LEGS. AN
UNNABOOT WOULD BE APPROPRIATE FOR HER RIGHT LEG AS WELL BUT THIS
LEG IS THE ONE USED TO OBTAIN HER BP READINGS SO DRY DRESSINGS
WEREAPPLIED PER MD ORDERS. PATIENT DID WELL WITH THE LLE
UNNABOOT THIS LAST TIME BUT DOESN'T LIKE IT AND REQUESTED HER TOES
LEFT OUT. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND CAREGIVER ON NEED TO MONITOR THE
SKIN ON HER TOES CLOSELY TO PREVENT TRAUMA. MEDS WERE REVIEWED
WITH CG WHOASSISTS HER WITH THEM AND HE HAD GOTTEN CONFUSED
ANDWAS ONLY GIVING RANEXA ONCE INSTEAD OF TWICE DAILY. PATIENT
BLOOD PRESSURE WAS AGAIN VERY LOW TODAY SO | CALLED DR MADERIC
OFFICE ANDSPOKE WITH MISSY AND LEFT A MESSAGE AT AKC IN MADISON
FOR THE KIDNEY DR.AS WELL. PATIENT DENIED SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION IN
WHICH | EDUCATED ON IMMEDIATE NEED TO CALL 911 WITH. CG AGREES TO
WATCH HER CLOSELY. WOUND CARE WAS PERFORMED AND PATIENT
ALREADY HAS AN ORDER IN FOR PT EVALUATION. PATIENT AND CG BOTH
AGAIN HAVE QUESTIONS ABOUT WHAT WOULD HAPPEN IF SHE WERETO
STOP DIALYSIS AND | EDUCATED ON THIS AND NEED TO DISCUSS THEIR
CONCERNS WITH NEPHROLOGIST AS WELL. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED FOR
ECHO ON WEDNESDAY. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED AND GETS SHORT
OF AIR WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION AND HAS DECREASED
ENDURANCE WITH UNSTEADY GAIT AND FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING
FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE WITH
WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM/TEACH PRESSURE ULCER CARE TO RIGHT AND LEFT BUTTOCKS AS FOLLOWS: CLEANSE WITH WOUND
CLEANSER OR SOAP AND WATER ,APPLY MOISTURE BARRIER OINTMENT AND COVER WITH FOAM BORDERED GAUZE USING CLEAN /ASEPTIC
TECHNIQUE. CHANGE DRESSING EVERY DAY AND PRN SOILING/DISLODGEMENT. MAY UTILIZE EQUIVALENT PRODUCTS. ONCE TAUGHT,
PATIENT/CAREGIVER TO PERFORM ON DAYS SKILLED NURSE IS NOT IN HOME.
SKILLED NURSE TO REVIEW MEDICATION PROFILE/RECONCILE MEDICATIONS, INSTRUCT/REINFORCE MEDICATION MANAGEMENT
SKILLED NURSE TO TEACH HEART FAILURE MANAGEMENT SUCH AS DEFINITION, RISKS FACTORS, AND SYMPTOM MONITORING.
SKILLED NURSE TO TEACH RELATED TO DIABETES MANAGEMENT INCLUDING DISEASE PROCESS, NUTRITION, MEDICATIONS, ACTIVITIES, AND
PERSONAL/ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS. BIOMETRICS OUT OF PARAMETERS AND SIGNIFICANT CHANGES IN STATUS WILL BE REPORTED TO THE
PHYSICIAN.

MADERIC, SUSAN F. MD [PHONE-3972] [PHONE-3973]
Note Date Note Type Entered By Note Status
[DOB-3974] NARRATIVE MARLENA WELTY, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
SKILLED NURSING ADD-ON ASSESSMENT PERFORMED AND PATIENT HAS BILATERAL BUTTOCKS
STAGE 2 PRESSURE ULCERS THAT WILL REQUIRE TEACHING AND INTERVENTION AND
MANAGEMENT. PATIENT HAS ALSO BEEN HAVING FLUCTUATIONS IN HER BLOOD SUGAR AND
COULD BENEFIT FROM DIABETIC TEACHING AND EDUCATION. PATIENT STARTED ON KIDNEY
DIALYSIS TODAY SECONDARY TO END STAGE RENAL DISEASE AND IS ALSO HAVING PROBLEMS
WITH ANEMIA AT THIS TIME IN WHICH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER COULD BOTH BENEFIT FROM
EDUCATION REGARDING AS WELL AS BASIC SYSTEMS ASSESSMENT. PER CAREGIVER PATIENT
HAS A HARD TIME WHEN SHE HAS BOWEL MOVEMENTS BECAUSE THEY ARE THICK AND HARD TO
CLEAN OFF HER PERI AREA WHICH OFTEN CAUSES HER SKIN BREAKDOWN ON HER BOTTOM TO
WORSEN. UPON ASSESSMENT PATIENT IS TAKING PO FERROUS SULFATE FOR ANEMIA IN WHICH|
EDUCATED THAT COULD CAUSE THICK TARRY STOOLS THAT ARE HARD TO CLEAN. |
ENCOURAGED PATIENT AND CAREGIVER TO USE MOIST WIPES WITH PERI WASH CLEANSER TO
CLEAN THE AREA AND TO MAKE SURE SHE IS HYDRATING WITH WATER AS WELLAS TAKING AN
OVER THE COUNTER STOOL SOFTENER AS SUGGESTED BY MD TO PREVENT INCREASED STOOL
THICKNESS. | ALSO EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES AND
PATIENT IS USING HER PRESSURE CUSHION WHILE SITTING IN CHAIR BUT PATIENT ALSO SLEEPS
IN THE CHAIR BECAUSE OF HER NEED TO HAVE HER HEAD ELEVATED WHEN SLEEPING. |
EDUCATED ON SKIN BREAKDOWN PRECAUTIONS AND PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES
INCLUDING NEED TO LIFT HER BUTT OFF AND NOT SCOOT WHEN CHANGING POSITIONS TO
PREVENT SHEARING AND FRICTION ASWELL AS TRYING TO STAND EVERY HOUR WHILE AWAKE
TO RELIEVE SOME OF THE PRESSURE FROM HER BUTTOCKS. SN EDUCATED ON CAUSE OF
PRESSURE SORES AND TREATMENT TO HEAL THEM WHICH INCLUDES DECREASING THE
PRESSURE AND KEEPING THE SKIN AS CLEAN AND DRY AS POSSIBLE. | SHOWED CAREGIVER
HOW TO APPLY OINTMENT TO OPEN WOUNDS AND COVER WITH FOAM BORDER GAUZE.
CAREGIVER AGREES HE WILL PICK UP SOME CALMOSEPTINE OINTMENT FROM THE PHARMACY
AND BEGAN USING THIS AT THIS TIME. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND CAREGIVER ON NEED TO
PHONE NURSE IF WOUNDS BEGAN TO DETERIORATE OR THERE WERE ANY NEW OPEN AREAS
NOTED. PATIENT DOES REQUIRE FREQUENT REPOSITIONING OF THE BODY IN WAYS NOT
FEASIBLE BY AN ORDINARY BED INCLUDING ELEVATION OF HER HEAD AND FEET SECONDARY TO
ORTHOPNEA CAUSED BY CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE REQUIRINGHOSPITAL BED. PATIENT HAS
NEW ONSET SKIN BREAKDOWN TO HER BUTTOCKS IN WHICH WILL REQUIRE NEED OF GEL
OVERLAY OR DRY FOAM MATTRESS AS WELL TO PREVENT DETERIORATION OF CURRENT
WOUNDS. PATIENT HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE, UNSTEADY GAIT, ANDWEAKNESS ALL
REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE AND WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME WITH FREQUENT
REST PERIODS TO RECOVER MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

HER BP WAS LOW AGAIN TODAY. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER BOTH EDUCATED ON NEED TO
CHANGE POSITION SLOWLY TO PREVENT ORTHOSTATIC HYPOTENSION AND DIZZINESS OR

UPON ARRIVAL PATIENT DOES HAVE SOME NOTED EDEMA TO HER FACE UPPER AND LOWER
EXTREMITIES. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER BOTH REPORTS SHE HAS BEEN UNABLE TO RECEIVE A
FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT AS OF LATE SECONDARY TO HER BLOOD PRESSURE CONTINUING TO
DROP. DIALYSIS WAS SUPPOSED TO GET IN TOUCH WITH HER PCP AND SEE ABOUT PLACING HER
ON A MEDICATION TO RAISE HER BLOOD PRESSURE SO THAT SHE COULD HAVE PROPER
DIALYSIS TREATMENTS. MD IS AWARE THAT PATIENT IS HOLDING EXTRA FLUID AT THIS TIME BUT
UNABLE TO HAVE IT REMOVED AT DIALYSIS. PATIENT WAS PLACED ON A FLUID RESTRICTION AND
| DID EDUCATE ON THIS AS WELL AS IMPORTANCE OF SODIUM RESTRICTION TO HER DIET AND|
REVIEWED NUTRITIONAL LABELS WITH BOTH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER AS WELL AS WORKED ON
MEAL PLANNING TO REDUCE SODIUM INTAKE. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER EDUCATED ON WHEN TO
CALL 911 FOR INCREASED SIGNS OF FLUID AND RESPIRATORY DISTRESS NONE OF WHICH
PATIENT HAS AT THIS TIME. CAREGIVER ALSO ENCOURAGED TO CALL THE OFFICE WITH ANY
INCREASING SYMPTOMS BUT TO CALL 911 WITH EMERGENCY SYMPTOMS. PATIENT HAS NO OPEN
AREAS ON HER SKIN AT THIS TIME.
PATIENT AND CAREGIVER BOTH EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF MONITORING THE SKIN TO
BUTTOCKS AND FEET AS WELL AS ENTIRE BODY DAILY DUE TO HER HIGH RISK FOR SKIN
BREAKDOWN. PATIENT HAS NO OPEN AREAS AT THIS TIME BUT THERE ARE SOME SMALL
SCATTERED SCABS NOTED TO BOTH HER LOWER LEGS AND WHICH | ENCOURAGED CAREGIVER
TO MONITOR CLOSELY. EDUCATED ON THE NEED TO CONTINUE TO APPLY MOISTURE BARRIER
OINTMENT TO HER BUTTOCKS AS PREVENTION.PATIENT EDUCATED ON NEED TO CHANGE
POSITION SLOWLY TO PREVENT ORTHOSTATIC HYPOTENSION AND DIZZINESS OR FALLS WITH
RECENT LOW BLOOD PRESSURE READINGS. PATIENT STATES SHE WILL HAVE INTERMITTENT
DIZZINESS BUT HAS HAD NO FALLS.PATIENT HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE, SOA, AND UNSTEADY
GAIT WITH NEUROPATHY AND IS AT HIGH RISK FOR FALLS REQUIRING USE OF WALKER AND
CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

SN VISIT FOR CP AND SKIN ASSESS. ALL VSS FOR PT AT THIS TIME. LS DIMINISHED WITH
WHEEZING NOTED IN ALL LUNG FIELDS. PT WITH DRY COUGH. PT ALSO STARTED ON 3202 FLUID

[DOB-4082] NARRATIVE MARLENA WELTY, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
NEW ORDERS WERE RECEIVED FOR UNNABOOT PLACEMENT/ WOUND CARE SECONDARY TO
NEW ONSET OPEN WOUND TO LEFT LOWER LEG WITH EDEMA. PATIENT WAS SEEN BY PCP IN
HER OFFICE YESTERDAY WITH CEPHALEXIN ANTIBIOTIC ADDED TO PREVENT CELLULITIS. UPON
ARRIVAL WOUND PICTURES AND MEASUREMENTS WERE TAKEN AND WOUND CARE PERFORMED
PER MD ORDER AND PATIENT TOLERATED WELL. ORDER WAS OBTAINED TO APPLY XERORFORM
OR PETROLEUM GAUZE OVER WOUND BEDS PRIOR TO APPLYING UNNABOOT AND TOES WERE
WRAPPED AS WELL DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE WOUNDS ON THE TOES. PATIENT WAS
EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF CIRCULATORY COMPROMISE TO REPORT AND HAS GOOD PEDAL
PULSES NOTED AT THIS TIME. PATIENT DID HAVE A DOPPLER OBTAINED YESTERDAY PRIOR TO
RETURNING HOME IN WHICH THEY SAID WAS NEGATIVE. PATIENTS BLOOD PRESSURE WAS
CHECK THE FIRST TIME AND READ 88 / 31 AND THEN WAS RECHECKED AND WAS STILL
CONSIDERABLY LOW SO DR. MADERIC OFFICE WAS CALLED AND PATIENT WAS ADVISED TO
HOLD HER EVENING DOSE OF PO FUROSEMIDE AND TO TAKE A MIDODRINE TABLET TO INCREASE
HER BLOOD PRESSURE READING. PATIENT HAD A REPORTED FALL YESTERDAY IN WHICH | MADE
AN INCIDENT REPORT AND AFTER TALKING WITH BOTH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER THEY WOULD
PREFER PHYSICAL THERAPY TO COME FOR PATIENTS LEG STRENGTHENING INSTEAD OF
OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY AT THIS TIME SO NEW ORDER OBTAINED FOR NEXT WEEK PT
EVALUATION WHICH IS IN THE 5-DAY RECERTIFICATION WINDOW AND |WILL NOTIFY
OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY OF PATIENTS REQUEST FOR PT ONLY. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER WERE
BOTH EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION TO IMMEDIATELY REPORT AND NEED TO MONITOR
HER SKIN THOROUGHLY AS SHE HAS BRUISES COVERING BOTH ARMS BUT LARGE HEMATOMAS
WITH DRIED SKIN TEARS TO HER RIGHT ARM AS WELL. PATIENT IS ASHEN IN APPEARANCE AND
HAS HAD A STEADY DECLINE SINCE STARTING DIALYSIS TREATMENTS IN WHICH | DISCUSSED
WITH BOTH PATIENT, CAREGIVER, AND MISSY AT DR. MADERICS OFFICE. PATIENT HAS
CONSIDERED NOT DOING DIALYSIS ANYMORE BUT UNDERSTANDS THE CONSEQUENCES OF THIS.
| DID ENCOURAGE HER TO DISCUSS HER FEELINGS WITH NEPHROLOGIST WHEN SHE IS SEEN BY
HIM NEXT WEEK.PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED WITH DECREASED ENDURANCE AND
FATIGUES AND GETS SOA WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION REQUIRING FREQUENT REST
PERIODS TO RECOVER AND CG ASSISTANCE WITH WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING
HER HOMEBOUND.

[DOB-4086] NARRATIVE MARLENA WELTY, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
PATIENT RECERTIFICATION ASSESSMENT COMPLETED AND PATIENT WILL REQUIRE CONTINUED
SKILLED NURSING SERVICES FOR NEW ONSET WOUNDS TO RIGHT LEG AND PREVIOUSLY NOTED
LEFT LEG AND TOE WOUNDSALL OF WHICH REQUIRE WOUND CARE TEACHING AND
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT HAS BEEN DECLINING RAPIDLY OVER THE PAST FEW WEEKS IN WHICH SN
MADE MD AWARE. SHE HAS BEEN UNABLE TO COMPLETE A FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT FOR THE
PAST SEVERAL VISITS SECONDARY TO HER HYPOTENSION AND HAS HAD MULTIPLE FALLS INA
SHORT PERIOD OF TIME FROM HER LEGS BEING SO WEAK. SHE IS HOLDING FLUID IN BOTH HER
LOWER EXTREMITIES AND SOME IN HER ABDOMEN AND IS NO LONGER URINATING
INDEPENDENTLY AT ALL. PATIENT HAS ECCHYMOSIS TO HER ENTIRE RIGHT ARM, SOME LEFT
ARM, AND MINIMALLY TO HER LEGS. AN UNNABOOT WOULD BE APPROPRIATE FOR HER RIGHT
LEG AS WELL BUT THIS LEG IS THE ONE USED TO OBTAIN HER BP READINGS SO DRY DRESSINGS
WERE APPLIED PER MD ORDERS. PATIENT DID WELL WITH THE LLE UNNABOOT THIS LAST TIME
BUT DOESN'T LIKE IT AND REQUESTED HER TOES LEFT OUT. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND
CAREGIVER ON NEED TO MONITOR THE SKIN ON HER TOES CLOSELY TO PREVENT TRAUMA.
MEDS WERE REVIEWED WITH CG WHO ASSISTS HER WITH THEM AND HE HAD GOTTEN
CONFUSED AND WAS ONLY GIVING RANEXA ONCE INSTEAD OF TWICE DAILY. PATIENT BLOOD
PRESSURE WAS AGAIN VERY LOW TODAY SO | CALLED DR MADERIC OFFICE AND SPOKE WITH
MISSY AND LEFT A MESSAGE AT AKC IN MADISON FOR THE KIDNEY DR.AS WELL. PATIENT DENIED
SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION IN WHICH | EDUCATED ON IMMEDIATE NEED TO CALL 911 WITH. CG
AGREES TO WATCH HER CLOSELY. WOUND CARE WAS PERFORMED AND PATIENT ALREADY HAS
AN ORDER IN FOR PT EVALUATION. PATIENT AND CG BOTH AGAIN HAVE QUESTIONS ABOUT
WHAT WOULD HAPPEN IF SHE WERE TO STOP DIALYSIS AND | EDUCATED ON THIS AND NEED TO
DISCUSS THEIR CONCERNS WITH NEPHROLOGIST AS WELL. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED FOR ECHO
ON WEDNESDAY. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED AND GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH MINIMAL TO
MODERATE EXERTION AND HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE WITH UNSTEADY GAIT AND FREQUENT
FALLS REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE WITH
WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

SKILLED NURSING ADD-ON ASSESSMENT PERFORMED AND PATIENT HAS BILATERAL BUTTOCKS STAGE 2 PRESSURE ULCERS THAT WILL
REQUIRE TEACHING AND INTERVENTION AND MANAGEMENT. PATIENT HAS ALSO BEEN HAVING FLUCTUATIONS IN HER BLOOD SUGAR AND
COULD BENEFIT FROM DIABETIC TEACHING AND EDUCATION. PATIENT STARTED ON KIDNEY DIALYSIS TODAY SECONDARY TO END STAGE RENAL
DISEASE AND IS ALSO HAVING PROBLEMS WITH ANEMIA AT THIS TIME IN WHICH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER COULD BOTH BENEFIT FROM
EDUCATION REGARDING AS WELL AS BASIC SYSTEMS ASSESSMENT. PER CAREGIVER PATIENT HAS A HARD TIME WHEN SHE HAS BOWEL
MOVEMENTS BECAUSE THEY ARE THICKAND HARD TO CLEAN OFF HER PERI AREA WHICH OFTEN CAUSES HER SKIN BREAKDOWN ON HER
BOTTOM TO WORSEN. UPON ASSESSMENT PATIENT IS TAKING PO FERROUS SULFATE FOR ANEMIA IN WHICH | EDUCATED THAT COULD CAUSE
THICKTARRY STOOLS THATARE HARD TO CLEAN. | ENCOURAGED PATIENT AND CAREGIVER TO USE MOIST WIPES WITH PERI WASH CLEANSER
TO CLEAN THE AREA AND TO MAKE SURE SHE IS HYDRATING WITH WATER AS WELL AS TAKING AN OVER THE COUNTER STOOL SOFTENER AS
SUGGESTED BY MD TO PREVENT INCREASED STOOL THICKNESS. | ALSO EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES
AND PATIENT IS USING HER PRESSURE CUSHION WHILE SITTING IN CHAIR BUT PATIENT ALSO SLEEPS IN THE CHAIR BECAUSE OF HER NEED TO
HAVE HER HEAD ELEVATED WHEN SLEEPING. | EDUCATED ON SKIN BREAKDOWN PRECAUTIONS AND PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES
INCLUDING NEED TO LIFT HER BUTT OFF ANDNOT SCOOT WHEN CHANGING POSITIONS TO PREVENT SHEARING AND FRICTION AS WELL AS
TRYING TO STAND EVERY HOUR WHILE AWAKE TO RELIEVE SOME OF THE PRESSURE FROM HER BUTTOCKS. SN EDUCATED ON CAUSE OF
PRESSURE SORES AND TREATMENT TO HEAL THEM WHICH INCLUDES DECREASING THE PRESSURE AND KEEPING THE SKIN AS CLEAN AND DRY
AS POSSIBLE. | SHOWED CAREGIVER HOW TO APPLY OINTMENT TO OPEN WOUNDS AND COVER WITH FOAM BORDER GAUZE. CAREGIVER
AGREES HE WILL PICK UP SOME CALMOSEPTINE OINTMENT FROM THE PHARMACY AND BEGAN USING THIS AT THIS TIME. | EDUCATED PATIENT
AND CAREGIVER ON NEED TO PHONE NURSE IF WOUNDS BEGAN TO DETERIORATE OR THERE WEREANY NEW OPEN AREAS NOTED. PATIENT
DOES REQUIRE FREQUENT REPOSITIONING OF THE BODY IN WAYS NOT FEASIBLE BY AN ORDINARY BED INCLUDING ELEVATION OF HER HEAD
AND FEET SECONDARY TO ORTHOPNEA CAUSED BY CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE REQUIRINGHOSPITAL BED. PATIENT HAS NEW ONSET SKIN
BREAKDOWN TO HER BUTTOCKS IN WHICH WILL REQUIRE NEED OF GEL OVERLAY OR DRY FOAM MATTRESS AS WELL TO PREVENT
DETERIORATION OF CURRENT WOUNDS. PATIENT HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE, UNSTEADY GAIT, AND WEAKNESS ALL REQUIRING CAREGIVER
ASSISTANCE ANDWALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME WITH FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

SN VISIT FOR CP AND SKIN ASSESS. ALL VSS FOR PT AT THIS TIME. LS DIMINISHED WITH WHEEZING NOTED IN ALL LUNG FIELDS. PT WITH DRY
COUGH. PT ALSO STARTED ON 3202 FLUID RESTRICTIONS DAILY PER DIALYSIS. PT WITH INCREASED EDEMA TO LLE THAT IS WEEPING. PT ALSO
REPORTS A FALL LAST EVENING WHERE SHE LOST HER BALANCE AND FELL CAUSING A SKIN TEAR TO LLE. WOUND CLEANSED AND DRY
BORBERED DRESSING APPLIED. PT HAS MULTIPLE BRUISING TO ALL EXT. SN CALLED PCP OFFICE AND SPOKE WITH MISSY MAKING HER AWARE
OF ALL ABOVE ISSUES TO INFORM MD. PT HAS APPNT ALREADY SCHEDULED WITH PCP TOMORROW AFTER DIALYSIS. PT A ND CG INSTRUCTED
TO GOTO ER IF SYMPTOMS BECOMES WORSE SUCH AS INCREASED SOA, FEELING OF FULLNESS IN CHEST/LUNGS AND HARD TO TAKE DEEP
BREATH OR INCREASED SWELLING IN BLE.

[DOB-4145] NARRATIVE MARLENA WELTY, RN
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
NEW ORDERS WERE RECEIVED FOR UNNABOOT PLACEMENT/ WOUND CARE SECONDARY TO NEW ONSET OPEN WOUND TO LEFT LOWER LEG
WITH EDEMA. PATIENT WAS SEEN BY PCP IN HER OFFICE YESTERDAY WITH CEPHALEXIN ANTIBIOTIC ADDED TO PREVENT CELLULITIS. UPON
ARRIVAL WOUND PICTURES AND MEASUREMENTS WERE TAKEN AND WOUND CARE PERFORMED PER MD ORDER ANDPATIENT TOLERATED
WELL. ORDER WAS OBTAINED TO APPLY XERORFORM OR PETROLEUM GAUZE OVER WOUND BEDS PRIOR TO APPLYING UNNABOOT AND TOES
WEREWRAPPED AS WELL DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE WOUNDS ON THE TOES. PATIENT WAS EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF CIRCULATORY
COMPROMISE TO REPORT AND HAS GOOD PEDAL PULSES NOTED AT THIS TIME. PATIENT DID HAVE A DOPPLER OBTAINED YESTERDAY PRIOR TO
RETURNING HOME IN WHICH THEY SAID WAS NEGATIVE. PATIENTS BLOOD PRESSURE WAS CHECK THE FIRST TIME AND READ 88 / 31 AND THEN
WAS RECHECKED AND WAS STILL CONSIDERABLY LOW SO DR. MADERIC OFFICE WAS CALLED AND PATIENT WAS ADVISED TO HOLD HER
EVENING DOSE OF PO FUROSEMIDE AND TO TAKE A MIDODRINE TABLET TO INCREASE HER BLOOD PRESSURE READING. PATIENT HADA
REPORTED FALL YESTERDAY IN WHICH | MADE AN INCIDENT REPORT AND AFTER TALKING WITH BOTH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER THEY WOULD
PREFER PHYSICAL THERAPY TO COME FOR PATIENTS LEG STRENGTHENING INSTEAD OF OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY AT THIS TIME SO NEW
ORDER OBTAINED FOR NEXT WEEK PT EVALUATION WHICH IS IN THE 5-DAY RECERTIFICATION WINDOW AND | WILL NOTIFY OCCUPATIONAL
THERAPY OF PATIENTS REQUEST FOR PT ONLY. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER WERE BOTH EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION TO IMMEDIATELY
REPORT AND NEED TO MONITOR HER SKIN THOROUGHLY AS SHE HAS BRUISES COVERING BOTH ARMS BUT LARGE HEMATOMAS WITH DRIED
SKIN TEARS TO HER RIGHT ARM AS WELL. PATIENT IS ASHEN IN APPEARANCE AND HAS HAD A STEADY DECLINE SINCE STARTING DIALYSIS
TREATMENTS IN WHICH I DISCUSSED WITH BOTH PATIENT, CAREGIVER, AND MISSY AT DR. MADERICS OFFICE. PATIENT HAS CONSIDERED NOT
DOING DIALYSIS ANYMORE BUT UNDERSTANDS THE CONSEQUENCES OF THIS. | DID ENCOURAGE HER TO DISCUSS HER FEELINGS WITH
NEPHROLOGIST WHEN SHE IS SEEN BY HIM NEXT WEEK-PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED WITH DECREASED ENDURANCE AND FATIGUES AND
GETS SOA WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND CG ASSISTANCE WITH WALKER TO
SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

[DOB-4146] NARRATIVE MARLENA WELTY, RN
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
PATIENT RECERTIFICATION ASSESSMENT COMPLETED AND PATIENT WILL REQUIRE CONTINUED SKILLED NURSING SERVICES FOR NEW ONSET
WOUNDS TO RIGHT LEG AND PREVIOUSLY NOTED LEFT LEG AND TOE WOUNDS ALL OF WHICH REQUIRE WOUND CARE TEACHING AND
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT HAS BEEN DECLINING RAPIDLY OVER THE PAST FEW WEEKS IN WHICH SN MADE MD AWARE. SHE HAS BEEN UNABLE TO
COMPLETE A FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT FOR THE PAST SEVERAL VISITS SECONDARY TO HER HYPOTENSION AND HAS HAD MULTIPLE FALLS INA
SHORT PERIOD OF TIME FROM HER LEGS BEING SO WEAK. SHE IS HOLDING FLUID IN BOTH HER LOWER EXTREMITIES AND SOME IN HER
ABDOMEN AND IS NO LONGER URINATING INDEPENDENTLY AT ALL. PATIENT HAS ECCHYMOSIS TO HER ENTIRE RIGHT ARM, SOME LEFT ARM,
AND MINIMALLY TO HER LEGS. AN UNNABOOT WOULD BE APPROPRIATE FOR HER RIGHT LEG AS WELL BUT THIS LEG IS THE ONE USED TO
OBTAIN HER BP READINGS SO DRY DRESSINGS WERE APPLIED PER MD ORDERS. PATIENT DID WELL WITH THE LLE UNNABOOT THIS LAST TIME
BUT DOESN'T LIKE IT AND REQUESTED HER TOES LEFT OUT. | EDUCATED PATIENT ANDCAREGIVER ON NEED TO MONITOR THE SKIN ON HER
TOES CLOSELY TO PREVENT TRAUMA. MEDS WERE REVIEWED WITH CG WHO ASSISTS HER WITH THEM AND HE HAD GOTTEN CONFUSED AND
WAS ONLY GIVING RANEXA ONCE INSTEAD OF TWICE DAILY. PATIENT BLOOD PRESSURE WAS AGAIN VERY LOW TODAY SO | CALLED DR MADERIC
OFFICE AND SPOKE WITH MISSY AND LEFT A MESSAGE AT AKC IN MADISON FOR THE KIDNEY DR.AS WELL. PATIENT DENIED SIGNS OF
HYPOTENSION IN WHICH | EDUCATED ON IMMEDIATE NEED TO CALL 911 WITH. CG AGREES TO WATCH HER CLOSELY. WOUND CARE WAS
PERFORMED AND PATIENT ALREADY HAS AN ORDER IN FOR PT EVALUATION. PATIENT AND CG BOTH AGAIN HAVE QUESTIONS ABOUT WHAT
WOULD HAPPEN IF SHE WERETO STOP DIALYSIS AND | EDUCATED ON THIS AND NEED TO DISCUSS THEIR CONCERNS WITH NEPHROLOGIST AS
WELL. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED FOR ECHO ON WEDNESDAY. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED AND GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH MINIMAL TO
MODERATE EXERTION AND HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE WITH UNSTEADY GAIT AND FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS
TO RECOVER AND CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE WITH WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

Client: PRINCE, BETTY M MR No: B3600047293201 Legacy MR No:
Client DOB: 3/1/1941
Insured ID: H43132048 Primary Payor: HUMANA PDGM
Visit Date: [DOB-4387] Visit Number: 1 Visit Type: RNO1 - RN ADD-ON EVALUATION
SKILLED NURSING ADD-ON ASSESSMENT PERFORMED AND PATIENT HAS BILATERAL BUTTOCKS STAGE 2 PRESSURE ULCERS THAT WILL
REQUIRE TEACHING AND INTERVENTION AND MANAGEMENT. PATIENT HAS ALSO BEEN HAVING FLUCTUATIONS IN HER BLOOD SUGAR AND
COULD BENEFIT FROM DIABETIC TEACHING AND EDUCATION. PATIENT STARTED ON KIDNEY DIALYSIS TODAY SECONDARY TO END STAGE
RENAL DISEASE AND IS ALSO HAVING PROBLEMS WITH ANEMIA AT THIS TIME IN WHICH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER COULD BOTH BENEFIT FROM
EDUCATION REGARDING AS WELL AS BASIC SYSTEMS ASSESSMENT. PER CAREGIVER PATIENT HAS A HARD TIME WHEN SHE HAS BOWEL
MOVEMENTS BECAUSE THEY ARE THICK AND HARD TO CLEAN OFF HER PERI AREA WHICH OFTEN CAUSES HER SKIN BREAKDOWN ON HER
BOTTOM TO WORSEN. UPON ASSESSMENT PATIENT IS TAKING PO FERROUS SULFATE FOR ANEMIA IN WHICH | EDUCATED THAT COULD
CAUSE THICKTARRY STOOLS THAT ARE HARD TO CLEAN. | ENCOURAGED PATIENT AND CAREGIVER TO USE MOIST WIPES WITH PERI WASH
CLEANSER TO CLEAN THE AREA AND TO MAKE SURE SHE IS HYDRATING WITH WATER AS WELL AS TAKING AN OVER THE COUNTER STOOL
SOFTENER AS SUGGESTED BY MD TO PREVENT INCREASED STOOL THICKNESS. | ALSO EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF PRESSURE
RELIEF TECHNIQUES AND PATIENT IS USING HER PRESSURE CUSHION WHILE SITTING IN CHAIR BUT PATIENT ALSO SLEEPS IN THE CHAIR
BECAUSE OF HER NEED TO HAVE HER HEAD ELEVATED WHEN SLEEPING. | EDUCATED ON SKIN BREAKDOWN PRECAUTIONS AND PRESSURE
RELIEF TECHNIQUES INCLUDING NEED TO LIFT HER BUTT OFF AND NOT SCOOT WHEN CHANGING POSITIONS TO PREVENT SHEARING AND
FRICTION AS WELL AS TRYING TO STAND EVERY HOUR WHILE AWAKE TO RELIEVE SOME OF THE PRESSURE FROM HER BUTTOCKS. SN
EDUCATED ON CAUSE OF PRESSURE SORES AND TREATMENT TO HEAL THEM WHICH INCLUDES DECREASING THE PRESSURE AND KEEPING
THE SKIN AS CLEAN AND DRY AS POSSIBLE. |SHOWED CAREGIVER HOW TO APPLY OINTMENT TO OPEN WOUNDS AND COVER WITH FOAM
BORDER GAUZE. CAREGIVER AGREES HE WILL PICK UP SOME CALMOSEPTINE OINTMENT FROM THE PHARMACY ANDBEGAN USING THIS AT
THIS TIME. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND CAREGIVER ON NEED TO PHONE NURSE IF WOUNDS BEGAN TO DETERIORATE OR THERE WERE ANY
NEW OPEN AREAS NOTED. PATIENT DOES REQUIRE FREQUENT REPOSITIONING OF THE BODY IN WAYS NOT FEASIBLE BYAN ORDINARY BED
INCLUDING ELEVATION OF HER HEAD AND FEET SECONDARY TO ORTHOPNEA CAUSED BY CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE REQUIRINGHOSPITAL
BED. PATIENT HAS NEW ONSET SKIN BREAKDOWN TO HER BUTTOCKS IN WHICH WILL REQUIRE NEED OF GEL OVERLAY OR DRY FOAM
MATTRESS AS WELL TO PREVENT DETERIORATION OF CURRENT WOUNDS. PATIENT HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE, UNSTEADY GAIT, AND
WEAKNESS ALL REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE ANDWALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME WITH FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER
MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.
Patient Goal
NO MORE FALLS AND TO HEAL SURGICAL WOUND AREA

PRESSURE READINGS AND AS OFTEN ALREADY EXPERIENCED DIZZINESS WITH POSITION CHANGE. PATIENT HAD HER BLOOD PRESSURE
MEDICATIONS DECREASED TODAY AT MD VISIT AS WELL AS CHANGES TO SOME OF HER OTHER MEDICATIONS INCLUDING BEING TAKEN OFF

PROBIOTICS AND RENALYVTE. WOUND CARE PERFORMED WITHOUT PROBLEMS AND THE RIGHT BUTTOCK HAS ALREADY BEGUN TO SHOW

INCONTINENCE -- INABILITY TO MAKE IT TO THE BATHROOM OR COMMODE IN TIMELY MANNER INCLUDES FREQUENCY, URGENCY, AND/OR

VISUAL IMPAIRMENT -- INCLUDES BUT NOT LIMITED TO, MACULAR DEGENERATION, DIABETIC RETINOPATHIES, VISUAL FIELD LOSS, AGE

NO

DEPRESSION OR COMPLIANCE WITH SAFETY RECOMMENDATIONS.
NO
COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT -- COULD INCLUDE PATIENTS WITH DEMENTIA, ALZHEIMER'S OR STROKE PATIENTS, OR PATIENTS WHO ARE

FUNCTIONAL TESTS AND MEASURES
PLEASE INDICATE WHICH TESTS AND MEASURES WILL BE PERFORMED DURING THIS VISIT (MARK ALL THAT APPLY):
BARTHEL INDEX OF DAILY LIVING (NEURO)
THE BARTHEL ADL INDEX
GUIDELINES
1. THE INDEX SHOULD BE USED AS A RECORD OF WHAT A PATIENT DOES, NOT AS A RECORD OF WHAT A PATIENT COULD DO.
2. THE MAIN AIM IS TO ESTABLISH DEGREE OF INDEPENDENCE FROM ANY HELP, PHYSICAL OR VERBAL, HOWEVER MINOR AND FOR
WHATEVER REASON.
3. THE NEED FOR SUPERVISION RENDERS THE PATIENT NOT INDEPENDENT.
4. A PATIENT'S PERFORMANCE SHOULD BE ESTABLISHED USING THE BEST AVAILABLE EVIDENCE. ASKING THE PATIENT,
ERIENDS/RELATIVES AND NURSES ARE THE USUAL SOURCES, BUT DIRECT OBSERVATION AND COMMON SENSE ARE ALSO IMPORTANT.
HOWEVER DIRECT TESTING IS NOT NEEDED.
5. USUALLY THE PATIENT'S PERFORMANCE OVER THE PRECEDING 24-48 HOURS IS IMPORTANT, BUT OCCASIONALLY LONGER PERIODS
WILL BE RELEVANT.
6. MIDDLE CATEGORIES IMPLY THAT THE PATIENT SUPPLIES OVER 50 PER CENT OF THE EFFORT.
7. USE OF AIDS TO BE INDEPENDENT IS ALLOWED.
FEEDING:
10 = INDEPENDENT
BATHING:
0 = DEPENDENT
GROOMING:
5 = INDEPENDENT FACE/HAIR/TEETH/SHAVING (IMPLEMENTS PROVIDED)
DRESSING:
5 = NEEDS HELP BUT CAN DO ABOUT HALF UNAIDED.
BOWELS:
10 = CONTINENT

UPON ARRIVAL PATIENT RESTING IN RECLINER WITH LEGS DANGLING IN NO ACUTE DISTRESS AND HER HUSBAND IS IN HOME WITH HER.
CAREGIVER STATES THAT THEY REALLY LIKE THE OT BUT PATIENT IS REFUSING FOR ANYONE ELSE TO ASSIST HER WITH BATHING WITH THE
EXCEPTION OF HIM AND PATIENT REPORTS THAT SHE IS VERY OLD-FASHIONED AND DOESN'T WISH FOR ANYONE ELSE TO HELP HER. PATIENT
CONTINUES TO HAVE LOWER BLOOD PRESSURES DESPITE HER CARVEDILOL BEING DISCONTINUED BUT DENIED SYMPTOMS OF
ORTHOSTATIC HYPOTENSION IN WHICH | EDUCATED ON. PATIENT ENCOURAGED TO CHANGE POSITION SLOWLY TO PREVENT DIZZINESS OR
FALLS. PATIENT HAS BEEN USING HER HOSPITAL BED WITH SO MY REALLY MATTRESS AND THIS IS BEEN HELPING WITH HER PRESSURE
ULCER STATUS AS WELL AS APPLYING THE MOISTURE BARRIER OINTMENT. WOUNDS ARE CONTINUING TO SHOW PROGRESS AND WOUND
CARE PERFORMED IN WHICH PATIENT TOLERATED WELL.PATIENT AND CAREGIVER BOTH EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF | NEED TO

HEALTHINESS.PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED WITH DECREASED ENDURANCE REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE ANDWALKER TO
SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

INDICATE ACTION PLAN DETAILS:
DR MADERIC WILL BE NOTIFIED
UPON ARRIVAL PATIENT DOES HAVE SOME NOTED EDEMA TO HER FACE UPPER AND LOWER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER BOTH
REPORTS SHE HAS BEEN UNABLE TO RECEIVE A FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT AS OF LATE SECONDARY TO HER BLOOD PRESSURE CONTINUING
TO DROP. DIALYSIS WAS SUPPOSED TO GET IN TOUCH WITH HER PCP ANDSEE ABOUT PLACING HER ON A MEDICATION TO RAISE HER BLOOD
PRESSURE SO THAT SHE COULD HAVE PROPER DIALYSIS TREATMENTS. MD IS AWARE THAT PATIENT IS HOLDING EXTRA FLUID AT THIS TIME
BUT UNABLE TO HAVE IT REMOVED AT DIALYSIS. PATIENT WAS PLACED ON A FLUID RESTRICTION AND | DID EDUCATE ON THIS AS WELL AS
IMPORTANCE OF SODIUM RESTRICTION TO HER DIET AND! REVIEWED NUTRITIONAL LABELS WITH BOTH PATIENT ANDCAREGIVER AS WELL
AS WORKED ON MEAL PLANNING TO REDUCE SODIUM INTAKE. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER EDUCATED ON WHEN TO CALL 911 FOR INCREASED
SIGNS OF FLUID AND RESPIRATORY DISTRESS NONE OF WHICH PATIENT HAS AT THIS TIME. CAREGIVER ALSO ENCOURAGED TO CALL THE
OFFICE WITH ANY INCREASING SYMPTOMS BUT TO CALL 911 WITH EMERGENCY SYMPTOMS. PATIENT HAS NO OPEN AREAS ON HER SKIN AT
THIS TIME.
PATIENT AND CAREGIVER BOTH EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF MONITORING THE SKIN TO BUTTOCKS AND FEET AS WELL AS ENTIRE
BODY DAILY DUE TO HER HIGH RISK FOR SKIN BREAKDOWN. PATIENT HAS NO OPEN AREAS AT THIS TIME BUT THERE ARE SOME SMALL
SCATTERED SCABS NOTED TO BOTH HER LOWER LEGS AND WHICH I ENCOURAGED CAREGIVER TO MONITOR CLOSELY. EDUCATED ON THE
NEED TO CONTINUE TO APPLY MOISTURE BARRIER OINTMENT TO HER BUTTOCKS AS PREVENTION.PATIENT EDUCATED ON NEED TO CHANGE
POSITION SLOWLY TO PREVENT ORTHOSTATIC HYPOTENSION AND DIZZINESS OR FALLS WITH RECENT LOW BLOOD PRESSURE READINGS.
PATIENT STATES SHE WILL HAVE INTERMITTENT DIZZINESS BUT HAS HAD NO FALLS.PATIENT HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE, SOA, AND
UNSTEADY GAIT WITH NEUROPATHY AND IS AT HIGH RISK FOR FALLS REQUIRING USE OF WALKER AND CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE TO SAFELY
LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

BARTHEL INDEX OF DAILY LIVING (NEURO)
THE BARTHEL ADL INDEX
GUIDELINES
1. THE INDEX SHOULD BE USED AS A RECORD OF WHAT A PATIENT DOES, NOT AS A RECORD OF WHAT A PATIENT COULD DO.
2. THE MAIN AIM IS TO ESTABLISH DEGREE OF INDEPENDENCE FROM ANY HELP, PHYSICAL OR VERBAL, HOWEVER MINOR AND FOR
WHATEVER REASON.
3. THE NEED FOR SUPERVISION RENDERS THE PATIENT NOT INDEPENDENT.
4. A PATIENT'S PERFORMANCE SHOULD BE ESTABLISHED USING THE BEST AVAILABLE EVIDENCE. ASKING THE PATIENT
ERIENDS/RELATIVES AND NURSES ARE THE USUAL SOURCES, BUT DIRECT OBSERVATION AND COMMON SENSE ARE ALSO IMPORTANT.
HOWEVER DIRECT TESTING IS NOT NEEDED.
5. USUALLY THE PATIENT'S PERFORMANCE OVER THE PRECEDING 24-48 HOURS IS IMPORTANT, BUT OCCASIONALLY LONGER PERIODS
WILL BE RELEVANT.
6. MIDDLE CATEGORIES IMPLY THAT THE PATIENT SUPPLIES OVER 50 PER CENT OF THE EFFORT.
7. USE OF AIDS TO BE INDEPENDENT IS ALLOWED.
FEEDING:
10 = INDEPENDENT
BATHING:
0 = DEPENDENT
GROOMING:
5 = INDEPENDENT FACE/HAIR/TEETH/SHAVING (IMPLEMENTS PROVIDED)
DRESSING:
5 = NEEDS HELP BUT CAN DO ABOUT HALF UNAIDED.
BOWELS:

N/A
Ww R FO T Ww ES TO TO
LEG WITH EDEMA. PATIENT WAS SEEN BY PCP IN HER OFFICE YESTERDAY WITH CEPHALEXIN ANTIBIOTIC ADDED TO PREVENT CELLULITIS.
UPON ARRIVAL WOUND PICTURES AND MEASUREMENTS WERE TAKEN AND WOUND CARE PERFORMED PER MD ORDER AND PATIENT
TOLERATED WELL. ORDER WAS OBTAINED TO APPLY XERORFORM OR PETROLEUM GAUZE OVER WOUND BEDS PRIOR TO APPLYING
UNNABOOT ANDTOES WERE WRAPPED AS WELL DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE WOUNDS ON THE TOES. PATIENT WAS EDUCATED ON SIGNS
OF CIRCULATORY COMPROMISE TO REPORT AND HAS GOOD PEDAL PULSES NOTED AT THIS TIME. PATIENT DID HAVE A DOPPLER OBTAINED
YESTERDAY PRIOR TO RETURNING HOME IN WHICH THEY SAID WAS NEGATIVE. PATIENTS BLOOD PRESSURE WAS CHECK THE FIRST TIME
AND READ 88/ 31 AND THEN WAS RECHECKED AND WAS STILL CONSIDERABLY LOW SO DR. MADERIC OFFICE WAS CALLED AND PATIENT WAS
ADVISED TO HOLD HER EVENING DOSE OF PO FUROSEMIDE AND TO TAKE A MIDODRINE TABLET TO INCREASE HER BLOOD PRESSURE
READING. PATIENT HAD A REPORTED FALL YESTERDAY IN WHICH | MADE AN INCIDENT REPORT ANDAFTER TALKING WITH BOTH PATIENT AND
CAREGIVER THEY WOULD PREFER PHYSICAL THERAPY TO COME FOR PATIENTS LEG STRENGTHENING INSTEAD OF OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY
AT THIS TIME SO NEW ORDER OBTAINED FOR NEXT WEEK PT EVALUATION WHICH IS IN THE 5-DAY RECERTIFICATION WINDOW AND |WILL
NOTIFY OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY OF PATIENTS REQUEST FOR PT ONLY. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER WERE BOTH EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF
HYPOTENSION TO IMMEDIATELY REPORT AND NEED TO MONITOR HER SKIN THOROUGHLY AS SHE HAS BRUISES COVERING BOTH ARMS BUT
LARGE HEMATOMAS WITH DRIED SKIN TEARS TO HER RIGHT ARM AS WELL. PATIENT IS ASHEN IN APPEARANCE AND HAS HAD A STEADY
DECLINE SINCE STARTING DIALYSIS TREATMENTS IN WHICH | DISCUSSED WITH BOTH PATIENT, CAREGIVER, AND MISSY AT DR. MADERICS
OFFICE. PATIENT HAS CONSIDERED NOT DOING DIALYSIS ANYMORE BUT UNDERSTANDS THE CONSEQUENCES OF THIS. | DID ENCOURAGE
HER TO DISCUSS HER FEELINGS WITH NEPHROLOGIST WHEN SHE IS SEEN BY HIM NEXT WEEK.PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED WITH
DECREASED ENDURANCE AND FATIGUES AND GETS SOA WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO
RECOVER AND CG ASSISTANCE WITH WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.
"Patient Goal
NO MORE FALLS AND TO HEAL SURGICAL WOUND AREA
!
1. INSTRUCT PATIENT /CAREGIVER ON SIGNS / SYMPTOMS OF DISEASE THAT REQUIRE IMMEDIATE ATTENTION.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT AND CG EDUCATED ON WHEN TO CALL 911 AS IT RELATES TO FLUID OVERLOAD OR HYPOTENSION

SENSORY PERCEPTION -ABILITY TO RESPOND MEANINGFULLY TO PRESSURE-RELATED DISCOMFORT
SLIGHTLY LIMITED - RESPONDS TO VERBAL COMMANDS BUT CANNOT ALWAYS COMMUNICATE DISCOMFORT OR NEED TO BE TURNED, OR
HAS SOME SENSORY IMPAIRMENT WHICH LIMITS ABILITY TO FEEL PAIN OR DISCOMFORT IN 1OR 2 EXTREMITIES.
MOISTURE - DEGREE TO WHICH SKIN IS EXPOSED TO MOISTURE
OCCASIONALLY MOIST -SKIN IS OCCASIONALLY MOIST, REQUIRING AN EXTRA LINEN CHANGE APPROXIMATELY ONCE A DAY.
ACTIVITY - DEGREE OF PHYSICAL ACTIVITY
CHAIRFAST -ABILITY TO WALK SEVERELY LIMITED OR NON-EXISTENT. CANNOT BEAR OWN WEIGHT AND/OR MUST BE ASSISTED INTO
CHAIR OR WHEELCHAIR.
MOBILITY -ABILITY TO CHANGE AND CONTROL BODY POSITION
VERY LIMITED - MAKES OCCASIONAL SLIGHT CHANGES IN BODY OR EXTREMITY POSITION BUT UNABLE TO MAKE FREQUENT OR
SIGNIFICANT CHANGES INDEPENDENTLY.
NUTRITION - USUAL FOOD INTAKE PATTERN
PROBABLY INADEQUATE -RARELY EATS A COMPLETE MEAL AND GENERALLY EATS ONLY ABOUT 1/2 OF ANY FOOD OFFERED. PROTEIN
INTAKE INCLUDES ONLY 3 SERVINGS OF MEAT OR DAIRY PRODUCTS PER DAY. OCCASIONALLY WILL TAKE A DIETARY SUPPLEMENT, OR
RECEIVES LESS THAN OPTIMUM AMOUNT OF LIQUID DIET OR TUBE FEEDING.
ERICTION AND SHEAR
POTENTIAL PROBLEM - MOVES FEEBLY OR REQUIRES MINIMUM ASSISTANCE. DURING A MOVE SKIN PROBABLY SLIDES TO SOME EXTENT
AGAINST SHEETS, CHAIR, RESTRAINTS, OR OTHER DEVICES. MAINTAINS RELATIVELY GOOD POSITION IN CHAIR OR BED MOST OF THE
TIME BUT OCCASIONALLY SLIDES DOWN.
TOTAL SCORE (PATIENTS WITH A TOTAL SCORE OF 12 OR LESS ARE CONSIDERED TO BE AT HIGH RISK OF DEVELOPING PRESSURE
ULCERS):
14

MODERATE

(M&E780) MANAGEMENT OF ORAL MEDICATIONS: PATIENT'S ABILITY TO PREPARE AND TAKE ALL PRESCRIBED ORAL MEDICATIONS RELIABLY
AND SAFELY, INCLUDING ADMINISTRATION OF THE CORRECT DOSAGE AT THE APPROPRIATE TIMES /INTERVALS. EXCLUDES INJECTABLE
AND IV MEDICATIONS. (NOTE: THIS REFERS TO ABILITY, NOT COMPLIANCE OR WILLINGNESS.)
2 - UNABLE TO TAKE MEDICATION UNLESS ADMINISTERED BY SOMEONE ELSE
(M&E790) MANAGEMENT OF INHALANT / MIST MEDICATIONS: PATIENT'S ABILITY TO PREPARE AND TAKE ALL PRESCRIBED INHALANT / MIST
MEDICATIONS (NEBULIZERS, METERED DOSE DEVICES) RELIABLY AND SAFELY, INCLUDING ADMINISTRATION OF THE CORRECT DOSAGE AT
THE APPROPRIATE TIMES / INTERVALS. EXCLUDES ALL OTHER FORMS OF MEDICATION (ORAL TABLETS, INJECTABLE AND IV MEDICATIONS):
2 - UNABLE TO TAKE MEDICATION UNLESS ADMINISTERED BY SOMEONE ELSE
(M2030) MANAGEMENT OF INJECTABLE MEDICATIONS: PATIENT'S CURRENT ABILITY TO PREPARE AND TAKE ALL PRESCRIBED INJECTABLE
MEDICATIONS RELIABLY AND SAFELY, INCLUDING ADMINISTRATION OF CORRECT DOSAGE AT THE APPROPRIATE TIMES/AINTERVALS.
EXCLUDES IV MEDICATIONS.
3 - UNABLE TO TAKE INJECTABLE MEDICATION UNLESS ADMINISTERED BY ANOTHER PERSON.

PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED AND GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION AND HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE
WITH UNSTEADY GAIT AND FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE WITH
WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.
FOCUS OF CARE (SUPPORTING CLINICAL INFORMATION, PERTINENT MEDICAL HISTORY, ADDITIONAL DIAGNOSIS, LAST REHOSPITALIZATION,
ER, OR URGENCY CARE VISIT):
PATIENT RECERTIFICATION ASSESSMENT COMPLETED ANDPATIENT WILL REQUIRE CONTINUED SKILLED NURSING SERVICES FOR NEW
ONSET WOUNDS TO RIGHT LEG AND PREVIOUSLY NOTED LEFT LEG AND TOE WOUNDS ALL OF WHICH REQUIRE WOUND CARE TEACHING
AND ASSESSMENT. PATIENT HAS BEEN DECLINING RAPIDLY OVER THE PAST FEW WEEKS IN WHICH SN MADE MD AWARE. SHE HAS BEEN
UNABLE TO COMPLETE A FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT FOR THE PAST SEVERAL VISITS SECONDARY TO HER HYPOTENSION AND HAS HAD
MULTIPLE FALLS IN A SHORT PERIOD OF TIME FROM HER LEGS BEING SO WEAK. SHE IS HOLDING FLUID IN BOTH HER LOWER
EXTREMITIES AND SOME IN HER ABDOMEN AND IS NO LONGER URINATING INDEPENDENTLY AT ALL. PATIENT HAS ECCHYMOSIS TO HER
ENTIRE RIGHT ARM, SOME LEFT ARM, ANDMINIMALLY TO HER LEGS. AN UNNABOOT WOULD BE APPROPRIATE FOR HER RIGHT LEG AS
WELL BUT THIS LEG IS THE ONE USED TO OBTAIN HER BP READINGS SO DRY DRESSINGS WERE APPLIED PER MD ORDERS. PATIENT DID
WELL WITH THE LLE UNNABOOT THIS LAST TIME BUT DOESN'T LIKE IT AND REQUESTED HER TOES LEFT OUT. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND
CAREGIVER ON NEED TO MONITOR THE SKIN ON HER TOES CLOSELY TO PREVENT TRAUMA. MEDS WERE REVIEWED WITH CG WHO
ASSISTS HER WITH THEM ANDHE HAD GOTTEN CONFUSED AND WAS ONLY GIVING RANEXA ONCE INSTEAD OF TWICE DAILY. PATIENT
BLOOD PRESSURE WAS AGAIN VERY LOW TODAY SO 1CALLED DR MADERIC OFFICE AND SPOKE WITH MISSY AND LEFT A MESSAGE AT
AKC IN MADISON FOR THE KIDNEY DR.AS WELL. PATIENT DENIED SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION IN WHICH | EDUCATED ON IMMEDIATE NEED TO
CALL 911 WITH. CG AGREES TO WATCH HER CLOSELY. WOUND CARE WAS PERFORMED AND PATIENT ALREADY HAS AN ORDER IN FOR PT
EVALUATION. PATIENT ANDCG BOTH AGAIN HAVE QUESTIONS ABOUT WHAT WOULD HAPPEN IF SHE WERE TO STOP DIALYSIS AND|
EDUCATED ON THIS AND NEED TO DISCUSS THEIR CONCERNS WITH NEPHROLOGIST AS WELL. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED FOR ECHO ON
WEDNESDAY. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED AND GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION AND HAS
DECREASED ENDURANCE WITH UNSTEADY GAIT AND FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND
CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE WITH WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

INCONTINENCE -- INABILITY TO MAKE IT TO THE BATHROOM OR COMMODE IN TIMELY MANNER INCLUDES FREQUENCY, URGENCY, AND/OR

VISUAL IMPAIRMENT -- INCLUDES BUT NOT LIMITED TO, MACULAR DEGENERATION, DIABETIC RETINOPATHIES, VISUAL FIELD LOSS, AGE
RELATED CHANGES, DECLINE IN VISUAL ACUITY, ACCOMMODATION, GLARE TOLERANCE, DEPTH PERCEPTION, AND NIGHT VISION OR NOT

NO
IMPAIRED FUNCTIONAL MOBILITY - MAY INCLUDE PATIENTS WHO NEED HELP WITH IADLS OR ADLS OR HAVE GAIT OR TRANSFER
PROBLEMS, ARTHRITIS, PAIN, FEAR OF FALLING, FOOT PROBLEMS, IMPAIRED SENSATION, IMPAIRED COORDINATION OR IMPROPER USE OF

YES
ENVIRONMENTAL HAZARDS -- MAY INCLUDE BUT NOT LIMITED TO, POOR ILLUMINATION, EQUIPMENT TUBING, INAPPROPRIATE FOOTWEAR,
PETS, HARD TO REACH ITEMS, FLOOR SURFACES THAT ARE UNEVEN OR CLUTTERED, OR OUTDOOR ENTRY AND EXITS.
YES
POLY PHARMACY (4 OR MORE PRESCRIPTIONS - ANY TYPE) -- ALL PRESCRIPTIONS INCLUDING PRESCRIPTIONS FOR OTC MEDS. DRUGS
HIGHLYASSOCIATED WITH FALL RISK INCLUDE BUT NOT LIMITED TO, SEDATIVES, ANTI-DEPRESSANTS, TRANQUILIZERS, NARCOTICS
ANTIHYPERTENSIVES, CARDIAC MEDS, CORTICOSTEROIDS, ANTI-ANXIETY DRUGS, ANTICHOLINERGIC DRUGS, AND HYPOGLYCEMIC DRUGS.
YES
PAIN AFFECTING LEVEL OF FUNCTION - PAIN OFTEN AFFECTS AN INDIVIDUAL'S DESIRE OR ABILITY TO MOVE, OR PAIN CAN BE_A FACTOR IN
DEPRESSION OR COMPLIANCE WITH SAFETY RECOMMENDATIONS.
NO
COGNITIVE IMPAIRMENT -- COULD INCLUDE PATIENTS WITH DEMENTIA, ALZHEIMER'S OR STROKE PATIENTS, OR PATIENTS WHOARE
CONFUSED, USE POOR JUDGMENT, HAVE DECREASED COMPREHENSION, IMPULSIVITY, MEMORY DEFICITS. CONSIDER PATIENTS ABILITY TO

FUNCTIONAL TESTS AND MEASURES
PLEASE INDICATE WHICH TESTS AND MEASURES WILL BE PERFORMED DURING THIS VISIT (MARK ALL THAT APPLY):
BARTHEL INDEX OF DAILY LIVING (NEURO)
THE BARTHEL ADL INDEX
GUIDELINES
1. THE INDEX SHOULD BE USED AS A RECORD OF WHAT A PATIENT DOES, NOT AS A RECORD OF WHAT A PATIENT COULD DO.
2. THE MAIN AIM IS TO ESTABLISH DEGREE OF INDEPENDENCE FROM ANY HELP, PHYSICAL OR VERBAL, HOWEVER MINOR AND FOR
WHATEVER REASON.
3. THE NEED FOR SUPERVISION RENDERS THE PATIENT NOT INDEPENDENT.
4.A PATIENT'S PERFORMANCE SHOULD BE ESTABLISHED USING THE BEST AVAILABLE EVIDENCE. ASKING THE PATIENT,
FRIENDS/RELATIVES AND NURSES ARE THE USUAL SOURCES, BUT DIRECT OBSERVATION AND COMMON SENSE ARE ALSO IMPORTANT.
HOWEVER DIRECT TESTING IS NOT NEEDED.
5. USUALLY THE PATIENT'S PERFORMANCE OVER THE PRECEDING 24-48 HOURS IS IMPORTANT, BUT OCCASIONALLY LONGER PERIODS
WILL BE RELEVANT.
6. MIDDLE CATEGORIES IMPLY THAT THE PATIENT SUPPLIES OVER 50 PER CENT OF THE EFFORT.
7. USE OF AIDS TO BE INDEPENDENT IS ALLOWED.
FEEDING:
10 = INDEPENDENT
BATHING:
0 = DEPENDENT
GROOMING:
5 = INDEPENDENT FACE/HAIR/TEETH/SHAVING (IMPLEMENTS PROVIDED)
DRESSING:
5 = NEEDS HELP BUT CAN DO ABOUT HALF UNAIDED.
BOWELS:
10 = CONTINENT
BLADDER:
10 = CONTINENT
TOILET USE:
5 = NEEDS SOME HELP, BUT CAN DO SOMETHING ALONE
TRANSEERS: (BED TO CHAIR AND BACK)
10 = MINOR HELP (VERBAL OR PHYSICAL)
MOBILITY (ON LEVEL SURFACES):
10 = WALKS WITH HELP OF ONE PERSON (VERBAL OR PHYSICAL) > 50 YARDS

SN VISIT FOR CP AND GENERAL ASSESS. PT LYING IN BEDTIRES FROM GOING TO DIALYSIS FOR 5 HRS THIS MORNING ANDJUST GOT BACK
HOME. PT AND CG REPORTS DIALYSIS PULLED OFF 7 POUNDS FLUID TODAY. PT REPORTS DIALYSIS HAD HARD TIME WITH PT BLOOD
PRESSURE TODAY AS IT WAS VERY LOW. BP CONTINUE LOW AT SN VISIT AND PT DENIES ANY OTHER HYPOTENSION SX. WOUND CARE
PROVIDED TO ALL AREAS ANDUNNA BOOTS REAPPLIED TO LLE. EDEMA TO LLE IS MUCH IMPROVED TO LOWER LEG BUT REMAINS ABOVE THE
KNEE. PT TEACHING TO KEEP LEGS ELEATED ABOVE HEART AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE WHILE LYNG IN BED RESTING. PT CONTINUES ON ABX.
DENIES ANY FURTHER FALLS.

Order Date: 2/2/2021 1:28 PM Order Type: ADD ON DISCIPLINE
Order Description:
SKILLED NURSE TO EVALUATE AND DEVELOP PLAN OF CARE TO BE COUNTERSIGNED BY PHYSICIAN. SKILLED
NURSE TO ASSESS/EVALUATE CO-MORBID CONDITIONS INCLUDING ESRD, DM II, CHF, PRESSURE ULCERS, ANEMIA,
AND OTHER CONDITIONS THAT PRESENT THEMSELVES DURING THE COURSE OF THIS EPISODE TO IDENTIFY
CHANGES AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. NURSING ASSESSMENT TO BE PERFORMED BY RN: LPN
TO OBSERVE AND REPORT CHANGES TO THE RN.
SKILLED NURSE TO OBSERVE/ASSESS CARDIOVASCULAR SYSTEM, IDENTIFY CHANGES, AND INTERVENE TO
MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. SKILLED NURSE TO TEACH RELATED TO ALTERED CARDIOVASCULAR STATUS
INCLUDING DISEASE PROCESS, NUTRITION, MEDICATIONS, ACTIVITIES, PERSONAL/ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS,
AND TO REPORT WEIGHT GAIN OF 2-3 LBS/DAY OR 5 LBS/WK. MAY PERFORM 02 SATURATION LEVELS PRN FOR
SIGNS AND/OR SYMPTOMS OF POSSIBLE RESPIRATORY COMPLICATIONS. SIGNIFICANT CHANGES IN STATUS WILL
BE REPORTED TO PHYSICIAN.
SKILLED NURSE TO OBSERVE/ASSESS GENITOURINARY SYSTEM, IDENTIFY CHANGES, AND INTERVENE TO
MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. SKILLED NURSE TO TEACH RELATED TO ALTERED GENITOURINARY STATUS
INCLUDING DISEASE PROCESS, NUTRITION, MEDICATIONS, ACTIVITIES, AND PERSONAL/ENVIRONMENTAL
FACTORS. SIGNIFICANT CHANGES IN CONDITION WILL BE REPORTED TO PHYSICIAN.
SKILLED NURSE TO OBSERVE/ASSESS INTEGUMENTARY SYSTEM, IDENTIFY CHANGES, AND INTERVENE TO
MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. SKILLED NURSE TO TEACH RELATED TO ALTERED SKIN INTEGRITY INCLUDING
DISEASE PROCESS, NUTRITION, MEDICATIONS, ACTIVITIES, AND PERSONAL/ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS.
SIGNIFICANT CHANGES IN STATUS WILL BE REPORTED TO PHYSICIAN. NURSING ASSESSMENT TO BE PERFORMED
BY RN. LPN TO OBSERVE AND REPORT CHANGES TO THE RN.
SKILLED NURSE TO PERFORM/TEACH PRESSURE ULCER CARE TO RIGHT AND LEFT BUTTOCKS AS FOLLOWS:
CLEANSE WITH WOUND CLEANSER OR SOAP AND WATER ,APPLY MOISTURE BARRIER OINTMENT AND COVER
WITH FOAM BORDERED GAUZE USING CLEAN / ASEPTIC TECHNIQUE. CHANGE DRESSING EVERY DAY AND PRN
SOILING/DISLODGEMENT. MAY UTILIZE EQUIVALENT PRODUCTS. ONCE TAUGHT, PATIENT/CAREGIVER TO
PERFORM ON DAYS SKILLED NURSE IS NOT IN HOME.

7/24/2020 6:25:33 AM DENISE MOORE, RN CARE PLAN ASSESSMENT, PHE, MEDICATION MANAGEMENT AND DISEASE Active
PROCESSES. RELATED TO RECENT FALL AND SUBDURAL HEMORRHAGE.
79 YEAR OLD FEMALE, LIVES WITH SPOUSE WHO IS PRIMARY CAREGIVER. HE
IS WILLING AND ABLE TO PROVIDE CARE FOR PATIENT. PATIENT WENT TO
OUTPATIENT REGISTRATION ON JULY 14TH, FOR PLACEMENT OF PORT FOR
FUTURE DIALYSIS USE. PATIENT WAS AMBULATING WITH ROLLING WALKER IN

SN FOR WOUND ASSESSMENT AND CARE, CP ASSESS, MEDICATION REGIMEN_ Active
REVIEW, MEDICATION ADHERENCE PROMOTION, MEDICATION TEACHING AND

NOTIFY SN IF ANY ABNORMAL WOUND FINDINGS SUCH AS INCREASED
DRAINAGE, FOUL ODOR, OR ACUTE PAIN ONSET. DISCUSSED IMPORTANCE OF
MAINTAINING A CLEAN ATMOSPHERE AND WASHING HANDS BEFORE AND
AFTER ANY CONTACT WITH WOUND OR WOUND AREA TO PREVENT SPREAD
OF INFECTION. PATIENT ADVISED ON HIGH PROTEIN DIET CHOICES. BASIC
WOUND HEALING CONCEPTS AND THEORIES APPROPRIATE FOR THIS
PATIENTS LEVEL OF UNDERSTANDING, REVIEWED AND ACKNOWLEDGED.
PATIENT IS ALERT AND ORIENTED TIMES 3 AND ABLE TO VERBALIZE NEEDS
WITHOUT DIFFICULTY. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE

COMPLIANT WITH ALL MEDICATIONS. PATIENT HAS NO QUESTIONS ABOUT

INTERVENTIONS AS INDICATED BY THE INTERVENTIONS AND GOALS
DOCUMENTATION. PATIENT VERBALIZES UNDERSTANDING AND HAS NO

ADEQUATELY CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES THAT

GLUCOSE. WOUNDED SCALP IS NEARLY HEALED AT THIS ASSESSMENT.
THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF INFECTION. THERE ARE NO SIGNS
ANDSYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS OBSERVED OR STATEDAT THIS
ASSESSMENT.

PATIENT WAS SEEN LAST WEEK AT PRIMARY CARE PHYSICIANS OFFICE. Active
PATIENT SUBMITTED LAB SAMPLES. THE LAB SAMPLE RESULTS WERE SENT
TO HER NEPHROLOGIST DR. RAZA. DR. RAZA INITIATED CEPHALEXIN 500 MG
CAPSULES ONE TWICE A DAY FOR 7 DAYS FOR A POSITIVE UA. PATIENT
STATES SHES HAD NO UTI SXS AND WAS SUPRISED TO GET ABX TX. THIS
MORNING PATIENT HAS BEEN HAVING INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH
AMBULATION RESULTING IN SEVERAL ALMOST FALLS AND TWO ACTUAL FALLS
OVER THE LAST WEEK. PATIENT HAS BRUISING ABOVE HER RIGHT EYE AND
MULTIPLE BRUISING ABOUT HER UPPER EXTREMITIES. PATIENT SKIN IS CLEAN
DRY AND INTACT AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE LAST FALL OCCURRED WHEN
THE PATIENT WAS TRYING TO EXIT A VEHICLE. PATIENT CAREGIVER STATES
THAT HE THINKS HER SHOES MAY HAVE BEEN A LITTLE BIT TOO BIG FOR HER
AND SHE MAY HAVE TRIPPED OVER SHOES. PATIENT ADVISED TO WEAR
STABLE PLATFORM SHOES AND TO MOVE SLOW AND WAIT FOR ASSISTANCE
FOR ALL TRANSFERS TO REDUCE FALL OCCURRENCES. PATIENT AND
CAREGIVERS STATE THAT SHE SEEMS TO JUST LOSE HER BALANCE WITHOUT
WARNING. BG AND BP HAVE REMAINED STABLE. PATIENT AND CG STATE THEY
BELIEVE ALL FALLS ARE RELATED TO WEAKNESS. PATIENT ADMITS TO NOT
FOLLOWING THROUGH WITH THERAPY EXERCISES BETWEEN VISITS. FALL
PRECAUTIONS REVIEWED. PATIENT VERBALIZES AGREEMENT. THERE HAVE
BEEN NO OTHER MEDICATION CHANGES AND PATIENT STATES WITH THE
EXCEPTION OF THE INCREASED DIFFICULTY WITH BALANCE THAT SHE
REMAINS STABLE AND IN GOOD SHAPE.

NEEDS WITHOUT DIFFICULTIES. THE PATIENT HAD VASCULAR ACCESS
SURGERY ON HER RIGHT UPPER ARM YESTERDAY WITH DR. DWIVEDI.
DISCHARGE PAPERWORK SAYS LEAVE CURRENT DRESSING ON RT UPPER
ARMIN PLACE UNTIL TOMORROW 11-19-20 AND THEN LEAVE STERI-STRIPS IN
PLACE UNTIL THEY FALL OFF NATURALLY. SKILLED NURSING INTENDS TO
ASSESS FOR DRESSING NEEDS WANTS CURRENT DRESSING IS REMOVED
ANDTHEAREA IS ASSESSED TOMORROW. ALSO PATIENT HAS NEW
PRESCRIPTION FOR PAIN MEDICATION. NEW ORDERS RECEIVED WITH
BRITNEY WITH DR MADERIC. PATIENTS SCHEDULED FOR SKILLED NURSING
RECERTIFICATION SECONDARY TO NEW SURGICAL WOUND AT RIGHT UPPER
ARM. PATIENT REMAINS STABLE AT THIS ASSESSMENT. THE DRESSING THAT
IS IN PLACE DOES SHOW SOME DRAINAGE THAT IS BLOODY AND NATURE. THE
DRESSING IS INTACT AND THE EXTREMITY IS NOT SWOLLEN AT THIS
ASSESSMENT. PATIENT STATES PAIN IS CONTROLLED AT THIS ASSESSMENT
BUT THE AREA REMAINS TENDER. PATIENT ALSO COMPLAINS OF A SLIGHT
SORE THROAT. THERE ARE NO SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF ACUTE DISTRESS
OBSERVED STATED.

SUPPLY PACKAGE: GAUZE SPONGE,4"X4"12PLY,STERILE,LF,2'S

DETAILS:
DAILY X 2 WEEKS
2/2/2021 9:44:42 PM MARLENA WELTY, RN SKILLED NURSING ADD-ON ASSESSMENT PERFORMED AND PATIENT HAS Active
BILATERAL BUTTOCKS STAGE 2 PRESSURE ULCERS THAT WILL REQUIRE
TEACHING AND INTERVENTION AND MANAGEMENT. PATIENT HAS ALSO BEEN
HAVING FLUCTUATIONS IN HER BLOOD SUGAR AND COULD BENEFIT FROM
DIABETIC TEACHING AND EDUCATION. PATIENT STARTED ON KIDNEY DIALYSIS
TODAY SECONDARY TO END STAGE RENAL DISEASE AND IS ALSO HAVING
PROBLEMS WITH ANEMIA AT THIS TIME IN WHICH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER
COULD BOTH BENEFIT FROM EDUCATION REGARDING AS WELL AS BASIC
SYSTEMS ASSESSMENT. PER CAREGIVER PATIENT HAS A HARD TIME WHEN
SHE HAS BOWEL MOVEMENTS BECAUSE THEY ARE THICKAND HARD TO
CLEAN OFF HER PERI AREA WHICH OFTEN CAUSES HER SKIN BREAKDOWN ON
HER BOTTOM TO WORSEN. UPON ASSESSMENT PATIENT IS TAKING PO
FERROUS SULFATE FOR ANEMIA IN WHICH | EDUCATED THAT COULD CAUSE
THICKTARRY STOOLS THAT ARE HARD TO CLEAN. | ENCOURAGED PATIENT
ANDCAREGIVER TO USE MOIST WIPES WITH PERI WASH CLEANSER TO
CLEAN THE AREA AND TO MAKE SURE SHE IS HYDRATING WITH WATER AS
WELL AS TAKING AN OVER THE COUNTER STOOL SOFTENER AS SUGGESTED
BY MD TO PREVENT INCREASED STOOL THICKNESS. | ALSO EDUCATED ON
THE IMPORTANCE OF PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES AND PATIENT IS USING
HER PRESSURE CUSHION WHILE SITTING IN CHAIR BUT PATIENT ALSO
SLEEPS IN THE CHAIR BECAUSE OF HER NEED TO HAVE HER HEAD ELEVATED
WHEN SLEEPING. | EDUCATED ON SKIN BREAKDOWN PRECAUTIONS AND
PRESSURE RELIEF TECHNIQUES INCLUDING NEED TO LIFT HER BUTT OFF AND
NOT SCOOT WHEN CHANGING POSITIONS TO PREVENT SHEARING AND
FRICTION AS WELL AS TRYING TO STAND EVERY HOUR WHILE AWAKE TO
RELIEVE SOME OF THE PRESSURE FROM HER BUTTOCKS. SN EDUCATED ON
CAUSE OF PRESSURE SORES AND TREATMENT TO HEAL THEM WHICH
INCLUDES DECREASING THE PRESSURE AND KEEPING THE SKIN AS CLEAN
AND DRY AS POSSIBLE. |SHOWED CAREGIVER HOW TO APPLY OINTMENT TO
OPEN WOUNDS AND COVER WITH FOAM BORDER GAUZE. CAREGIVER
AGREES HE WILL PICK UP SOME CALMOSEPTINE OINTMENT FROM THE
PHARMACY AND BEGAN USING THIS AT THIS TIME. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND
CAREGIVER ON NEED TO PHONE NURSE IF WOUNDS BEGAN TO DETERIORATE
OR THERE WERE ANY NEW OPEN AREAS NOTED. PATIENT DOES REQUIRE
FREQUENT REPOSITIONING OF THE BODY IN WAYS NOT FEASIBLE BY AN
ORDINARY BED INCLUDING ELEVATION OF HER HEAD AND FEET SECONDARY
TO ORTHOPNEA CAUSED BY CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE
REQUIRINGHOSPITAL BED. PATIENT HAS NEW ONSET SKIN BREAKDOWN TO
HER BUTTOCKS IN WHICH WILL REQUIRE NEED OF GEL OVERLAY OR DRY
FOAM MATTRESS AS WELL TO PREVENT DETERIORATION OF CURRENT
WOUNDS. PATIENT HAS DECREASED ENDURANCE, UNSTEADY GAIT, AND
WEAKNESS ALL REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE AND WALKER TO
SAFELY LEAVE HOME WITH FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER MAKING
HER HOMEBOUND.

TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

SECONDARY TO NEW ONSET OPEN WOUND TO LEFT LOWER LEG WITH
EDEMA. PATIENT WAS SEEN BY PCP IN HER OFFICE YESTERDAY WITH

WOUND PICTURES AND MEASUREMENTS WERE TAKEN ANDWOUND CARE
PERFORMED PER MD ORDER AND PATIENT TOLERATED WELL. ORDER WAS
OBTAINED TO APPLY XERORFORM OR PETROLEUM GAUZE OVER WOUND
BEDS PRIOR TO APPLYING UNNABOOT ANDTOES WERE WRAPPED AS WELL
DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE WOUNDS ON THE TOES. PATIENT WAS
EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF CIRCULATORY COMPROMISE TO REPORT AND HAS
GOOD PEDAL PULSES NOTED AT THIS TIME. PATIENT DID HAVE A DOPPLER
OBTAINED YESTERDAY PRIOR TO RETURNING HOME IN WHICH THEY SAID
WAS NEGATIVE. PATIENTS BLOOD PRESSURE WAS CHECK THE FIRST TIME
AND READ 88/31 AND THEN WAS RECHECKED AND WAS STILL CONSIDERABLY
LOW SO DR. MADERIC OFFICE WAS CALLED AND PATIENT WAS ADVISED TO
HOLD HER EVENING DOSE OF PO FUROSEMIDE AND TO TAKE A MIDODRINE
TABLET TO INCREASE HER BLOOD PRESSURE READING. PATIENT HADA
REPORTED FALL YESTERDAY IN WHICH | MADE AN INCIDENT REPORT AND
AFTER TALKING WITH BOTH PATIENT AND CAREGIVER THEY WOULD PREFER
PHYSICAL THERAPY TO COME FOR PATIENTS LEG STRENGTHENING INSTEAD
OF OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY AT THIS TIME SO NEW ORDER OBTAINED FOR
NEXT WEEK PT EVALUATION WHICH IS IN THE 5-DAY RECERTIFICATION
WINDOW AND | WILL NOTIFY OCCUPATIONAL THERAPY OF PATIENTS
REQUEST FOR PT ONLY. PATIENT AND CAREGIVER WERE BOTH EDUCATED
ON SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION TO IMMEDIATELY REPORT AND NEED TO
MONITOR HER SKIN THOROUGHLY AS SHE HAS BRUISES COVERING BOTH
ARMS BUT LARGE HEMATOMAS WITH DRIED SKIN TEARS TO HER RIGHT ARM
AS WELL. PATIENT IS ASHEN IN APPEARANCE AND HAS HAD A STEADY
DECLINE SINCE STARTING DIALYSIS TREATMENTS IN WHICH | DISCUSSED
WITH BOTH PATIENT, CAREGIVER, AND MISSY AT DR. MADERICS OFFICE.
PATIENT HAS CONSIDERED NOT DOING DIALYSIS ANYMORE BUT
UNDERSTANDS THE CONSEQUENCES OF THIS. | DID ENCOURAGE HER TO
DISCUSS HER FEELINGS WITH NEPHROLOGIST WHEN SHE IS SEEN BY HIM

REQUIRE CONTINUED SKILLED NURSING SERVICES FOR NEW ONSET

A FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT FOR THE PAST SEVERAL VISITS SECONDARY TO
HER HYPOTENSION AND HAS HAD MULTIPLE FALLS IN A SHORT PERIOD OF
TIME FROM HER LEGS BEING SO WEAK. SHE IS HOLDING FLUID IN BOTH HER
LOWER EXTREMITIES AND SOME IN HER ABDOMEN AND IS NO LONGER
URINATING INDEPENDENTLY AT ALL. PATIENT HAS ECCHYMOSIS TO HER
ENTIRE RIGHT ARM, SOME LEFT ARM, AND MINIMALLY TO HER LEGS. AN

WITH CG WHOASSISTS HER WITH THEM AND HE HAD GOTTEN CONFUSED
ANDWAS ONLY GIVING RANEXA ONCE INSTEAD OF TWICE DAILY. PATIENT
BLOOD PRESSURE WAS AGAIN VERY LOW TODAY SO | CALLED DR MADERIC
OFFICE ANDSPOKE WITH MISSY AND LEFT A MESSAGE AT AKC IN MADISON
FOR THE KIDNEY DR.AS WELL. PATIENT DENIED SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION IN
WHICH | EDUCATED ON IMMEDIATE NEED TO CALL 911 WITH. CG AGREES TO
WATCH HER CLOSELY. WOUND CARE WAS PERFORMED AND PATIENT
ALREADY HAS AN ORDER IN FOR PT EVALUATION. PATIENT AND CG BOTH
AGAIN HAVE QUESTIONS ABOUT WHAT WOULD HAPPEN IF SHE WERETO
STOP DIALYSIS AND | EDUCATED ON THIS AND NEED TO DISCUSS THEIR
CONCERNS WITH NEPHROLOGIST AS WELL. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED FOR
ECHO ON WEDNESDAY. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED AND GETS SHORT
OF AIR WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION AND HAS DECREASED
ENDURANCE WITH UNSTEADY GAIT AND FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING
FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE WITH
WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

SN VISIT FOR CP AND GENERAL ASSESS. PT LYING IN BEDTIRES FROM GOING Active
TO DIALYSIS FOR 5 HRS THIS MORNING AND JUST GOT BACK HOME. PT AND
CG REPORTS DIALYSIS PULLED OFF 7 POUNDS FLUID TODAY. PT REPORTS
DIALYSIS HAD HARD TIME WITH PT BLOOD PRESSURE TODAY AS IT WAS VERY
LOW. BP CONTINUE LOW AT SN VISIT AND PT DENIES ANY OTHER
HYPOTENSION SX. WOUND CARE PROVIDED TO ALL AREAS AND UNNA BOOTS
REAPPLIED TO LLE. EDEMA TO LLE IS MUCH IMPROVED TO LOWER LEG BUT
REMAINS ABOVE THE KNEE. PT TEACHING TO KEEP LEGS ELEATED ABOVE
HEART AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE WHILE LYNG IN BED RESTING. PT CONTINUES
ON ABX. DENIES ANY FURTHER FALLS.
MEDICATION PROFILE MAILED TO PATIENT. Active
SN VISIT ODAY FOR CP ANDWOUND ASSESS. PT HAD DIALYSIS TODAY AND Active
TOL3 HRS. DIALYSIS NOTED PT LLE TOE WERE PURPLISH IN COLOR AND HAD
CG CALL PCP TO MAKE APPNT FOR TODAY TO ASSESS POOR CIRCULATION
ISSUES. UPON ASSESS TODAY BU SN LLE TOES PEURPLISH IN COLOR, COOL
TO TOUCH AND NON BLANCHABLE. NABLE TO PALPATE PEDAL PULSE IN LLE
PT DOES REPORT FEELING WHEN SN TOUCHED FEET AND REPORTS NO
TINGLING OR NUMBNESS. EDEMA TO BLE REMAINS BUT IMPROVED TO LEFT
LOWER LEG BUT KNEE ANDABOVE WITH EDEMA NOTED. RLE WITH EDEMA
NOTED AS WELL. WOUND CARE PROVIDED- WOUNDS CLEANSED AND
COVERED WITH BORDERED DRRESSING FOR ROTECTION AND UNNA BOOTS
LEFT OFF SO PCP CAN ASSESS BLE CIRCULATION. VISIT SHORT TODAY R/T PT
WAS HEADING TO PCP APPNT..

3/25/2021 10:38:54 PM MARLENA WELTY, RN UPON ARRIVAL PATIENT LYING IN BED AND HAD SCOOTED DOWN SO HER Active
HEAD WAS KINKED. | SHOWED HER HOW TO SIT UP IN THE BED TO PREVENT
SLIDING AND HOW HIGH SHE NEEDED TO ELEVATE HER LEGS WHILE IN BED.
CG THEY COULD ONLY REMOVE 3 POUNDS AT DIALYSIS ANDTHAT SHE SAW
DR MADERIC REGARDING HER LEG AND TOE DISCOLORATION AND WAS
PRESCRIBED SILVADENE OINTMENT ONLY. PATIENT HAS MULTIPLE
SCATTERED NEW OPEN AREAS TO HER RLE IN WHICH | PERFORMEDA
GENERALIZED MEASUREMENT WITH SEVERE BRUISING AND DISCOLORATION
WITH SHINY FRAGILE SKIN TO BOTH LOWER LEGS THAT IS VERY CONCERNING
FOR ARTERIAL PROBLEMS. PATIENT ALSO HAS A NEW LEFT GREAT TOE
WOUND THAT APPEARS TO BE ARTERIAL IN ORIGIN. PATIENT HAS PEDAL
PULSES BUT THEY ARE FAINT. DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE LEGS
UNNABOOTS WERE NOT APPLIED AND MD ON CALL WAS NOT ABLE TO BE
REACHED AS IT RINGS STRAIGHT TO KDH ER. SN WILL CONTACT DR MADERIC
IN THE MORNING TO CLARIFY IF THERE IS A DIAGNOSIS OF PAD. PATIENT DID
HAVE RECENT TESTING PERFORMED. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED TO SEE
CARDIOLOGIST DR STAVENS TOMORROW AS WELL.
PATIENT HAS DIMINISHED LONG SOUNDS TO HER LOWER LUNGS IN WHICH |
EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF PNEUMONIA OR FLUID IN LUNGS TO REPORT.
PATIENT EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF DEEP BREATHING AND MOVING
AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE TO PROMOTE HEART AND LUNG HEALTH. PATIENT
EDUCATED ON CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE DIAGNOSIS ANDDISEASE
PROCESS. PATIENT EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF CHF TO REPORT INCLUDING
SWELLING TO LOWER EXTREMITIES AND INCREASED SHORTNESS OF AIR. |
EDUCATED PATIENT ON THE IMPORTANCE OF WATCHING HIS SODIUM INTAKE
ANDTRYING TO STAY UNDER 2000 MG OF SODIUM DAILY. PATIENT STATUS
CONTINUES TO DETERIORATE DESPITE MULTIPLE SN AND MD VISITS AND SHE
REFUSES TO GOTO HOSPITAL AFTER PROMPTED MULTIPLE TIMES DURING
MY VISIT. THEY CONTINUE TO BE UNABLE TO PERFORM DIALYSIS ROUTINELY
BECAUSE OF HER HYPOTENSION SO SHE KEEPS BUILDING UP MORE FLUID IN
HER LOWER LEGS WHICH ARE NOW CONTINUOUSLY WEEPING CLEAR TO
YELLOW TINGE FLUID IN WHICH MD IS AWARE. PATIENT LEGS CLEANSED AND
SILVADENE OINTMENT APPLIED THEN COVERED WITH XEROFORM GAUZE AND
WRAPPED LOOSELY WITH KERLIXANDSECURED EUTHANASIA TAPE AND
STRETCH NET. CG ADVISED TO DO THIS DAILY. WOUND CARE PERFORMED TO
TOES IN WHICH | EDUCATED CG ON AS WELL. THEY BOTH UNDERSTAND TO
CALL 911 WITH ANY INCREASED PROBLEMS. PATIENT IS
IMMUNOCOMPROMISED, GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH MINIMAL EXERTION,
FATIGUES VERY EASILY AND HAS UNSTEADY GAIT WITH FREQUENT FALLS
REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE AND WHEELCHAIR TO SAFELY LEAVE
HOME MAKING HER HOME BOUND.
3/26/2021 4:52:03 PM MARLENA WELTY, RN | SPOKE WITH AMANDA AT DR MADERICS OFFICE AND WAS ABLE TO CONFIRM Active
A NEW DIAGNOSIS OF PERIPHERAL ARTERIAL DISEASE. | EXPLAINED TO
AMANDA THAT DUE TO PAD DIAGNOSIS PATIENT IS NOT ABLE TO RECEIVE

ORDERS WERE CHANGED AS PREVIOUSLY NOTED.

SKILLED NURSE TO OBSERVE FOR FALL RISK, IDENTIFY CHANGES, AND INTERVENE TO MINIMIZE COMPLICATIONS. SKILLED NURSE TO TEACH
RELATED TO FALL RISK REDUCTION INCLUDING HOME/ENVIRONMENTAL SAFETY, MEDICATION MANAGEMENT, AND FALL PREVENTION.

SN VISIT ODAY FOR CP ANDWOUND ASSESS. PT HAD DIALYSIS TODAY AND TOL 3 HRS. DIALYSIS
NOTED PT LLE TOE WERE PURPLISH IN COLOR AND HAD CG CALL PCP TO MAKE APPNT FOR
TODAY TO ASSESS POOR CIRCULATION ISSUES. UPON ASSESS TODAY BU SN LLE TOES
PEURPLISH IN COLOR, COOL TO TOUCH AND NON BLANCHABLE. NABLE TO PALPATE PEDAL
PULSE IN LLE PT DOES REPORT FEELING WHEN SN TOUCHED FEET AND REPORTS NO TINGLING
OR NUMBNESS. EDEMA TO BLE REMAINS BUT IMPROVED TO LEFT LOWER LEG BUT KNEE AND
ABOVE WITH EDEMA NOTED. RLE WITH EDEMA NOTED AS WELL. WOUND CARE PROVIDEDWOUNDS CLEANSED AND COVERED WITH BORDERED DRRESSING FOR ROTECTION AND UNNA
BOOTS LEFT OFF SO PCP CAN ASSESS BLE CIRCULATION. VISIT SHORT TODAY R/T PT WAS
HEADING TO PCP APPNT..

Note Date Note Type Entered By Note Status
[DOB-5507] NARRATIVE MARLENA WELTY, RN Active
Last Update Last Updated By
Note
UPON ARRIVAL PATIENT LYING IN BED AND HAD SCOOTED DOWN SO HER HEAD WAS KINKED. |
SHOWED HER HOW TO SIT UP IN THE BED TO PREVENT SLIDING AND HOW HIGH SHE NEEDED TO
ELEVATE HER LEGS WHILE IN BED. CG THEY COULD ONLY REMOVE 3POUNDS AT DIALYSIS AND
THAT SHE SAW DR MADERIC REGARDING HER LEG AND TOE DISCOLORATION AND WAS
PRESCRIBED SILVADENE OINTMENT ONLY. PATIENT HAS MULTIPLE SCATTERED NEW OPEN
AREAS TO HER RLE IN WHICH | PERFORMED A GENERALIZED MEASUREMENT WITH SEVERE
BRUISING AND DISCOLORATION WITH SHINY FRAGILE SKIN TO BOTH LOWER LEGS THAT |S VERY
CONCERNING FOR ARTERIAL PROBLEMS. PATIENT ALSO HAS A NEW LEFT GREAT TOE WOUND
THAT APPEARS TO BE ARTERIAL IN ORIGIN. PATIENT HAS PEDAL PULSES BUT THEY ARE FAINT.
DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE LEGS UNNABOOTS WERE NOT APPLIED AND MD ON CALL WAS NOT
ABLE TO BE REACHED AS IT RINGS STRAIGHT TO KDH ER. SN WILL CONTACT DR MADERIC IN THE
MORNING TO CLARIFY IF THERE IS A DIAGNOSIS OF PAD. PATIENT DID HAVE RECENT TESTING
PERFORMED. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED TO SEE CARDIOLOGIST DR STAVENS TOMORROW AS
WELL.
PATIENT HAS DIMINISHED LONG SOUNDS TO HER LOWER LUNGS IN WHICH | EDUCATED ON
SIGNS OF PNEUMONIA OR FLUID IN LUNGS TO REPORT. PATIENT EDUCATED ON THE
IMPORTANCE OF DEEP BREATHING AND MOVING AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE TO PROMOTE HEART
AND LUNG HEALTH. PATIENT EDUCATED ON CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE DIAGNOSIS AND
DISEASE PROCESS. PATIENT EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF CHF TO REPORT INCLUDING SWELLING TO
LOWER EXTREMITIES AND INCREASED SHORTNESS OF AIR. | EDUCATED PATIENT ON THE
IMPORTANCE OF WATCHING HIS SODIUM INTAKE AND TRYING TO STAY UNDER 2000 MG OF
SODIUM DAILY. PATIENT STATUS CONTINUES TO DETERIORATE DESPITE MULTIPLE SN AND MD
VISITS AND SHE REFUSES TO GO TO HOSPITAL AFTER PROMPTED MULTIPLE TIMES DURING MY
VISIT. THEY CONTINUE TO BE UNABLE TO PERFORM DIALYSIS ROUTINELY BECAUSE OF HER
HYPOTENSION SO SHE KEEPS BUILDING UP MORE FLUID IN HER LOWER LEGS WHICH ARE NOW
CONTINUOUSLY WEEPING CLEAR TO YELLOW TINGE FLUID IN WHICH MD IS AWARE. PATIENT
LEGS CLEANSED AND SILVADENE OINTMENT APPLIED THEN COVERED WITH XEROFORM GAUZE
ANDWRAPPED LOOSELY WITH KERLIX AND SECURED EUTHANASIA TAPE AND STRETCH NET. CG
ADVISED TO DO THIS DAILY. WOUND CARE PERFORMED TO TOES IN WHICH | EDUCATED CG ON
AS WELL. THEY BOTH UNDERSTAND TO CALL 911 WITH ANY INCREASED PROBLEMS. PATIENT IS
IMMUNOCOMPROMISED, GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH MINIMAL EXERTION, FATIGUES VERY EASILY
AND HAS UNSTEADY GAIT WITH FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE AND
WHEELCHAIR TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOME BOUND.

WELL. THEY BOTH UNDERSTAND TO CALL 911 WITH ANY INCREASED PROBLEMS. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED, GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH
MINIMAL EXERTION, FATIGUES VERY EASILY ANDHAS UNSTEADY GAIT WITH FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE AND
WHEELCHAIR TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOME BOUND.

ON AT NB DS WW Al D. HE TT ED
PREVENT SLIDING AND HOW HIGH SHE NEEDED TO ELEVATE HER LEGS WHILE IN BED. CG THEY COULD ONLY REMOVE 3 POUNDS AT DIALYSIS
AND THAT SHE SAW DR MADERIC REGARDING HER LEG AND TOE DISCOLORATION AND WAS PRESCRIBED SILVADENE OINTMENT ONLY.
PATIENT HAS MULTIPLE SCATTERED NEW OPEN AREAS TO HER RLE IN WHICH | PERFORMED A GENERALIZED MEASUREMENT WITH SEVERE
BRUISING AND DISCOLORATION WITH SHINY FRAGILE SKIN TO BOTH LOWER LEGS THAT IS VERY CONCERNING FOR ARTERIAL PROBLEMS.
PATIENT ALSO HAS A NEW LEFT GREAT TOE WOUND THAT APPEARS TO BE ARTERIAL IN ORIGIN. PATIENT HAS PEDAL PULSES BUT THEY ARE
FAINT. DUE TO THE NATURE OF THE LEGS UNNABOOTS WERE NOT APPLIED AND MD ON CALL WAS NOT ABLE TO BE REACHED AS IT RINGS
STRAIGHT TO KDH ER. SN WILL CONTACT DR MADERIC IN THE MORNING TO CLARIFY IF THERE IS A DIAGNOSIS OF PAD. PATIENT DID HAVE
RECENT TESTING PERFORMED. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED TO SEE CARDIOLOGIST DR STAVENS TOMORROW AS WELL.
PATIENT HAS DIMINISHED LONG SOUNDS TO HER LOWER LUNGS IN WHICH | EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF PNEUMONIA OR FLUID IN LUNGS TO
REPORT. PATIENT EDUCATED ON THE IMPORTANCE OF DEEP BREATHING AND MOVING AS MUCH AS POSSIBLE TO PROMOTE HEART AND
LUNG HEALTH. PATIENT EDUCATED ON CONGESTIVE HEART FAILURE DIAGNOSIS AND DISEASE PROCESS. PATIENT EDUCATED ON SIGNS OF
CHF TO REPORT INCLUDING SWELLING TO LOWER EXTREMITIES AND INCREASED SHORTNESS OF AIR. | EDUCATED PATIENT ON THE
IMPORTANCE OF WATCHING HIS SODIUM INTAKE AND TRYING TO STAY UNDER 2000 MG OF SODIUM DAILY. PATIENT STATUS CONTINUES TO
DETERIORATE DESPITE MULTIPLE SN AND MD VISITS AND SHE REFUSES TO GO TO HOSPITAL AFTER PROMPTED MULTIPLE TIMES DURING MY
VISIT. THEY CONTINUE TO BE UNABLE TO PERFORM DIALYSIS ROUTINELY BECAUSE OF HER HYPOTENSION SO SHE KEEPS BUILDING UP MORE
FLUID IN HER LOWER LEGS WHICH ARE NOW CONTINUOUSLY WEEPING CLEAR TO YELLOW TINGE FLUID IN WHICH MD IS AWARE. PATIENT
LEGS CLEANSED AND SILVADENE OINTMENT APPLIED THEN COVERED WITH XEROFORM GAUZE AND WRAPPED LOOSELY WITH KERLIX AND
SECURED EUTHANASIA TAPE AND STRETCH NET. CG ADVISED TO DO THIS DAILY. WOUND CARE PERFORMED TO TOES IN WHICH | EDUCATED
CG ON AS WELL. THEY BOTH UNDERSTAND TO CALL 911 WITH ANY INCREASED PROBLEMS. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED, GETS SHORT
OF AIR WITH MINIMAL EXERTION, FATIGUES VERY EASILY AND HAS UNSTEADY GAIT WITH FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING CAREGIVER
ASSISTANCE AND WHEELCHAIR TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOME BOUND.
Patient Goal
I WANT TO GET THESE THINGS OFF MY LEGS

PROPER BODY AND UPPER EXTREMITY POSITIONING FOR SEATED UPRIGHT EOB - EDUCATION PROVIDED TO USE THE BEDRAIL ON BED NOT
TO LEAVE IT DOWN SO THE PT CAN HOLD TO TRANSFER SUPINE <> SIT , TO USE FOR ROLLING SIDE TO SIDE/SCOOTING ,AND TO HOLD FOR
UE SUPPORT WHILE SEATED EOB - CG SAID THE PT MAKES HIM LEAVE IT DOWN SO SHE CAN REACH HER STUFF ON TABLE BESIDE THE BED -
CG EDUCATION CAN LOWER IT ONCE THE PT IS IN THE BED -OTHERWISE RAISE IT AND HAVE THE PT USE TO MAKE HER MORE INDEPENDENT
WITH MOBILITY/ TRANSFERS - OTHERWISE SHE IS RELYING ON THE CG TO DO ALL THE WORK TO TRANSFER HER
SAFE SIT TO STAND TECHNIQUE- FROM SEATED EOB HOLDING THE BEDRAIL WITH R UE FOR SUPPORT
PATIENT REQUIRES FREQUENT VERBAL/TACTILE CUES FOR HAND PLACEMENT
PATIENT REQUIRES CGA TO SAFELY PERFORM SIT <> STAND TRANSFERS FROM EOB USING THE BEDRAIL
2. INSTRUCT IN THERAPEUTIC EXERCISES AND HOME EXERCISE PROGRAM.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: INSTRUCTED WITH 2 TO 3 VERAL / VISUAL/TACTILE CUES FOR LE THER EX TO IMPROVE STRENGTH WITH PT IN SUPINEHEELSLIDES, SLR,AP, BRIDGES X 10 REPS EACH . PT AND CG EDUCATION TO DO THE PREVIOUS LISTED EXERCISES DAILY FOR HEP IN ORDER
TO IMPROVE STRENGTH
3. INSTRUCT PATIENT/ CAREGIVER IN PROPER TECHNIQUES TO FACILITATE SAFE BED MOBILITY.
DETAILS/COMMENTS: TASK DESIGN WITH 3 TO 4 VERBAL/VISUAL CUES TO IMPROVE BED MOBILITY -INSTRUCTED ON SCOOTING WITH PT
BENDING KNEES AND LIFTING BOTTOM USING THE BEDRAILS SCOOT UP IN BED AND INSTRUCTED ON ROLLING SIDE TO SIDE USING THE
BEDRAILS
MIN A FOR SUPINE <> SIT WITH BEDRAIL - PT REQUIRED MIN A TO GET LEGS IN THE BED
4. INSTRUCT PATIENT /CAREGIVER IN THERAPEUTIC ACTIVITIES TO FACILITATE INCEASED FUNCTIONALITY.

PUT TABLE IN FRONT OF HER SO SHE CAN WORK ON SITTING BALANCE WITH BACK UNSUPPORTED / TO PROMOTE FUNCTIONAL ACTIVITY
TOLERANCE
5. EDUCATE PATIENT / CAREGIVER IN FALL PREVENTION STRATEGIES.

ALWAYS USE ASSISTIVE DEVICE; WC / WALKER

: 1. ASSESS AND EVALUATE CO-MORBID CONDITIONS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PATIENT'S SYMPTOMS RELATED TO CO-MORBID CONDITIONS ARE CURRENTLY CONTROLLED.
REVIEWED AND INSTRUCTED ON RECENT EXACERBATION OF CO-MORBID CONDITIONS
WHEN TO REPORT TO NURSE/PHYSICIAN
DIETARY RESTRICTIONS THAT PROMOTE CONTROL OF CARDIAC DISEASE
MEDICATION REGIMEN, SIDE EFFECTS/DESIRED EFFECTS, POTENTIAL INTERACTIONS AND REFILL PROCESS
BALANCING ACTIVITIES AND NEED FOR FREQUENT REST PERIODS
ENVIRONMENTAL FACTORS
DISEASE PROCESS
2. PROVIDE WOUND CARE
DETAILS/COMMENTS: PERFORMED WOUND CARE TO PROMOTE CLINICAL STABILITY.
3. INSTRUCT PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON WOUND CARE, SUCH AS DEFINITION, SYMPTOM MONITORING, DRESSING CHANGES, AND INTENDED
EFFECTS
DETAILS/COMMENTS: EDUCATED PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON TREATMENT APPLICATION TECHNIQUE, FREQUENCY, ANDINTENDED EFFECTS TO
PROMOTE WOUND TREATMENT/MONITORING ADHERENCE USING TASK-SPECIFIC RETURN DEMONSTRATION.
GUIDED PATIENT/CAREGIVER ON OVERCOMING CHALLENGES TO PROMOTE WOUND MANAGEMENT USING SITUATION-SPECIFIC PROBLEM
SOLVING.
VALIDATED CORRECT TREATMENT APPLICATION TECHNIQUE TO SUSTAIN WOUND MANAGEMENT USING REPETITIVE SITUATIONAL SOLUTION
FINDING.
"1. CHANGES TO CO-MORBID CONDITIONS WILL BE IDENTIFIED AND REPORTED TO THE PHYSICIAN.
2. PATIENT TOLERATES WOUND CARE WITHOUT COMPLICATIONS.
3. PATIENT TOLERATES INCISION/SUTURE SITE CARE WITHOUT COMPLICATIONS.
4. PATIENT TOLERATES PRESSURE ULCER CARE WITHOUT COMPLICATIONS.
5. PATIENT/CAREGIVER VERBALIZES/DEMONSTRATES WOUND MANAGEMENT, TREATMENT APPLICATION TECHNIQUE, AND INTENDED
EFFECTS.

Supporting Documentation for Home Health Eligibility:
FOCUS OF CARE (SUPPORTING CLINICAL INFORMATION, PERTINENT MEDICAL HISTORY, ADDITIONAL DIAGNOSIS, LAST REHOSPITALIZATION,
ER, OR URGENCY CARE VISIT):
PATIENT RECERTIFICATION ASSESSMENT COMPLETED AND PATIENT WILL REQUIRE CONTINUED SKILLED NURSING SERVICES FOR NEW
ONSET WOUNDS TO RIGHT LEG AND PREVIOUSLY NOTED LEFT LEG AND TOE WOUNDS ALL OF WHICH REQUIRE WOUND CARE TEACHING
AND ASSESSMENT. PATIENT HAS BEEN DECLINING RAPIDLY OVER THE PAST FEW WEEKS IN WHICH SN MADE MD AWARE. SHE HAS BEEN
UNABLE TO COMPLETE A FULL DIALYSIS TREATMENT FOR THE PAST SEVERAL VISITS SECONDARY TO HER HYPOTENSION AND HAS HAD
MULTIPLE FALLS IN A SHORT PERIOD OF TIME FROM HER LEGS BEING SO WEAK. SHE IS HOLDING FLUID IN BOTH HER LOWER
EXTREMITIES AND SOME IN HER ABDOMEN AND IS NO LONGER URINATING INDEPENDENTLY AT ALL. PATIENT HAS ECCHYMOSIS TO HER
ENTIRE RIGHT ARM, SOME LEFT ARM, AND MINIMALLY TO HER LEGS. AN UNNABOOT WOULD BE APPROPRIATE FOR HER RIGHT LEG AS
WELL BUT THIS LEG IS THE ONE USED TO OBTAIN HER BP READINGS SO DRY DRESSINGS WERE APPLIED PER MD ORDERS. PATIENT DID
WELL WITH THE LLE UNNABOOT THIS LAST TIME BUT DOESN'T LIKE IT AND REQUESTED HER TOES LEFT OUT. | EDUCATED PATIENT AND
CAREGIVER ON NEED TO MONITOR THE SKIN ON HER TOES CLOSELY TO PREVENT TRAUMA. MEDS WERE REVIEWED WITH CG WHO
ASSISTS HER WITH THEM AND HE HAD GOTTEN CONFUSED AND WAS ONLY GIVING RANEXA ONCE INSTEAD OF TWICE DAILY. PATIENT
BLOOD PRESSURE WAS AGAIN VERY LOW TODAY SO |CALLED DR MADERIC OFFICE AND SPOKE WITH MISSY AND LEFT A MESSAGE AT
AKC IN MADISON FOR THE KIDNEY DR.AS WELL. PATIENT DENIED SIGNS OF HYPOTENSION IN WHICH | EDUCATED ON IMMEDIATE NEED TO
CALL 911 WITH. CG AGREES TO WATCH HER CLOSELY. WOUND CARE WAS PERFORMED AND PATIENT ALREADY HAS AN ORDER IN FOR PT
EVALUATION. PATIENT AND CG BOTH AGAIN HAVE QUESTIONS ABOUT WHAT WOULD HAPPEN IF SHE WERE TO STOP DIALYSIS AND |
EDUCATED ON THIS AND NEED TO DISCUSS THEIR CONCERNS WITH NEPHROLOGIST AS WELL. PATIENT IS SCHEDULED FOR ECHO ON
WEDNESDAY. PATIENT IS IMMUNOCOMPROMISED AND GETS SHORT OF AIR WITH MINIMAL TO MODERATE EXERTION AND HAS
DECREASED ENDURANCE WITH UNSTEADY GAIT AND FREQUENT FALLS REQUIRING FREQUENT REST PERIODS TO RECOVER AND
CAREGIVER ASSISTANCE WITH WALKER TO SAFELY LEAVE HOME MAKING HER HOMEBOUND.

SN TO TEACH/INSTRUCT PATIENT/CG TO CLEANSE BILATERAL LEGS AND TOES WITH WOUND CLEANSER OR SOAP
